227.title: COMPRESSION PRESSURE
227.body: COMPRESSION PRESSURE EM-13 Measurement of Compression Pressure Warm up engine. Turn ignition switch off. Release fuel pressure. Refer to section EF & EC. Remove all spark plugs. Disconnect distributor center cable. Attach a compression tester to No. 1 cylinder. Depress accelerator pedal fully to keep throttle valve wide open. Crank engine and record highest gauge indication. Repeat the measurement on each cylinder as shown above. Always use a fully-charged battery to obtain specified engine revolution. If cylinder compression in one or more cylinders is low, pour a small amount of engine oil into cylinders through spark plug holes and retest compression. If adding oil helps compression, piston rings may be worn or damaged. If so, replace piston rings after checking piston. If pressure stays low, a valve may be sticking or seating improperly. Inspect and repair valve and valve seat. (Refer to S.D.S.) If valve or valve seat is damaged excessively, replace them. If compression in any two adjacent cylinders is low and if adding oil does not help compression, there is leakage past the gasket surface. If so, replace cylinder head gasket. Compression pressure — Standard 1324 (13.2, 13.5, 192)/350 kPa (bar, kg/cm², psi)/rpm Standard Compression pressure — Minimum 1128 (11.3, 11.5, 164)/350 kPa (bar, kg/cm², psi)/rpm Minimum Difference limit between cylinders 98 (0.98, 1.0, 14)/350 kPa (bar, kg/cm², psi)/rpm Difference limit between cylinders Use compressor tester whose end (rubber portion) is less than 20 mm (0.79 in) dia. Otherwise, it may be caught by cylinder head during removal. Photo showing compression tester attached to engine cylinder head Illustration of compression tester probe showing 20 mm (0.79 in) dia. rubber end. Caption: Use compressor tester whose end (rubber portion) is less than 20 mm (0.79 in) dia. Otherwise, it may be caught by cylinder head during removal.

230.title: OIL PAN
230.body: OIL PAN EM-14 Removal Remove splashguards for access to oil pan area. Drain engine oil. Remove center member. Remover front exhaust tube. Remove oil pan bolts. (1) Insert Tool between cylinder block and oil pan. Be careful not to damage aluminum mating surface. Do not insert screwdriver, or oil pan flange will be deformed. (2) Slide Tool by tapping its side with a hammer, and remove oil pan. Installation Before installing oil pan, remove all traces of liquid gasket from mating surfaces using a scraper. Also remove traces of liquid gasket from mating surface of cylinder block. Fit new seals as shown. Ensure correct length, front & rear: Length "l": Front seal 134 mm (5.28 in); Rear seal 123 mm (4.84 in) Apply a continuous bead of liquid gasket to mating surface of oil pan as shown. Use Genuine Liquid Gasket or equivalent. Front seal length 134 mm (5.28 in) Installation Rear seal length 123 mm (4.84 in) Installation Liquid gasket bead width 4.0 - 5.0 mm (0.158 - 0.197 in) Applied to oil pan mating surface Liquid gasket bead offset from edge 20 mm (0.79 in) Both front and rear Underside view showing splashguard removal area and front tube location with arrows indicating bolt/fastener positions Tool KV10111100 inserted between cylinder block and oil pan to separate oil pan Tool KV10111100 being tapped with hammer to slide and remove oil pan Oil pan diagram showing front and rear seal positions with length indicators Oil pan top view showing liquid gasket bead application dimensions: 20mm (0.79 in) from edges and 4.0-5.0mm (0.158-0.197 in) bead width

239.title: TIMING CHAIN
239.body: TIMING CHAIN EM-17 After removing timing chain, do not turn crankshaft and camshaft separately, or valves will strike piston heads. Removal Drain engine coolant from radiator and cylinder block. Be careful not to spill coolant on drive belts. Remove the alternator and power steering drive belts. Remove air cleaner. Remove front right-side wheel. Remove front and right-side splashguards. Remove front exhaust tube. Remove rocker cover. Remove distributor cap. Remove all spark plugs. Set No. 1 piston at T.D.C. on compression stroke. Make sure No. 1 cylinder is at T.D.C. by looking at distributor rotor position. Remove distributor. Remove cam sprocket cover. 242.title: TIMING CHAIN - Removal (Cont'd)

Remove camshaft sprockets. Remove camshaft brackets and camshafts. These parts should be reassembled in their original positions. Bolts should be loosened in two or three steps. Remove idler sprocket bolt. Remove cylinder head with manifolds. Head warpage or cracking could result from removing bolts in incorrect order. Cylinder head bolts should be loosened in two or three steps. Remove idler sprocket and shaft. Remove upper timing chain. Remove oil pan. Refer to "Removal" in "OIL PAN". Remove oil strainer. Remove crankshaft pulley. Support engine with a suitable jack. Remove engine front mounting bracket. 248.title: TIMING CHAIN
248.body: TIMING CHAIN EM-20 Installation Confirm that No. 1 piston is set at T.D.C. on its compression stroke. Install crankshaft sprocket and lower timing chain. Set timing chain by aligning its mating mark with the one on crankshaft sprocket. Make sure sprocket's mating mark faces engine front. The number of links between the alignment marks (silver) are the same for the left and right sides, so either side can be used during alignment with the sprocket. Fit the lower chain tensioner. Apply liquid gasket to front cover. Install front cover. Check alignment of mating marks on chain and crankshaft sprocket. Align oil drive spacer with oil pump. Ensure O-rings are fitted correctly. Be careful not to damage oil seal when installing front cover. Install engine front mounting. Install oil strainer. Install oil pan. Refer to "Installation" in "OIL PAN". Install crankshaft pulley. Install center member. Liquid gasket bead width 2.0 - 3.0 mm Application to front cover Links between alignment marks (each side) 36 links Lower timing chain, left and right sides Crankshaft key position diagram showing crankshaft sprocket orientation Lower timing chain alignment diagram showing mating marks (silver), same number link, 36 links on each side, and mating marks on crankshaft sprocket Front cover with liquid gasket application, showing 2.0-3.0 mm bead width Front cover O-rings location diagram

251.title: TIMING CHAIN - Installation (Cont'd)
251.body: TIMING CHAIN - Installation (Cont'd) EM-21 Installation (Cont'd) Set idler sprocket by aligning the mating mark on the larger sprocket with the silver mating mark on the lower timing chain. Install upper timing chain and set it by aligning the mating mark on the smaller sprocket with the silver mating marks on the upper timing chain. Make sure sprocket's mating mark faces engine front. Install idler sprocket shaft from the rear side. Ensure that the shaft is located correctly, as shown. Install cylinder head with new gasket. Do not rotate crankshaft and camshaft separately, or valves will strike piston heads. Be sure to install washers between bolts and cylinder head. Tightening procedure (bolts 1-10) Tighten bolts (a) - bolts 1-10 39 N·m (4.0 kg-m, 29 ft-lb) Step a Tighten bolts (b) - bolts 1-10 78 N·m (8.0 kg-m, 58 ft-lb) Step b Loosen bolts (c) - bolts 1-10 completely Step c Tighten bolts (d) - bolts 1-10 3[?] N·m (3.6 kg-m, 26 ft-lb) Step d Turn bolts (e) - bolts 1-10 60 degrees clockwise Step e Tighten bolts (f) - bolts 11 to 15 6.3 to 8.3 N·m (0.64 to 0.85 kg-m, 4.6 to 6.1 ft-lb) Step f Timing chain sprocket alignment diagram showing upper timing chain, lower timing chain, mating marks (silver), and link counts (10 links, 14 links, 22 links) between sprockets Idler sprocket shaft installation diagram showing dowel and idler shaft positioning Cylinder head bolt washer installation diagram showing washer orientation with cylinder head side indicated Cylinder head bolt tightening sequence diagram showing numerical order for bolts 1-15

Install idler sprocket shaft from the rear side. Ensure that the shaft is located correctly, as shown. Install cylinder head with new gasket. Do not rotate crankshaft and camshaft separately, or valves will strike piston heads. Before installing cylinder head gasket, apply a continuous bead of liquid gasket as shown in the figure. 257.title: TIMING CHAIN - Installation (Cont'd)
257.body:
TIMING CHAIN - Installation (Cont'd) EM-23 Timing Chain Installation (Cont'd) Install camshaft sprocket bolts. Make sure camshafts are aligned as shown in figure. Assemble the tensioner by rotating and pushing the ratchet up the spiral until it stops. The tensioner is now pre-set. Chain lash will automatically set the tensioner when the engine is started. Install the tensioner. Check no problems occur when engine is rotated. Make sure that No. 1 piston is set at T.D.C. on its compression stroke. Apply liquid gasket to thermostat housing. Install thermostat housing. CAUTION:
• Check no problems occur when engine is rotated.
• Make sure that No. 1 piston is set at T.D.C. on its compression stroke. • Make sure that No. 1 piston is set at T.D.C. on its compression stroke. 260.title: TIMING CHAIN — Installation (Cont'd)
260.body: TIMING CHAIN — Installation (Cont'd) EM-24 Installation (Cont'd) Install distributor. Make sure that position of camshaft is as shown in figure. Install cam sprocket cover. Liquid gasket bead width: 2.0 - 3.0 mm (0.079 - 0.118 in) Clean the rocker cover, ensure it is free from grease. Fit the gasket & cover within 5 minutes of applying the liquid gasket, as follows: Apply liquid gasket to the cylinder head as shown. Fit the gasket to the cover. Liquid gasket application dimensions: 3.0 (0.118) mm and 3.0 (0.018) mm Unit: mm (in) Apply liquid gasket to the rubber in the distributor bracket aperture, as shown. Apply sealant 20 (0.79) mm on each side of the gasket at the distributor bracket aperture. 261.title: TIMING CHAIN — Installation (Cont'd)
261.body: TIMING CHAIN — Installation (Cont'd) EM-24 Installation (Cont'd) Apply liquid gasket to thermostat housing. Ensure mating surfaces are clean and free from dirt, grease and traces of old liquid gasket. Install thermostat housing. Install distributor. Make sure that position of camshaft is as shown in figure. Apply liquid gasket to cam sprocket cover as illustrated. Then install cam sprocket cover. Clean the rocker cover, ensure it is free from grease. Fit the gasket & cover within 5 minutes of applying the liquid gasket, as follows: Apply liquid gasket to the cylinder head as shown. Fit the gasket to the cover. 263.title: TIMING CHAIN - Installation (Cont'd)
263.body: TIMING CHAIN - Installation (Cont'd) EM-25 Installation (Cont'd) Fit the rocker cover to the cylinder head. Tighten the bolts in the correct order, as shown. Install all spark plugs. Install engine front mounting bracket. Install front exhaust tube. Install the front and side splashguards. Install front right wheel. Install air cleaner. Install and correctly tension the alternator and power steering drive belts. Refer to MA section for correct tension. Refer to MA section for correct tension. Install the front and side splashguards. Install front right wheel. Install air cleaner. Install and correctly tension the alternator and power steering drive belts. Refer to MA section for correct tension. Refer to MA section for correct tension. 275.title: CYLINDER HEAD
275.body: CYLINDER HEAD EM-29 During reassembly, always apply new engine oil to bearing surfaces, e.g. camshaft, and oil seal contact surfaces. When tightening cylinder head bolts, camshaft sprocket bolts and camshaft bracket bolts, apply new engine oil to threads and seat surfaces of bolts. Identify individual components such as valve lifters, to ensure correct assembly. Refer to "TIMING CHAIN" section for cylinder head removal. Disassembly Remove valve components with Service Tool. Remove valve oil seal. Head surface flatness - Standard Less than 0.03 mm (0.0012 in) Head surface flatness Head surface flatness - Limit 0.1 mm (0.004 in) Head surface flatness If beyond the specified limit, replace or re-surface. Removing valve components with service tool on cylinder head Removing valve oil seal using tool KV10107902 Checking cylinder head surface flatness/distortion with straight edge

278.title: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd)
278.body: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) EM-30 Check camshaft for scratches, seizure and wear. CAMSHAFT RUNOUT Measure camshaft runout at the center journal. If it exceeds the limit, replace camshaft. Runout (Total indicator reading) — Standard Less than 0.02 mm (0.0008 in) Camshaft runout at center journal Runout (Total indicator reading) — Limit 0.1 mm (0.004 in) Camshaft runout at center journal CAMSHAFT CAM HEIGHT Measure camshaft cam height. If wear is beyond the limit, replace camshaft. Standard cam height — Intake & Exhaust — CG10DE 38.005 - 38.195 mm (1.4963 - 1.5037 in) Standard cam height Standard cam height — Intake & Exhaust — CG13DE 39.880 - 40.070 mm (1.5701 - 1.5776 in) Standard cam height Cam wear limit — Intake & Exhaust 0.20 mm (0.0079 in) Cam wear limit CAMSHAFT JOURNAL CLEARANCE Install camshaft bracket and tighten bolts to the specified torque. Measure inner diameter of camshaft bearing. Standard inner diameter (intake and exhaust) — No. 1 bearing 28.000 - 28.021 mm (1.1024 - 1.1032 in) Camshaft bearing inner diameter Standard inner diameter (intake and exhaust) — No. 2 to No. 5 bearings 23.500 - 23.521 mm (0.9252 - 0.9260 in) Camshaft bearing inner diameter Cam height specs differentiated by engine variant: CG10DE and CG13DE. Journal clearance specs apply to both intake and exhaust camshafts. Dial indicator measuring camshaft runout at the center journal Micrometer measuring camshaft cam height Measuring inner diameter of camshaft bearing with bore gauge after installing camshaft bracket

If it exceeds the limit, replace camshaft. Standard cam height: Intake & Exhaust — CG10DE: 38.005 - 38.195 mm (1.4963 - 1.5037 in); CG13DE: 39.880 - 40.070 mm (1.5701 - 1.5776 in). Cam wear limit: Intake & Exhaust — 0.20 mm (0.0079 in). If wear is beyond the limit, replace camshaft. Measure inner diameter of camshaft bearing. Standard inner diameter (intake and exhaust): No. 1 bearing: 28.000 - 28.021 mm (1.1024 - 1.1032 in). No. 2 to No. 5 bearings: 23.500 - 23.521 mm (0.9252 - 0.9260 in). Approx. 25 mm (0.98 in) measurement position from valve tip Valve deflection limit (Dial gauge reading) – Intake & Exhaust 0.2 mm Intake & Exhaust Valve deflection limit (Dial gauge reading) – Intake & Exhaust (in) 0.008 in Intake & Exhaust Measuring outer diameter of camshaft journal with a micrometer Measuring camshaft end play with a dial gauge indicator on the camshaft installed in the cylinder head Measuring camshaft sprocket runout with a dial gauge indicator Measuring valve deflection (valve guide clearance) with a dial gauge in a parallel direction with rocker arm, approx. 25 mm (0.98 in) from valve tip

284.title: CYLINDER HEAD - Inspection (Cont'd) / Valve Guide Replacement
284.body: CYLINDER HEAD - Inspection (Cont'd) / Valve Guide Replacement EM-32 Inspection (Cont'd) If it exceeds the limit, check valve to valve guide clearance. a. Measure valve stem diameter and valve guide inner diameter. b. Check that clearance is within specification. c. If it exceeds the limit, replace valve or valve guide. Valve to valve guide clearance Standard Limit Intake 0.020 - 0.050 mm (0.0008 - 0.0020 in) 0.110 mm (0.0043 in) Exhaust 0.040 - 0.070 mm (0.0016 - 0.0028 in) 0.130 mm (0.0051 in) Valve Guide Replacement To remove valve guide, heat cylinder head to 110 to 120°C (230 to 248°F). Press out valve guide or use a hammer and suitable tool. Ream cylinder head valve guide hole. Valve guide hole diameter (for service parts) - Intake & Exhaust 9.680 - 9.695 mm (0.3811 - 0.3817 in) For service parts Micrometer being used to measure valve stem diameter and valve guide inner diameter Cylinder head being heated in oil bath for valve guide removal Pressing out valve guide using hammer and suitable tool Reaming cylinder head valve guide hole

287.title: CYLINDER HEAD - Inspection (Cont'd) / Valve Seats
287.body: CYLINDER HEAD - Inspection (Cont'd) / Valve Seats EM-33 Inspection (Cont'd) Heat cylinder head to 110 to 120°C (230 to 248°F) and press service valve guide into cylinder head. Projection "L": 11.5 - 11.7 mm (0.453 - 0.461 in) Ream valve guide. Finished size: Intake & Exhaust 5.500 - 5.515 mm (0.2165 - 0.2171 in) Valve guide projection "L" (service) 11.5 - 11.7 mm (0.453 - 0.461 in) After pressing service valve guide into heated cylinder head Valve guide finished size (Intake & Exhaust) 5.500 - 5.515 mm (0.2165 - 0.2171 in) After reaming Check valve seats for any evidence of pitting at valve contact surface, and reset or replace if it has worn out excessively. Before repairing valve seats, check valve and valve guide for wear. If they have worn, replace them. Then correct valve seat. Cut with both hands to uniform the cutting surface. Replacing Valve Seat for Service Parts Bore out old seat until it collapses. The machine depth stop should be set so that boring cannot continue beyond the bottom face of the seat recess in cylinder head. Ream cylinder head recess. Reaming bore for service valve seat Oversize [0.5 mm (0.020 in)]: Intake 29.500 - 29.516 mm (1.1614 - 1.1620 in) Exhaust 25.500 - 25.516 mm (1.0039 - 1.0046 in) Reaming should be done in circles concentric to the valve guide center so that valve seat will have the correct fit. Reaming bore for service valve seat - Intake (Oversize 0.5 mm / 0.020 in) 29.500 - 29.516 mm (1.1614 - 1.1620 in) Service oversize 0.5 mm (0.020 in) Reaming bore for service valve seat - Exhaust (Oversize 0.5 mm / 0.020 in) 25.500 - 25.516 mm (1.0039 - 1.0046 in) Service oversize 0.5 mm (0.020 in) Cylinder head being heated, showing valve guide installation area Reaming valve guide in cylinder head Cutting/resetting valve seat with tool Diagram showing recess diameter for valve seat in cylinder head

288.title: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) / Valve Seats / Replacing Valve Seat for Service Parts
288.body: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) / Valve Seats / Replacing Valve Seat for Service Parts EM-33 Inspection (Cont'd) Heat cylinder head to 110 to 120°C (230 to 248°F) and press service valve guide into cylinder head. Ream valve guide. Check valve seats for any evidence of pitting at valve contact surface, and reset or replace if it has worn out excessively. Before repairing valve seats, check valve and valve guide for wear. If they have worn, replace them. Then correct valve seat. Cut with both hands to uniform the cutting surface. Replacing Valve Seat for Service Parts Bore out old seat until it collapses. The machine depth stop should be set so that boring cannot continue beyond the bottom face of the seat recess in cylinder head. Ream cylinder head recess. Reaming should be done in circles concentric to the valve guide center so that valve seat will have the correct fit. 290.title: CYLINDER HEAD - Inspection (Cont'd)
290.body: CYLINDER HEAD - Inspection (Cont'd) EM-34 Valve Seat Inspection (Continued) Heat cylinder head to 110 to 120°C (230 to 248°F). Press fit valve seat until it seats on the bottom. Cut or grind valve seat using suitable tool at the specified dimensions as shown in S.D.S. After cutting, lap valve seat with abrasive compound. Check valve seating condition. Seat face angle "α" 45°15' - 45°45' deg Contacting width "W" - Intake 1.34 - 1.63 mm (0.0528 - 0.0642 in) Contacting width "W" - Exhaust 1.70 - 2.12 mm (0.0669 - 0.0835 in) Check dimensions in each valve. For dimensions, refer to S.D.S. When valve head has been worn down to 0.5 mm (0.020 in) in margin thickness, replace valve. Grinding allowance for valve stem tip is 0.2 mm (0.008 in) or less. Valve Spring Squareness Measure "S" dimension. If it exceeds the limit, replace spring. Out-of-square Less than 1.74 mm (0.0685 in) Check valve spring pressure. Pressure: Standard 370 (37.7, 83.1) at 23.64 N (kg, lb) at height mm (0.9307 in) Standard Pressure: Limit More than 330.41 (33.69, 74.31) at 23.64 N (kg, lb) at height mm (0.9307 in) Limit If it exceeds the limit, replace spring. Cylinder head submerged in oil bath for heating prior to valve seat press fit Valve seat cross-section showing seat face angle alpha and contacting width W Valve dimensions diagram showing margin thickness T, face angle alpha, stem diameter d, head diameter D, and length L Valve spring squareness measurement showing out-of-square dimension S Valve spring pressure tester being used to check valve spring pressure

293.title: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) / Assembly / Installation
293.body: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) / Assembly / Installation EM-35 Valve Lifter Inspection Check contact and sliding surfaces for wear or scratches. Check diameter of valve lifter and valve lifter guide bore. Valve lifter diameter 29.960 - 29.975 mm (1.1795 - 1.1801 in) Lifter guide bore diameter 30.000 - 30.021 mm (1.1811 - 1.1819 in) Assembly Install valve component parts. Always use new valve oil seal. Refer to OIL SEAL REPLACEMENT. Before installing valve oil seal, install valve spring seat. After installing valve component parts, use plastic hammer to lightly tap valve stem tip to assure a proper fit. This installation is the same procedure as those for timing chain. Refer to "Installation" in "TIMING CHAIN". Valve lifter showing contact and sliding surfaces with arrows indicating the surfaces to check Measuring valve lifter diameter with a micrometer Measuring lifter guide bore diameter in the cylinder head

294.title: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) / Assembly / Installation
294.body: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) / Assembly / Installation EM-35 Valve Lifter Inspection Check contact and sliding surfaces for wear or scratches. Check diameter of valve lifter and valve lifter guide bore. Valve lifter diameter 29.960 - 29.975 mm (1.1795 - 1.1801 in) Lifter guide bore diameter 30.000 - 30.021 mm (1.1811 - 1.1819 in) Assembly Install valve component parts. Always use new valve oil seal. Refer to OIL SEAL REPLACEMENT. Before installing valve oil seal, install valve spring seat. Install uneven pitch type spring with narrow pitch end toward cylinder head. After installing valve component parts, use plastic hammer to lightly tap valve stem tip to assure a proper fit. This installation is the same procedure as those for timing chain. Refer to "Installation" in "TIMING CHAIN". Valve lifter showing contact and sliding surfaces with arrows indicating inspection areas Measuring valve lifter diameter with a micrometer Measuring valve lifter guide bore diameter in the cylinder head Valve spring orientation diagram showing wide pitch (top) and narrow pitch (bottom, cylinder head side)

295.title: VALVE CLEARANCE
295.body: VALVE CLEARANCE EM-36 Check valve clearance while engine is warmed up sufficiently and not running. Checking Remove rocker cover. Remove all spark plugs. Set No. 1 cylinder at T.D.C. on its compression stroke. Align pointer with T.D.C. mark on crankshaft pulley. Check that valve lifters on No. 1 cylinder are loose and valve lifters on No. 4 are tight. If not, turn crankshaft one revolution (360°) and align as above. Check only those valves shown in the figure. Using a feeler gauge, measure clearance between valve lifter and camshaft. Record any valve clearance measurements which are out of specification. They will be used later to determine the required replacement adjusting shim. Turn crankshaft one revolution (360°) and align mark on crankshaft pulley with pointer. Check those valves shown in the figure. Use the same procedure as mentioned in step 4. If all valve clearances are within specification, install the following parts: Rocker cover. All spark plugs. Valve clearance for checking (Hot) — Intake 0.33 - 0.41 mm Hot Valve clearance for checking (Hot) — Intake 0.0130 - 0.0161 in Hot Valve clearance for checking (Hot) — Exhaust 0.36 - 0.44 mm Hot Valve clearance for checking (Hot) — Exhaust 0.0142 - 0.0173 in Hot Adjust valve clearance while engine is cold. Adjusting Turn crankshaft, to position cam lobe on camshaft of valve that must be adjusted upward. Place Tool (A) around camshaft as shown in figure. Be careful not to damage cam surface with Tool (A). Before placing Tool (A), rotate notch toward center of cylinder head (See figure.), to simplify shim removal later. Before placing Tool (A), rotate notch toward center of cylinder head (See figure.), to simplify shim removal later. CAUTION: Be careful not to damage cam surface with Tool (A). Crankshaft pulley showing TDC mark and pointer, with degree markings 5°, 10°, 15°, 20° visible Top view of cylinder head showing valve positions for first check (No.1 cylinder at TDC compression stroke) — valves numbered 1,1,2,2 on INT side and 1,1,3,3 on EXH side Photo showing feeler gauge being used to measure clearance between valve lifter and camshaft Top view of cylinder head showing valve positions for second check (after 360° rotation) — valves numbered 3,3,4,4 on INT side and 2,2,4,4 on EXH side Tool (A) KV10115110 placed around camshaft with notch visible, used for shim removal during valve clearance adjustment

296.title: VALVE CLEARANCE
296.body: VALVE CLEARANCE EM-36 Check valve clearance while engine is warmed up sufficiently and not running. Checking Valve Clearance Remove rocker cover. Remove all spark plugs. Set No. 1 cylinder at T.D.C. on its compression stroke. Align pointer with T.D.C. mark on crankshaft pulley. Check that valve lifters on No. 1 cylinder are loose and valve lifters on No. 4 are tight. If not, turn crankshaft one revolution (360°) and align as above. Check only those valves shown in the figure. Using a feeler gauge, measure clearance between valve lifter and camshaft. Record any valve clearance measurements which are out of specification. They will be used later to determine the required replacement adjusting shim. Valve clearance for checking (Hot) — Intake 0.32 - 0.40 mm Hot Valve clearance for checking (Hot) — Intake 0.0126 - 0.0157 in Hot Valve clearance for checking (Hot) — Exhaust 0.36 - 0.44 mm Hot Valve clearance for checking (Hot) — Exhaust 0.0142 - 0.0173 in Hot Checking Valve Clearance (continued) Turn crankshaft one revolution (360°) and align mark on crankshaft pulley with pointer. Check those valves shown in the figure. Use the same procedure as mentioned in step 4. If all valve clearances are within specification, install the following parts: Rocker cover. All spark plugs. Adjust valve clearance while engine is cold. Adjusting Valve Clearance Turn crankshaft, to position cam lobe on camshaft of valve that must be adjusted upward. Place Tool (A) around camshaft as shown in figure. Before placing Tool (A), rotate notch toward center of cylinder head (See figure.), to simplify shim removal later. CAUTION: Be careful not to damage cam surface with Tool (A). Crankshaft pulley showing timing marks: 5° ATDC, TDC, 5°, 10°, 15°, 20° BTDC with pointer Top view of cylinder head showing valve positions for No.1 cylinder at TDC compression stroke. Valves labeled 1,1,2,2 (INT) and 1,1,3,3 (EXH) Photo showing feeler gauge being used to measure valve clearance between valve lifter and camshaft Top view of cylinder head showing valve positions after 360° crankshaft rotation. Valves labeled 3,3,4,4 (INT) and 2,2,4,4 (EXH) Tool (A) KV10115110 placed around camshaft with notch visible, used for shim removal during valve clearance adjustment

297.title: VALVE CLEARANCE — Adjusting (Cont'd)
297.body: VALVE CLEARANCE — Adjusting (Cont'd) EM-37 Adjusting (Cont'd) Rotate Tool (A) (See figure.) so that valve lifter is pushed down. Place Tool (B) between camshaft and valve lifter to retain valve lifter. CAUTION: Tool (B) must be placed as close to camshaft bracket as possible. CAUTION: Be careful not to damage cam surface with Tool (B). Remove Tool (A). Remove adjusting shim using a small screwdriver and a magnetic finger. Determine replacement adjusting shim size following formula. Using a micrometer determine thickness of removed shim. Calculate thickness of new adjusting shim so valve clearance comes within specified values. R = Thickness of removed shim, N = Thickness of new shim, M = Measured valve clearance. Intake: N = R + [M − 0.37 mm (0.0146 in)]. Exhaust: N = R + [M − 0.40 mm (0.0157 in)]. Shims are available in 50 sizes from 2.00 mm (0.0787 in) to 2.98 mm (0.1173 in), in steps of 0.02 mm (0.0008 in). Select new shim with thickness as close as possible to calculated value. CAUTION: Tool (B) must be placed as close to camshaft bracket as possible. Be careful not to damage cam surface with Tool (B). Intake shim formula N = R + [M − 0.37 mm (0.0146 in)] mm (in) Intake valve clearance adjustment Exhaust shim formula N = R + [M − 0.40 mm (0.0157 in)] mm (in) Exhaust valve clearance adjustment Shim size range — minimum 2.00 mm (0.0787 in) Available shim sizes Shim size range — maximum 2.98 mm (0.1173 in) Available shim sizes Shim size increment (step) 0.02 mm (0.0008 in) Available shim sizes — 50 sizes total Example shim identification mark TS 224 2.24 mm (0.0882 in) Shim identification mark example Adjusting shim identification mark — example shows TS 224 = 2.24 mm (0.0882 in) Identification mark TS 224 Tool (A) being rotated to push valve lifter down — engine top view Tool (A) and Tool (B) KV10115120 positioned between camshaft and valve lifter to retain valve lifter Tool (B) retaining valve lifter with magnetic finger removing adjusting shim via small screwdriver Micrometer being used to measure thickness of removed adjusting shim Adjusting shim identification mark example: TS 224 = 2.24 mm (0.0882 in)

300.title: VALVE CLEARANCE — Adjusting (Cont'd)
300.body: VALVE CLEARANCE — Adjusting (Cont'd) EM-38 Adjusting (Cont'd) Install new shim using a suitable tool. Install with the surface on which the thickness is stamped facing down. Place Tool (A) as mentioned in steps 2 and 3. Remove Tool (B). Remove Tool (A). Recheck valve clearance. Valve clearance for adjusting Hot Cold* (reference data) Intake 0.32 - 0.40 mm (0.013 - 0.016 in) 0.25 - 0.33 mm (0.010 - 0.013 in) Exhaust 0.36 - 0.44 mm (0.0142 - 0.0173 in) 0.32 - 0.40 mm (0.013 - 0.016 in) *: At a temperature of approximately 20°C (68°F) If valve clearances are set under cold conditions, i.e. during an engine rebuild, check that the clearances are correct after the engine has been run to normal operating temperature. Adjust if necessary. Photo showing installation of new shim using Tool (B) and a suitable tool, with the stamped surface facing down Photo showing placement of Tool (A) and Tool (B) during valve clearance adjustment procedure (steps 9-11)

302.title: VALVE CLEARANCE — Adjusting (Cont'd)
302.body: VALVE CLEARANCE — Adjusting (Cont'd) EM-38 Adjusting (Cont'd) Install new shim using a suitable tool. Install with the surface on which the thickness is stamped facing down. Place Tool (A) as mentioned in steps 2 and 3. Remove Tool (B). Remove Tool (A). Recheck valve clearance. Valve clearance for adjusting Hot Cold* (reference data) Intake 0.32 - 0.40 mm (0.013 - 0.016 in) 0.25 - 0.33 mm (0.010 - 0.013 in) Exhaust 0.36 - 0.44 mm (0.0142 - 0.0173 in) 0.32 - 0.40 mm (0.013 - 0.016 in) *: At a temperature of approximately 20°C (68°F) If valve clearances are set under cold conditions, i.e. during an engine rebuild, check that the clearances are correct after the engine has been run to normal operating temperature. Adjust if necessary. Illustration showing installation of new shim using Tool (B) and a suitable tool Illustration showing Tool (A) and Tool (B) placement on the camshaft/bucket assembly

306.title: ENGINE REMOVAL
306.body: ENGINE REMOVAL EM-40 Do not remove engine until exhaust system has completely cooled off. When lifting engine, take care not to foul other components, cables and wires. Pay special attention to brake lines, brake master cylinder, and radiator (fan) assembly. When hoisting the engine, always use engine slingers in a safe and secure manner. When removing drive shaft, be careful not to damage the transmission oil seal. Engine cannot be removed separately from vehicle. Remove engine with transaxle. Removal Drive vehicle on to a two post lift. If a two post lift is not available, place the vehicle on axle stands on a flat and solid surface. Drain transmission oil. Disconnect gear selector rods from transaxle (M/T). Disconnect NCVT selector cable (NCVT). Remove stabiliser bar. Remove lower suspension arm bolts. Disconnect drive shafts from transaxle. Remove exhaust by disconnecting at front (below first catalytic converter) and at rear (at rear axle). Make sure to disconnect the earth strap at the second catalytic converter. Remove hood. Release fuel pressure. Refer to section MA. Disconnect battery harness and remove battery. Remove front splash guards. Drain cooling system by removing lower radiator hose. Remove radiator expansion tank for access. Remove air filter assembly. Disconnect gearbox earth strap. Disconnect exhaust gas harness. Disconnect distributor harness and distributor sensor harness. Disconnect connectors for access for battery tray removal. Remove battery tray. Disconnect gear position switch harness (M/T). Disconnect clutch cable (M/T). Disconnect radiator top hose. On NCVT models, also disconnect two lower hoses from radiator. Disconnect earth wires on engine bracket. Disconnect injector harness. Disconnect 3 connectors on throttle chamber. Disconnect 2 connectors on main relay box. Disconnect earth strap on inner wing. Steps 3, 21, 22 apply to M/T models only. Step 4 applies to NCVT models only. Step 23 notes additional hoses for NCVT models.

307.title: ENGINE REMOVAL
307.body:
ENGINE REMOVAL EM-40 Do not remove engine until exhaust system has completely cooled off. When lifting engine, take care not to foul other components, cables and wires. Pay special attention to brake lines, brake master cylinder, and radiator (fan) assembly.
When hoisting the engine, always use engine slingers in a safe and secure manner.
When removing drive shaft, be careful not to damage the transmission oil seal.
Engine cannot be removed separately from vehicle.
Remove engine with transaxle. Removal Drive vehicle on to a two post lift. If a two post lift is not available, place the vehicle on axle stands on a flat and solid surface. Drain transmission oil. Disconnect gear selector rods from transaxle (M/T). Disconnect NCVT selector cable (NCVT). Remove stabiliser bar. Remove lower suspension arm bolts. Disconnect drive shafts from transaxle. Remove exhaust by disconnecting at front (below first catalytic converter) and at rear (at rear axle). Make sure to disconnect the earth strap at the second catalytic converter. Remove hood. Release fuel pressure. Refer to section MA. Disconnect battery harness and remove battery. Remove front splash guards. Drain cooling system by removing lower radiator hose. Remove radiator reservoir tank for access. Remove air filter assembly. Disconnect gearbox earth strap. Disconnect exhaust gas sensor harness. Disconnect distributor harness and crank angle sensor harness. Disconnect connectors for access for battery tray removal. Remove battery tray. Disconnect gear position switch harness (M/T). Disconnect clutch cable (M/T). Disconnect radiator top hose. On NCVT models, also disconnect two lower hoses from radiator. Disconnect earth wires on engine bracket. Disconnect injector harness. Disconnect 3 connectors on throttle chamber. Disconnect 2 connectors on main relay box. Disconnect earth strap on inner wing. Steps 3, 21, 22 apply to M/T models only. Step 4 applies to NCVT models. Step 23 notes additional hose disconnection for NCVT models. Engine must be removed with transaxle.

When removing drive shaft, be careful not to damage the transmission oil seal. Engine cannot be removed separately from vehicle. Remove engine with transaxle. Removal Drive vehicle on to a two post lift. If a two post lift is not available, place the vehicle on axle stands on a flat and solid surface. Drain transmission oil. Disconnect gear selector rods from transaxle (M/T). Disconnect NCVT selector cable (NCVT). Remove stabiliser bar. Remove lower suspension arm bolts. Disconnect drive shafts from transaxle. 309.title: ENGINE REMOVAL — Removal (Cont'd) / Installation
309.body: ENGINE REMOVAL — Removal (Cont'd) / Installation EM-41 Removal (Cont'd) Remove engine harness from harness saddle on engine mounting. Disconnect two engine temperature sensors. Remove main earth lead from body. On power steering equipped models, slacken water pump drive belt. Refer to section MA. Loosen and remove power steering pump fixing bolts. Tie power steering pump onto vehicle body. Disconnect fuel lines. Disconnect throttle cable. Disconnect kickdown cable (NCVT). Disconnect speedometer cable. Disconnect carbon canister hoses. Disconnect brake vacuum hose from brake servo. On air conditioning equipped models, slacken alternator drive belt. Refer to section MA. Remove alternator. Loosen and remove air conditioning compressor fixing bolts. Tie air conditioning compressor onto vehicle body. Attach engine slinger brackets to engine. Engine slinger brackets and engine slinger bracket bolts are described in PARTS CATALOG. Remove 4 centermember mounting bolts from vehicle body. Remove centermember from engine. Attach slingers to engine slinger brackets and use crane to release load from both top engine mountings. Disconnect or remove engine mountings. Lower engine onto transmission jack or floor. Installation Install top engine mounting brackets and fixing bolts. Carefully move engine onto engine mounting insulators. Ensure that insulators are positioned on brackets correctly. Engine mounting bracket clearance (top-left) 10 mm (0.39 in) Clearance at engine mounting bracket — see UEM069 diagram Engine mounting bracket clearance (top-right) 10 mm (0.39 in) Clearance at engine mounting bracket — see UEM069 diagram Engine mounting bracket clearance (bottom-left) 10 mm (0.39 in) Clearance at engine mounting bracket — see UEM069 diagram Engine mounting bracket clearance (bottom-right) 10 mm (0.39 in) Clearance at engine mounting bracket — see UEM069 diagram Steps 32 and 38 apply to power steering equipped and air conditioning equipped models respectively. Step 34 references kickdown cable for NCVT (CVT) models. Power steering pump on engine, showing location for removal/tie-off to vehicle body Air conditioning compressor location on engine, showing fixing bolts for removal/tie-off to vehicle body Engine slinger brackets attached to engine, with crane slingers in position Centremember mounting bolt locations on vehicle body underside Diagram of engine mounting bracket showing clearance dimensions: 10 mm (0.39 in) on all four sides

Remove main earth lead from body. On power steering equipped models, slacken water pump drive belt. Refer to section MA. Loosen and remove power-steering pump fixing bolts. Tie power steering pump onto vehicle body. Disconnect fuel lines. Disconnect throttle cable. Disconnect kickdown cable (N·CVT). Disconnect speedometer cable. Disconnect carbon canister hoses. Disconnect brake vacuum hose from brake servo. On air conditioning equipped models, slacken alternator drive belt. Refer to section MA. Remove alternator. Loosen and remove air conditioning compressor fixing bolts. Tie air conditioning compressor onto vehicle body. Attach engine slinger brackets to engine. Engine slinger brackets and engine slinger bracket bolts are described in K11 PARTS CATALOG. Tighten engine slinger bracket bolts. Remove 4 centermember mounting bolts from vehicle body. Remove centermember from engine. Attach slingers to engine slinger brackets and use crane to release load from both top engine mountings. Disconnect or remove engine mountings. Lower engine onto transmission jack or floor. Engine slinger bracket bolt torque 16 - 21 N·m Tightening torque Engine slinger bracket bolt torque 1.6 - 2.1 kg-m Tightening torque Engine slinger bracket bolt torque 11.6 - 15.2 lb-ft Tightening torque Step 32 applies to power steering equipped models only. Step 34 kickdown cable applies to N·CVT models only. Step 38 applies to air conditioning equipped models only. 318.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Disassembly (Piston and Crankshaft)
318.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Disassembly (Piston and Crankshaft) EM-44 When installing moving parts such as bearings and pistons, always apply new, unused engine oil on the contact surfaces. Place disassembled parts such as bearings and bearing caps in correct order and direction. When tightening connecting rod bolts and main bearing cap bolts, apply engine oil to threads and seating surface of nuts. Piston and Crankshaft Disassembly Place engine on a work stand. Drain coolant and oil. Remove timing chain. Refer to "Removal" in "TIMING CHAIN". 327.title: CYLINDER BLOCK - Inspection (Cont'd)

Piston pin hole inner diameter - Piston pin outer diameter If it exceeds the above value, replace piston assembly with pin. Piston pin standard diameter 17.995 - 18.000 mm (0.7085 - 0.7087 in) Piston pin clearance (CG10DE) 0.018 to 0.022 mm (0.0007 - 0.0009 in) Piston pin hole inner diameter - Piston pin outer diameter Piston pin clearance (CG13DE) 0.008 to 0.012 mm (0.0003 - 0.0015 in) Piston pin hole inner diameter - Piston pin outer diameter Side clearance - Top ring 0.040 - 0.080 mm (0.0016 - 0.0031 in) Side clearance - 2nd ring 0.030 - 0.070 mm (0.0012 - 0.0028 in) Max. limit of side clearance 0.2 mm (0.008 in) If out of specification, replace piston and/or piston ring assembly. End gap - Top ring 0.18 - 0.28 mm (0.0071 - 0.0110 in) End gap - 2nd ring 0.30 - 0.45 mm (0.0118 - 0.0177 in) End gap - Oil ring 0.25 - 0.60 mm (0.0098 - 0.0236 in) Max. limit of ring gap - Top ring 0.37 mm (0.0146 in) Max. limit of ring gap - 2nd ring 0.54 mm (0.0213 in) Max. limit of ring gap - Oil ring 0.69 mm (0.0272 in) If out of specification, replace piston ring. If gap still exceeds the limit even with a new ring, rebore cylinder and use oversized piston and piston rings. Refer to S.D.S. Bend limit 0.15 mm (0.0059 in) per 100 mm (3.94 in) length Torsion limit 0.3 mm (0.012 in) per 100 mm (3.94 in) length If it exceeds the limit, replace connecting rod assembly. Piston pin clearance values differ between CG10DE and CG13DE engines. 330.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd)
330.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) EM-48 Cylinder Block Distortion and Wear Clean upper face of cylinder block. Cover the oil hole to cylinder head with a shop towel before removing all traces of cylinder head gasket. This will prevent blockage of the oil hole. Measure the distortion of the upper face of cylinder block. Limit: 0.10 mm (0.0039 in) If out of specification, resurface it. If necessary, replace cylinder block. Cylinder block upper face distortion limit 0.10 mm (0.0039 in) Piston-to-Bore Clearance Using a bore gauge, measure cylinder bore for wear, out-of-round and taper. Standard inner diameter: 71.0 - 71.03 mm (2.7953 - 2.7965 in). Wear limit: 0.2 mm (0.008 in). Out-of-round (X–Y) limit: 0.015 mm (0.0006 in). Taper (A–B) limit: 0.010 mm (0.0004 in). If it exceeds the limit, rebore all cylinders. Replace cylinder block if necessary. Check for scratches and seizure. If seizure is found, hone it. Measure piston skirt diameter. Piston diameter 'A': 70.980 - 71.010 mm (2.7945 - 2.7957 in). Measuring point 'a' (Distance from the bottom): 9.5 mm (0.374 in) Check that piston-to-bore clearance is within specification. Piston-to-bore clearance 'B': 0.010 - 0.030 mm (0.0004 - 0.0012 in) If both cylinder block and piston are replaced with new ones, select piston of the same grade number punched on cylinder block lower surface. 331.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd)
331.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) EM-48 Cylinder Block Distortion and Wear Clean upper face of cylinder block. Measure the distortion of the upper face of cylinder block. Limit: 0.10 mm (0.0039 in) If out of specification, resurface it. The resurfacing limit is determined by cylinder head resurfacing in engine. Amount of cylinder head resurfacing is "A". Amount of cylinder block resurfacing is "B". The maximum limit is as follows: A + B = 0.2 mm (0.008 in). Nominal cylinder block height from crankshaft center: 188.95 - 189.05 mm (7.4390 - 7.4429 in) If necessary, replace cylinder block. 333.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd)
333.body:
CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) EM-49 Cylinder Block Inspection (Continued) Determine piston oversize according to amount of cylinder wear. Oversize pistons are available for service. Refer to S.D.S. Cylinder bore size is determined by adding piston-to-bore clearance to piston diameter "A".
Rebored size calculation:
D = A + B - C
where,
D: Bored diameter
A: Piston diameter as measured
B: Piston-to-bore clearance
C: Honing allowance 0.02 mm (0.0008 in) Install main bearing caps, and tighten to the specified torque to prevent distortion of cylinder bores in final assembly. Cut cylinder bores. When any cylinder needs boring, all other cylinders must also be bored. Ensure that boring machines instructions are closely followed. Hone cylinders to obtain specified piston-to-bore clearance. Measure finished cylinder bore for out-of-round and taper. Measurement should be done after cylinder bore cools down. Crankshaft Inspection Check crankshaft main and pin journals for score, wear or cracks. With a micrometer, measure journals for taper and out-of-round. Measure crankshaft runout. Out-of-round (X - Y) Less than 0.005 mm (0.0002 in) Crankshaft main and pin journals Taper (A - B) Less than 0.002 mm (0.0001 in) Crankshaft main and pin journals Runout (Total indicator reading) Less than 0.05 mm (0.0020 in) Crankshaft runout Diagram illustrating crankshaft journal taper (A-B) and out-of-round (X-Y) measurement directions Photo showing measurement of crankshaft runout using a dial indicator

336.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) — BEARING CLEARANCE
336.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) — BEARING CLEARANCE EM-50 Main bearing clearance measurement Set main bearings in their proper positions on cylinder block and main bearing cap. Install main bearing cap to cylinder block. Tighten all bolts in correct order in two or three stages. Tighten all bolts in correct order in two or three stages. Measure inner diameter of each main bearing. Measure outer diameter of each main journal in crankshaft. Calculate main bearing clearance. Main bearing clearance = Inner dia. - Outer dia. Standard: 0.010 - 0.044 mm (0.0004 - 0.0017 in). Limit: 0.064 mm (0.0025 in). If it exceeds the limit, replace bearing. If clearance cannot be adjusted within the standard of any bearing, grind crankshaft journal and use undersized bearing. Main bearing clearance — Standard 0.010 - 0.044 mm Main bearing clearance — Standard 0.0004 - 0.0017 in Main bearing clearance — Limit 0.064 mm Main bearing clearance — Limit 0.0025 in When grinding crank pin and crank journal: a. Grind until clearance is within specified standard bearing clearance. b. Fillets should be finished as shown in the figure. Refer to S.D.S. for standard bearing clearance and available spare parts. Fillet radius — CG10DE R2.3 - 2.7 mm Crank pin and crank journal fillet Fillet radius — CG13DE R1.5 mm Crank pin and crank journal fillet Upper bearing Has oil hole Thrust bearing Only upper bearing; No.5 bearing position Lower bearing Has identification color and oil groove No.1 bearing Front position Fillet radius differs by engine: CG10DE R2.3-2.7 mm; CG13DE R1.5 mm Exploded/layout diagram of main bearings showing upper bearing, thrust bearing (only upper), No.5 bearing, oil hole, lower bearing, identification color, oil groove, front direction, and No.1 bearing positions Photo showing main bearing cap installed on cylinder block with measuring equipment for inner diameter measurement Photo showing measurement of outer diameter of crankshaft main journal using a micrometer Diagram showing crank pin/journal fillet dimensions: CG10DE R2.3-2.7 mm, CG13DE R1.5 mm, with fillet radius R2.3-2.7 mm indicated

338.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) — BEARING CLEARANCE EM-50 Upper bearing Thrust bearing (only upper) No. 5 bearing Oil hole Lower bearing Identification color Oil groove Front direction indicator No. 1 bearing Main bearing clearance measurement Set main bearings in their proper positions on cylinder block and main bearing cap. If it exceeds the limit, replace bearing. If clearance cannot be adjusted within the standard of any bearing, grind crankshaft journal and use undersized bearing. Fillets should be finished as shown in the figure. Refer to S.D.S. for standard bearing clearance and available spare parts. CG10DE: R2.3-2.7 mm; CG13DE: R1.5 mm for crank journal. 342.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd)
342.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) EM-52 Connecting Rod Bearing Clearance Measure outer diameter of each crankshaft pin journal. Calculate connecting rod bearing clearance. Connecting rod bearing clearance = Inner dia. – Outer dia. If it exceeds the limit, replace bearing. If clearance cannot be adjusted within the standard of any bearing, grind crankshaft journal and use undersized bearing. Refer to step 7 of "BEARING CLEARANCE — Main bearing". If bearing, crankshaft or connecting rod is replaced with a new one, select connecting rod bearing according to the following table. Connecting rod bearing clearance — Standard 0.010 - 0.044 mm (0.0004 - 0.0017 in) Connecting rod bearing clearance = Inner dia. – Outer dia. Connecting rod bearing clearance — Limit 0.064 mm (0.0025 in) Connecting rod bearing thickness Grade Thickness mm (in) Standard 1.504 - 1.508 (0.0592 - 0.0594) Undersize 1.629 - 1.633 (0.0641 - 0.0643) CONNECTING ROD CLEARANCE (Small end) Measure inner diameter of connecting rod small end. Measure outer diameter of piston pin. Calculate connecting rod bushing clearance. Connecting rod bushing clearance = Inner diameter – pin outer diameter If it exceeds the limit, replace connecting rod assembly or connecting rod bushing and/or piston pin. Connecting rod small end inner diameter — Standard 17.962 - 17.978 mm (0.7072 - 0.7078 in) Piston pin outer diameter — Standard 17.995 - 18.000 mm (0.7085 - 0.7087 in) Connecting rod bushing clearance — Standard -0.017 to -0.038 mm (-0.0007 to -0.0015 in) Connecting rod bushing clearance = Inner diameter – pin outer diameter Flywheel Runout (Total indicator reading) Less than 0.15 mm (0.0059 in) Total indicator reading Measuring outer diameter of crankshaft pin journal with a micrometer Measuring inner diameter of connecting rod small end with an inside dial gauge Measuring outer diameter of piston pin with a micrometer Measuring flywheel runout with a dial indicator

345.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Assembly
345.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Assembly EM-53 Assembly Assemble piston, piston pin and connecting rod, using a piston pin press stand. Align the direction of piston and connecting rod. Numbers stamped on connecting rod and cap correspond to each cylinder. After assembly, make sure connecting rod swings smoothly. Set piston rings as shown. CAUTION: When piston rings are not replaced, make sure that piston rings are mounted in their original position. CAUTION: When piston rings are being replaced and no punch mark is present, piston rings can be mounted with either side up. Align the direction of piston and connecting rod. Numbers stamped on connecting rod and cap correspond to each cylinder. After assembly, make sure connecting rod swings smoothly. When piston rings are not replaced, make sure that piston rings are mounted in their original position. When piston rings are being replaced and no punch mark is present, piston rings can be mounted with either side up. 348.title: CYLINDER BLOCK - Assembly (Cont'd) - CRANKSHAFT
348.body: CYLINDER BLOCK - Assembly (Cont'd) - CRANKSHAFT EM-54 Crankshaft Assembly Set main bearings in their proper positions on cylinder block and main bearing cap. Confirm that correct main bearings are used. Refer to "Inspection". Install crankshaft and main bearing beam and tighten bolts as follows: Main bearing beam bolts (1) Tighten to 25 to 30 N·m (2.5 to 3.1 kg-m, 18 - 22 ft-lb) in order shown. (2) Turn bolts to 60° to 65° degrees clockwise with an angle wrench in order shown. Prior to tightening bearing cap bolts, place bearing cap in its proper position by shifting crankshaft in the axial direction. After securing bearing cap bolts, make sure crankshaft turns smoothly by hand. Measure crankshaft end play. If beyond the limit, replace thrust bearing with a new one. Main bearing beam bolts torque - Stage 1 25 to 30 N·m Tighten in order shown Main bearing beam bolts torque - Stage 1 2.5 to 3.1 kg-m Tighten in order shown Main bearing beam bolts torque - Stage 1 18 - 22 ft-lb Tighten in order shown Main bearing beam bolts torque - Stage 2 angle 60° to 65° degrees clockwise Angle tighten in order shown Crankshaft end play - Standard 0.060 - 0.26 mm Standard Crankshaft end play - Standard 0.0024 - 0.0102 in Standard Crankshaft end play - Limit 0.3 mm Limit Crankshaft end play - Limit 0.012 in Limit Upper bearing Has oil hole Thrust bearing Only upper No. 5 bearing Lower bearing Has identification color and oil groove No. 1 bearing Front end Exploded diagram showing main bearing components: upper bearing with oil hole, thrust bearing (only upper), No. 5 bearing, lower bearing with identification color and oil groove, No. 1 bearing, and front orientation Top view of main bearing beam showing bolt tightening sequence order with numbered positions Illustration showing angle wrench being used to tighten main bearing beam bolts 60°-65° clockwise Illustration showing dial gauge setup to measure crankshaft end play

351.title: CYLINDER BLOCK - Assembly (Cont'd)
351.body: CYLINDER BLOCK - Assembly (Cont'd) EM-55 Assembly (Cont'd) Install connecting rod bearings in connecting rods and connecting rod caps. Confirm that correct bearings are used. Refer to "Inspection". Install pistons with connecting rods. Be careful not to scratch cylinder wall by connecting rod. Arrange so that front mark on piston head faces toward front of engine. Install them into corresponding cylinders with Tool. Install connecting rod bearing caps. Tighten connecting rod bearing cap nuts to the specified torque. Measure connecting rod side clearance. Connecting rod side clearance - Standard 0.200 - 0.470 mm (0.0079 - 0.0185 in) Connecting rod side clearance - Limit 0.55 mm (0.0217 in) If beyond the limit, replace connecting rod and/or crankshaft. If beyond the limit, replace connecting rod and/or crankshaft. 354.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Assembly (Cont'd)
354.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Assembly (Cont'd) EM-56 Assembly (Cont'd) When oil seal housing is installed, make sure new oil seal is fitted. Apply liquid gasket to oil seal housing as indicated. Liquid gasket bead width at section A-A 2.0 - 3.0 mm (0.079 - 0.118 in) Oil seal housing mating surface Liquid gasket bead width at section B-B 2.0 - 3.0 mm (0.079 - 0.118 in) Oil seal housing mating surface Oil seal housing diagram showing liquid gasket application areas at sections A-A and B-B, with bead width 2.0–3.0 mm (0.079–0.118 in) indicated at both locations

355.title: CYLINDER BLOCK - Assembly (Cont'd)
355.body: CYLINDER BLOCK - Assembly (Cont'd) EM-56 Assembly (Cont'd) When oil seal housing is installed, make sure new oil seal is fitted. Remove liquid gasket from retainer, including grooves and cylinder block. Apply a 2-3 mm dia. bead of liquid gasket without gaps to oil seal housing as indicated. Assemble retainer within 5 minutes of applying liquid gasket. After assembly wait at least 30 minutes before starting engine. Attach rear plate and flywheel (M/T), drive plate (CVT). For CVT rear plate, scrape off all traces of acrylic tape from cylinder block and clean mating surface. Remove backing paper from acrylic tape before fitting new rear plate. Oil bolt threads and flange prior to attaching. Tighten bolts diagonally. Oil bolt threads and flange prior to attaching. Tighten bolts diagonally. Attach engine front mounting bracket. Attach air conditioning compressor bracket. Liquid gasket bead diameter 2.0 - 3.0 mm (0.079 - 0.118 in) Oil seal housing application points A-A and B-B Tightening torque - oil seal housing retainer 6.3 - 8.3 N·m 0.64 - 0.85 kg-m, 4.6 - 6.1 ft-lb Tightening torque - flywheel and drive plate 83 - 93 N·m 8.5 - 9.5 kg-m, 61 - 69 ft-lb Tightening torque - engine front mounting bracket 64 - 74 N·m 6.5 - 7.5 kg-m, 47 - 54 ft-lb Tightening torque - air conditioning compressor bracket 37 - 50 N·m 3.8 - 5.1 kg-m, 27 - 37 ft-lb Step 8 distinguishes between M/T (flywheel) and CVT (drive plate) variants. CVT rear plate requires additional surface preparation steps. Oil seal housing diagram showing liquid gasket bead application positions A-A and B-B with dimensions 2.0-3.0 mm (0.079-0.118 in) at two locations Rear plate and flywheel/drive plate assembly with special tool KV10105610 Engine front mounting bracket and air conditioning compressor bracket attachment

379.title: OUTER COMPONENT PARTS — Air Cleaner
379.body:
OUTER COMPONENT PARTS — Air Cleaner EM-4002 NOTES:
• Lubricate inner surface of insulator with water to ease fitting
• Ensure insulator is correctly located on flange Cover Metal plate Filter Air cleaner body Hose Insulator See note Rocker cover Resonator PCV-filter Airduct Hose Hose clamp torque (air cleaner body side) 2.9 - 3.9 N·m Manual transmission model Hose clamp torque (air cleaner body side) 0.30 - 0.40 kg·m Manual transmission model Hose clamp torque (air cleaner body side) 2.2 - 2.9 ft·lb Manual transmission model Airduct/resonator/PCV-filter fastener torque 3.7 - 5.0 N·m Manual transmission model Airduct/resonator/PCV-filter fastener torque 0.38 - 0.51 kg·m Manual transmission model Airduct/resonator/PCV-filter fastener torque 2.7 - 3.7 ft·lb Manual transmission model NOTES:
• Lubricate inner surface of insulator with water to ease fitting
• Ensure insulator is correctly located on flange Cover Metal plate Filter Air cleaner body Hose Insulator See note Rocker cover Resonator PCV-filter Airduct Hose Hose clamp torque (air cleaner body side) 2.9 - 3.9 N·m N-CVT model Hose clamp torque (air cleaner body side) 0.30 - 0.40 kg·m N-CVT model Hose clamp torque (air cleaner body side) 2.2 - 2.9 ft·lb N-CVT model Airduct/resonator/PCV-filter fastener torque 3.7 - 5.0 N·m N-CVT model Airduct/resonator/PCV-filter fastener torque 0.38 - 0.51 kg·m N-CVT model Airduct/resonator/PCV-filter fastener torque 2.7 - 3.7 ft·lb N-CVT model Torque symbol: N·m (kg·m, ft·lb) Page is divided into two sections: upper section covers models with manual transmission, lower section covers models with automatic transmission (N·CVT). Torque specifications are identical for both variants. Exploded view of Air Cleaner assembly for models with manual transmission, showing cover, metal plate, filter, air cleaner body, hose, insulator, rocker cover, resonator, PCV-filter, airduct, and associated torque values. 382.title: PRECAUTIONS
382.body:
PRECAUTIONS EM-5002 Use an angle wrench for the final tightening of the following engine parts:
(1) Cylinder head bolts
(2) Main bearing cap bolts Do not use a torque value for final tightening. The torque value for these parts are for a preliminary step. Ensure thread and seat surfaces are clean. Liquid Gasket Application Procedure Use a scraper to remove all traces of old liquid gasket from mating surfaces and grooves. Also, completely clean any oil from these areas. Apply a continuous bead of liquid gasket to mating surfaces. (Use Genuine Liquid Gasket or equivalent.) For oil pan, be sure liquid gasket diameter is 3.5 to 4.5 mm (0.138 to 0.177 in). For areas except oil pan, be sure liquid gasket diameter is 2.0 to 3.0 mm (0.079 to 0.118 in). Apply liquid gasket around the inner side of bolt holes (unless otherwise specified). Assembly should be done within 5 minutes after coating. Wait at least 30 minutes before refilling engine oil and engine coolant. Liquid gasket diameter — oil pan 3.5 to 4.5 mm (0.138 to 0.177 in) Oil pan application Liquid gasket diameter — areas except oil pan 2.0 to 3.0 mm (0.079 to 0.118 in) Areas except oil pan Assembly time after coating within 5 minutes Maximum time before assembly Wait before refilling engine oil and coolant at least 30 minutes After assembly Diagram showing groove, bolt hole, and inner side positions for liquid gasket application on mating surfaces Cross-section diagram showing liquid gasket bead application in groove on inner side of mating surface
