388.title: Compression Pressure — Measurement of Compression Pressure
388.body: Compression Pressure — Measurement of Compression Pressure EM-5008 Measurement of Compression Pressure Warm up engine and switch off the ignition switch. Remove glow cable and glow plugs. Fit compression gauge adapter SST KV109B0040 to cylinder head. Compression gauge adapter torque: 15 - 20 N·m (1.5 - 2.0 kg-m, 11 - 14 ft-lb) Ensure the ignition switch is in the "LOCK" position and disconnect harness connector (black coloured) from injection pump. Crank engine, then read gauge indication. Always use a fully-charged battery to obtain specified engine revolution. Engine compression measurement should be made as quickly as possible. If cylinder compression in one or more cylinders is low, pour a small amount of engine oil into cylinders through the glow holes and retest compression. If adding oil helps compression, piston rings may be worn or damaged. If pressure stays low, valve may be sticking or seating incorrectly. If cylinder compression in any two adjacent cylinders is low, and if adding oil does not help the compression, there is leakage past the gasket surface. Leakage past the cylinder gasket can result in oil and water becoming mixed in the engine block cooling and lubrication chambers. Compression gauge adapter torque 15 - 20 N·m Step 3 Compression gauge adapter torque 1.5 - 2.0 kg-m Step 3 Compression gauge adapter torque 11 - 14 ft-lb Step 3 Compression Pressure (Unit: kPa (bar, kg/cm², psi)/200 rpm) Condition kPa (bar, kg/cm², psi)/200 rpm Standard 2,942 (29.4, 30, 427) Minimum 2,452 (24.5, 25, 356) Differential limit between cylinders 490 (4.9, 5, 71) VJZ engine variant. Diesel engine (glow plugs referenced). Top view of cylinder head showing glow plug locations with arrows indicating removal points Compression gauge adapter SST KV109B0040 fitted to cylinder head Engine bay showing harness connector (black coloured) on injection pump being disconnected

389.title: OIL PAN
389.body: OIL PAN EM-5009 Removal Remove engine under covers. Set a suitable transmission jack under the transaxle. Remove center member. Remove rear cover. Drain engine oil. Remove oil pan bolts. Remove oil pan using tool as illustrated. Insert Tool between oil pan and cylinder block. Do not drive seal cutter into front or rear oil seal retainer portion, or aluminum mating surfaces will be damaged. Do not insert screwdriver, or oil pan flange will be deformed. Slide Tool by tapping it with a hammer, and remove oil pan. Installation Before installing oil pan, remove all traces of liquid gasket from mating surface using a scraper. Also remove traces of liquid gasket from mating surface of cylinder block. Liquid gasket bead width 3.5 to 4.5 mm (0.138 to 0.177 in) Oil pan installation Be sure liquid gasket is 3.5 to 4.5 mm (0.138 to 0.177 in) wide. Use Genuine Liquid Gasket or equivalent. VJZ engine variant indicated by section code top right Underside of vehicle showing engine under cover removal points with arrows indicating fastener locations Illustration showing tool being inserted between oil pan and cylinder block Illustration showing tool being slid by tapping with a hammer to remove oil pan Oil pan mating surface being cleaned of liquid gasket traces with a scraper Liquid gasket tube with cut point indicated at nozzle tip

390.title: OIL PAN — Installation (Cont'd)
390.body: OIL PAN — Installation (Cont'd) EM-5010 Installation (Cont'd) Apply liquid gasket to inner sealing surface as shown at left. Apply a continuous bead of liquid gasket to mating surface of oil pan. Attaching should be done within 5 minutes after coating. Install oil pan and tighten bolts gradualy in two or three stages. Wait at least 30 minutes before refilling engine oil. Oil pan bolts torque 10 N·m Oil pan bolts torque 1.02 kg-m Oil pan bolts torque 88.5 in-lb Liquid gasket application: 7 mm (0.28 in) from bolt hole, applied to inner side sealing surface. Groove and bolt hole locations shown in diagram. Cross-section diagram showing liquid gasket application location on inner sealing surface, 7 mm (0.28 in) from bolt hole, indicating groove and inner side Illustration of applying a continuous bead of liquid gasket to the mating surface of the oil pan using a sealant applicator gun

391.title: TIMING BELT
391.body:
TIMING BELT EM-5011 CAUTION:
a. Do not bend or twist timing belt.
b. After removing timing belt, do not turn crankshaft and camshaft separately because valves will strike piston heads.
c. Ensure that timing belt, camshaft sprocket, crankshaft sprocket, injection pump sprocket and belt tensioner are clean and free from oil and water. Camshaft pulley Camshaft hub Injection pump pulley Tensioner pulley Idler pulley Water pump Timing belt Upper timing belt cover Lower timing belt cover Crankshaft pulley Crankshaft sprocket Crankshaft spacer Camshaft pulley bolt 80 (8.2, 59.0) N·m (kg·m, ft·lb) With locking sealant Camshaft hub bolt 25 (2.6, 18.4) N·m (kg·m, in·lb) Injection pump pulley bolt 25 (2.6, 18.4) N·m (kg·m, in·lb) Tensioner pulley bolt 20 (2.0, 14.8) N·m (kg·m, in·lb) Idler pulley bolt 25 (2.6, 18.4) N·m (kg·m, in·lb) Upper timing belt cover bolt 7 (0.71, 62.0) N·m (kg·m, in·lb) Lower timing belt cover bolt 7 (0.71, 62.0) N·m (kg·m, in·lb) Crankshaft pulley bolt 110 (11.2, 81.1) N·m (kg·m, ft·lb) Crankshaft pulley small bolt 15 (1.5, 11.1) N·m (kg·m, ft·lb) Water pump bolt 20 (2.0, 14.8) N·m (kg·m, in·lb) Timing belt cover bolt (upper region) 7 (0.71, 62.0) N·m (kg·m, in·lb) VJZ engine variant. Tensioner pulley marked Do not re-use. Camshaft pulley bolt uses locking sealant. Exploded view of timing belt assembly showing all components with torque specifications

392.title: TIMING BELT
392.body: TIMING BELT EM-5012 Removal Remove engine under cover. Remove front RH wheel and splash guard. Remove drive belt. Remove crankshaft pulley bolts and pulley. Support engine with a suitable jack. Remove engine front mounting bracket. Temporarily remove coolant reservoir. Remove RH insulator bracket. Remove timing belt covers. Turn crankshaft to TDC and lock crankshaft using SST KV109B0112. Lock camshaft and injection pump using SST KV109B0111 and KV109B0110. Loosen tensioner pulley nut and remove timing belt. VJZ engine variant. Page references RH wheel removal suggesting LHD or RHD variant with engine on RH side. View of crankshaft pulley area showing bolts to remove for timing belt access Engine front mounting bracket and RH insulator bracket removal area Timing belt covers with arrows indicating cover removal points Crankshaft at TDC position with SST locking tool in place Camshaft and injection pump locked with SST tools, timing belt being removed from tensioner pulley

393.title: TIMING BELT — Inspection
393.body: TIMING BELT — Inspection EM-5013 Visually check the condition of timing belt. Replace if any abnormality is found. Timing Belt Inspection Table Item to check Problem Cause Tooth is broken/tooth root is cracked. See figure SEM394A — broken teeth and cracked tooth root illustrations Camshaft jamming; Damaged camshaft/crankshaft oil seal Back surface is cracked/worn. See figure SEM395A — cracked/worn back surface illustration Tensioner jamming; Overheated engine; Interference with belt cover Side surface is worn. See figure SEM396A — worn side surface illustration. Belt corners are worn and round. Wicks are frayed and coming out. Improper installation of belt; Malfunctioning crankshaft pulley plate/timing belt plate Teeth are worn. See figure SEM397A — worn teeth illustration with rotating direction indicated. Canvas on tooth face is worn down. Canvas on tooth is fluffy, rubber layer is worn down and faded white, or welt is worn down and invisible. Poor belt cover sealing; Coolant leakage at water pump; Camshaft not functioning properly; Excessive belt tension Oil, coolant or water is stuck to belt. — Poor oil sealing of each oil seal; Coolant leakage at water pump; Poor belt cover sealing Illustration of broken tooth / cracked tooth root on timing belt Illustration of cracked/worn back surface of timing belt Illustration of worn side surface of timing belt, showing worn/round corners and frayed wicks Illustration of worn teeth on timing belt, showing canvas wear and rubber layer wear, with rotating direction arrows

394.title: TIMING BELT
394.body: TIMING BELT EM-5014 Belt tensioner and idler Check belt tensioner and idler for smooth turning. Confirm that the flywheel, camshaft and injection pump locking pins are still fitted. Installation Loosen the camshaft and injection pump sprocket bolts slightly. Check that the sprockets rotate freely on their hubs. Turn the sprockets in the direction of engine rotation (clockwise) to the end of their slots. Install timing belt in the following order beginning at the crankshaft over the idler pulley to the injection pump sprocket. Point arrow on timing belt towards engine rotation (clockwise). Turn the injection pump sprocket slightly anti-clockwise to engage the timing belt teeth. Follow the same procedure for the camshaft sprocket. Angular travel of the sprocket, with respect to the timing belt, must not be greater than one belt tooth. Engage the belt on the tensioner pulley and water pump. Confirm that the flywheel, camshaft and injection pump locking pins are still fitted. Pretensioning the timing belt Install SST KV109B0030 on the edge of the belt. Take care not to obstruct any other component. Tighten the screw on the tool until a loud click is heard. Rotate the tensioner counter-clockwise to obtain a reading of: 98 SEEM units for a new belt; 75 SEEM units for a used belt. Tighten the tensioners nut to the specified torque. Remove SST KV109B0030. Belt tension - new belt 98 SEEM units New belt Belt tension - used belt 75 SEEM units Used belt VJZ engine variant. Procedure covers diesel engine with injection pump sprocket. Hand checking belt tensioner and idler for smooth turning Sprockets with arrows showing direction of rotation and locking pins fitted Timing belt installation order showing crankshaft, idler pulley, injection pump sprocket and camshaft sprocket with direction arrow Timing belt pretensioning showing SST KV109B0030 and KV109B0080 in use on tensioner

395.title: TIMING BELT - Installation (Cont'd)
395.body: TIMING BELT - Installation (Cont'd) EM-5015 Installation (Cont'd) Remove one bolt from each sprocket to make certain that they are not at the end of their slots. Re-install the sprocket bolts and tighten the bolts to the specified torque while holding the sprockets from rotating with SST KV109B0070. Remove all locking pins and rotate crankshaft two revolutions clockwise. Tensioning the timing belt Re-install flywheel locking pin SST KV109B0112. Re-install camshaft and injection pump locking pins SST KV109B0111 and KV109B0110. Loosen the camshaft and injection pump sprocket bolts slightly and loosen tensioner nut. Install SST KV109B0030 on the edge of the belt. Take care not to obstruct any other component. Rotate tensioner counter-clockwise to obtain a reading of: 54 SEEM units for a new belt; 44 SEEM units for a used belt. Tighten tensioner nut to the specified torque. Remove SST KV109B0030. Timing belt tension (new belt) 54 SEEM units New belt Timing belt tension (used belt) 44 SEEM units Used belt VJZ engine variant indicated by section code. Procedure references injection pump, indicating diesel engine application. Timing belt sprocket area showing sprocket bolt slots and removal arrows Flywheel area showing flywheel locking pin SST KV109B0112 installation point Camshaft and injection pump sprocket area showing locking pin installation arrows Timing belt area showing loosening of camshaft and injection pump sprocket bolts and tensioner nut Timing belt area showing SST KV109B0030 installed on belt edge and KV109B0080 tensioner tool

396.title: TIMING BELT — Installation (Cont'd)
396.body: TIMING BELT — Installation (Cont'd) EM-5016 Installation (Cont'd) Tighten sprocket bolts to the specified torque while holding the sprockets from rotating with SST KV109B0070. Remove all locking pins. Turn crankshaft two revolutions and check timing and belt tension using SST KV109B0030 and the 3 locking pins. Belt tension should be between: 51 - 57 SEEM units for a new belt; 41 - 47 SEEM units for used belt. Install timing belt covers. Belt tension — new belt 51 - 57 SEEM units New belt Belt tension — used belt 41 - 47 SEEM units Used belt Engine view showing sprocket bolts being tightened with arrows indicating bolt locations Engine view showing timing belt covers being installed with arrows indicating cover locations

397.title: OIL SEAL REPLACEMENT
397.body: OIL SEAL REPLACEMENT EM-5017 Valve Oil Seal Removal Remove timing belt and rocker cover. Remove camshaft sprocket and camshaft hub. Remove camshaft brackets by loosening bracket nuts from center to outside in two or three stages. Remove camshaft oil seal and camshaft. Remove valve lifters and mark order No. on each lifter. Replace valve oil seal according to the following procedure. When replacing valve oil seal, set the corresponding piston at TDC. Failure to do so causes the valve to drop into the cylinder. Valve Oil Seal Replacement Procedure Set No. 1 cylinder at TDC. Remove valve springs and valve oil seals for No. 1 and No. 4 cylinders. Valve spring seats should not be removed. Install new valve oil seals for No. 1 and No. 4 cylinders using a suitable tool. Re-install valve springs. Install valve spring retainers and remount valve assembly. Set No. 2 cylinder at TDC. Replace valve oil seals for No. 2 and No. 3 cylinders according to steps 2) and 3). Install valve lifters in original positions. When installing camshaft and crankshaft oil seals, be careful to install them correctly, as shown in the figure. Apply engine oil to oil seal lip, outer face, camshaft and bracket. Wipe off excess oil after installing oil seal. Oil seal cross-section showing correct installation orientation with engine inside and engine outside directions indicated Engine inside Engine outside Oil seal lip Dust seal lip Camshaft Oil Seal Removal Remove timing belt, sprocket and hub. Pull out oil seal with a suitable tool. Valve oil seal removal using tools KV10109210 and KV10109220 on cylinder head with valve springs visible Valve oil seal installation using tool KV10107902 on cylinder head Cross-section diagram showing oil seal installation direction with engine inside/outside orientation, oil seal lip and dust seal lip labeled Camshaft oil seal removal using a suitable tool being pulled out from camshaft bracket

398.title: OIL SEAL REPLACEMENT
398.body: OIL SEAL REPLACEMENT EM-5018 Camshaft Oil Seal (continued) Apply engine oil to new oil seal and install oil seal using a suitable tool. Crankshaft Front Oil Seal Remove timing belt, crankshaft sprocket and spacer. Remove oil seal with a suitable tool. Apply engine oil to new oil seal and install oil seal using a suitable tool. Crankshaft Rear Oil Seal Remove transaxle assembly. (Refer to "REMOVAL AND INSTALLATION" in MT section.) Remove flywheel. Remove rear oil seal with a suitable tool. Apply engine oil to new oil seal and install oil seal using a suitable tool. Section code VJZ indicated in top-right corner. Crankshaft rear oil seal procedure references MT (Manual Transaxle) section. Installing oil seal using a suitable tool (camshaft or similar seal area) Removing crankshaft front oil seal with a suitable tool after removing timing belt and sprocket Installing crankshaft front oil seal using a suitable tool Removing crankshaft rear oil seal with a suitable tool Installing crankshaft rear oil seal using a suitable tool

399.title: CYLINDER HEAD
399.body: CYLINDER HEAD EM-5019 Camshaft Lubricate with new engine oil Camshaft oil seal Do not re-use Cylinder head gasket Do not re-use Valve Lubricate with new engine oil Spring seat Valve spring Valve oil seal Lubricate with new engine oil; Do not re-use Spring retainer Valve guide Valve cotter Shim Valve lifter Cylinder head bolt Refer to "Installation" in "CYLINDER HEAD" Oil filler cap Rocker cover Rocker cover gasket Do not re-use Camshaft bracket Cylinder head Rocker cover bolt torque 10 N·m (1.0 kg·m, 88.5 in·lb) Camshaft bracket bolt torque 20 N·m (2.0 kg·m, 14.8 ft·lb) Lubricate with new engine oil. N·m (kg·m, in·lb) N·m (kg·m, ft·lb) Do not re-use Refer to "Installation" in "CYLINDER HEAD" Swirl chamber (Combustion chamber) — Valve seat VJZ engine variant. Section code VJZ shown top-right. Exploded view of cylinder head assembly including camshaft, valves, springs, rocker cover, head gasket, and related components with torque values indicated

400.title: CYLINDER HEAD
400.body: CYLINDER HEAD EM-5020 After removing timing belt, do not turn crankshaft and camshaft separately, or valves will strike piston heads. When installing camshaft, oil seals or valve lifters, lubricate contacting surfaces with new engine oil. Removal Drain coolant from radiator and cylinder block. Be careful not to spill coolant on drive belt. Remove timing belt. Refer to "TIMING BELT", (EM-5012). Disconnect front exhaust pipe from manifold. Remove injection tubes. Disconnect spill hose, glow plug cable, EGR hose and coolant temperature sensor connectors. Remove air duct and air cleaner. Disconnect vacuum hoses, coolant hoses, fuel hoses from thermostat housing and vacuum pump. Remove intake manifold support and dipstick tube bolt. Disconnect fast idle device wire from injection pump. Remove rocker cover bolts in numerical order as shown in the figure. Remove cylinder head bolts in numerical order as shown in the figure. Head warpage or cracking could result from removing in incorrect order. Cylinder head bolts should be loosened in two or three stages. Remove cylinder head complete with manifolds. VJZ engine variant. Procedure applies to diesel engine with injection pump and glow plugs. Photo showing cylinder head area with arrow indicating a component on the engine Rocker cover bolt removal numerical order diagram showing bolt positions numbered 1-8 Cylinder head bolt removal numerical order diagram showing bolt positions numbered 1-10

401.title: CYLINDER HEAD
401.body: CYLINDER HEAD EM-5021 Disassembly Remove EGR valve, intake and exhaust manifold, vacuum pump and thermostat housing. Remove camshaft sprocket and hub using SST KV109B0070 or a suitable tool. Remove camshaft brackets in order from outside to inside. Loosen camshaft bracket nuts in two or three stages. Remove camshaft and oil seal. Remove valve lifters. Remove valve component parts using special service tool or a suitable tool. Keep each valve and its components together and mark them so they can be reassembled in their original positions. Remove valve oil seals using special service tool or a suitable tool. Cylinder Head Distortion Visually check for cracks and deformation. Check cylinder head for distortion. Head surface flatness Less than 0.05 mm (0.002 in) Maximum allowable distortion Resurfacing limit 0.4 mm (0.016 in) Maximum material removal during resurfacing Nominal height of cylinder head 136.3 - 136.5 mm (5.366 - 5.374 in) Standard cylinder head height Warpage of surface Less than 0.1 mm (0.004 in) As shown on figure caption If beyond the specified limit, replace or resurface cylinder head. After resurfacing cylinder head, check that camshaft rotates freely by hand. If resistance is felt, the cylinder head must be replaced. Cylinder head assembly with camshaft sprocket and timing belt visible Removing valve component parts using special service tools KV10109210 and KV10109220 Removing valve oil seals using special service tool KV10107902 Cylinder head surface flatness check pattern (cross-hatch measurement points) and hand-checking cylinder head height

402.title: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd)
402.body: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) EM-5022 VALVE GUIDE CLEARANCE Measure valve stem diameter and valve guide inner diameter as illustrated. Standard clearance (valve guide) 0.025 - 0.052 mm (0.0010 - 0.0020 in) Valve stem to valve guide inner diameter VALVE GUIDE REPLACEMENT Heat cylinder head in oil to 150 to 160°C (302 to 320°F). Drive out valve guide using a press or hammer and a suitable tool. Ream cylinder head valve guide bore. Heat cylinder head to 150 to 160°C (302 to 320°F) and press service valve guide onto cylinder head. Reaming bore (service part) 13.495 - 13.527 mm (0.5313 - 0.5326 in) Valve guide bore after reaming Valve guide protrusion — INTAKE 39 mm From cylinder head surface Valve guide protrusion — EXHAUST 35 mm From cylinder head surface VALVE SEATS Check valve and valve seat for contact. Coat the valve face with prussian red lead. If contact is wrong, correct valve seat. If the valve red lead appears 360° around face, the valve stem and face are concentric. If not, repair or replace valve. Check valve seats for pitting at contact surface. Resurface or replace if excessively worn. Correct valve seat surface. When repairing valve seat, check valve and valve guide for wear beforehand. If worn, replace them. Then correct valve seat. Micrometer measuring valve stem diameter and valve guide inner diameter, showing measurement positions: Top, Center, Bottom along X and Y axes Driving out valve guide using press or hammer and suitable tool Reaming cylinder head valve guide bore Cross-section of valve guide installed in cylinder head showing INTAKE 39mm and EXHAUST 35mm protrusion dimensions Valve seat contact pattern check using prussian red lead, showing OK (full 360° contact ring) and NG (partial/offset contact) examples

403.title: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) — VALVE SEAT REPLACEMENT
403.body: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) — VALVE SEAT REPLACEMENT EM-5023 Reaming bore for service valve seat Recess Diameter Recess Depth Intake 37.922 - 37.947 mm (1.4930 - 1.4941 in) 8.017 - 8.167 mm (0.3156 - 0.3215 in) Exhaust 32.422 - 32.447 mm (1.2765 - 1.2774 in) 8.30 - 8.45 mm (0.327 - 0.333 in) VALVE SEAT REPLACEMENT Bore out old seat until it collapses. Set machine depth stop so that boring cannot contact bottom face of seat recess in cylinder head. Ream the cylinder head recess diameter and depth. Reaming bore for service valve seat — Unit: mm (in). Intake recess diameter: 37.922 - 37.947 (1.4930 - 1.4941), depth: 8.017 - 8.167 (0.3156 - 0.3215). Exhaust recess diameter: 32.422 - 32.447 (1.2765 - 1.2774), depth: 8.30 - 8.45 (0.327 - 0.333). Heat cylinder head to a temperature of 150 to 160°C (302 to 320°F) and press fit seat until it seats on the bottom. Use the valve guide center for reaming to ensure valve seat will have the correct fit. Install valve seats. When replacing valve seat, valve should be replaced as well. Cut or grind valve seat using a suitable tool at the specified dimensions as shown in SDS (EM-5047). The cutting should be done with both hands to obtain a uniform and concentric finish. Apply a small amount of fine grinding compound to the valve's contacting face and put the valve into its guide. Lap valve against its seat until proper valve seating is obtained. Check valve seating condition. W: Refer to SDS. Diagram showing recess diameter (top and bottom) in cylinder head for valve seat replacement Illustration showing cutting or grinding of valve seat using a suitable tool with both hands Illustration showing application of fine grinding compound and lapping valve against its seat Illustration showing valve seating condition check; W dimension refers to SDS

404.title: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd)
404.body: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) EM-5024 VALVE DIMENSIONS Check dimensions of each valve. For dimensions, refer to SDS (EM-5047). Correct or replace any valve that is faulty. Valve face or valve stem end surface should be refaced by using a valve grinder. Measure distance from cylinder head surface to intake and exhaust valves. If specified distance is exceeded, replace valve(s) or valve seat(s). Specified distance — Intake (Standard) 0.85 - 1.19 mm (0.033 - 0.047 in) Distance from cylinder head surface to intake valve Specified distance — Exhaust (Standard) 1.05 - 1.39 mm (0.041 - 0.055 in) Distance from cylinder head surface to exhaust valve Measure the tension of each spring. If the measured value exceeds the specified value, replace spring. Refer to SDS (EM-5047). VALVE LIFTER Check contact and sliding surfaces for wear or scratches. Valve dimension diagram showing margin thickness (T), valve face angle (alpha), stem diameter (d), valve head diameter (D), and overall length (L) Valve grinder being used to reface valve face or valve stem end surface Measuring distance from cylinder head surface to intake and exhaust valves using a dial gauge setup, with intake and exhaust labels shown Valve spring pressure load being measured using a spring tester machine Valve lifter showing contact and sliding surfaces to check for wear or scratches

405.title: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd)
405.body: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) EM-5025 Usually combustion chambers should not be removed. However, if they show cracks or extensive damage, they should be replaced. Combustion Chamber Replacement Remove glow plug connecting plate, glow plugs and injection nozzle. Heat cylinder head in oil to between 150 and 160°C (302 and 320°F). Remove combustion chamber so that cylinder head will not be damaged. Be careful not to scratch inside of nozzle hole. Install combustion chamber. Heat cylinder head 150 to 160°C (302 to 320°F) in oil. Align combustion chamber knock pin with cylinder head notch, and install it into cylinder head using a plastic-tip hammer. Combustion chamber protrusion 0 - 0.03 mm Combustion chamber protrusion 0.000 - 0.001 in Check camshaft for scratches, seizure and wear. Camshaft Journal Clearance Measure the inner diameter of camshaft bearings. Measure the outer diameter of camshaft journals. Standard inner diameter — No. 1 (flywheel side) 27.505 - 27.533 mm Standard inner diameter — No. 1 (flywheel side) 1.083 - 1.084 in Standard inner diameter — No. 2 28.005 - 28.033 mm Standard inner diameter — No. 2 1.103 - 1.104 in Standard inner diameter — No. 3 28.505 - 28.533 mm Standard inner diameter — No. 3 1.122 - 1.123 in Standard outer diameter — No. 1 (flywheel side) 27.459 - 27.48 mm Standard outer diameter — No. 1 (flywheel side) 1.0811 - 1.0819 in Standard outer diameter — No. 2 27.959 - 27.98 mm Standard outer diameter — No. 2 1.1007 - 1.1016 in Standard outer diameter — No. 3 28.459 - 28.48 mm Standard outer diameter — No. 3 1.1204 - 1.1213 in Standard clearance 0.025 - 0.074 mm Standard clearance 0.0010 - 0.0029 in VJZ engine variant. Diesel engine with combustion chambers, glow plugs and injection nozzles. Combustion chamber removal procedure showing glow plug and injection nozzle area with stars indicating removal points Combustion chamber installation showing knock pin and notch alignment Measuring inner diameter of camshaft bearings using micrometer/gauge tool Measuring outer diameter of camshaft journals using micrometer

406.title: CYLINDER HEAD — Assembly and Installation
406.body: CYLINDER HEAD — Assembly and Installation EM-5026 Assembly Install valve component parts. Always install new valve oil seals. Refer to "OIL SEAL REPLACEMENT", (EM-00). When installing valve, apply engine oil on the valve stem and lip of valve oil seal. Check whether the valve face is free from foreign matter. Install camshaft and brackets and tighten bracket nuts to the specified torque in two or three stages. Camshaft-bracket nuts: 20 N·m (2.0 kg-m, 14.8 ft-lb) Tighten bracket from center to outside. Apply sealant to brackets No. 1 and No. 3. Install new camshaft oil seal. Refer to "OIL SEAL REPLACEMENT", (EM-5017). Install camshaft hub and sprocket and tighten hub bolt to the specified torque using special service tool or a suitable tool. Install camshaft locking pin SST KV109B0111. Install intake and exhaust manifold, EGR valve, vacuum-pump and thermostat housing. Installation Install cylinder head gasket, with printing facing up. Confirm that the flywheel and injection pump locking pins are still fitted. When replacing or repairing cylinder block, piston, connecting rod and crankshaft, select piston referring to "DETERMINING THE PISTON TYPE", (EM-5041). Check cylinder head bolt length, and replace if limit is exceeded. Install cylinder head and tighten cylinder head bolts according to the following sequence. Apply MOLYKOTE G RAPID PLUS to threads and under-head seating face of each bolt. Tightening procedure Tighten bolts in the numerical order shown in the figure and following the steps below using angle wrenchs SST KV10112100. Tightening procedure: (a) Tighten all bolts to 40 N·m (4.1 kg-m, 29.5 ft-lb); (b) Turn all bolts 255° - 265° Install timing belt. Refer to "TIMING BELT", (EM-5011). Camshaft-bracket nuts torque 20 N·m Camshaft-bracket nuts torque 2.0 kg-m Camshaft-bracket nuts torque 14.8 ft-lb Cylinder head bolt maximum length — Hex head 197.5 mm Cylinder head bolt maximum length — TORX head 197.1 mm Cylinder head bolt tightening torque step (a) 40 N·m Cylinder head bolt tightening torque step (a) 4.1 kg-m Cylinder head bolt tightening torque step (a) 29.5 ft-lb Cylinder head bolt angle tightening step (b) 255° - 265° degrees Camshaft and brackets installed on cylinder head, showing bracket numbering sequence (3, 2, 1) from left to right Camshaft hub and sprocket installed on cylinder head with timing chain/belt Cylinder head bolt showing maximum length L measurement; Hex head max 197.5 mm, TORX head max 197.1 mm Cylinder head bolt tightening sequence diagram showing numbered bolt positions 1-10 with flywheel side indicated

407.title: CYLINDER HEAD — Installation (Cont'd)
407.body: CYLINDER HEAD — Installation (Cont'd) EM-5027 Installation (Cont'd) Check valve clearance. Refer to "VALVE CLEARANCE", (EM-5028). Install rocker cover. Tighten rocker cover bolts to the specified torque in 2 stages in numerical order as shown in the figure. Install front exhaust pipe to manifold. Install injection tubes. Install all futher parts in reverse order of removal. Rocker cover bolt tightening sequence diagram showing 8 numbered bolt positions on the rocker cover

408.title: VALVE CLEARANCE
408.body: VALVE CLEARANCE EM-5028 Check valve clearance while engine is cold and not running. Checking Remove rocker cover. Set No. 1 cylinder at TDC on its compression stroke. Install flywheel locking pin SST KV109B0112. Check that valve lifters on No. 1 cylinder are loose and valve lifters on No. 4 are tight. If not, turn crankshaft one revolution (360°) and align as above. Check only those valves shown in the figure. Using a feeler gauge, measure clearance between valve lifter and camshaft. Record any valve clearance measurements which are out of specification. They will be used later to determine the required replacement adjusting shim. Turn crankshaft one revolution (360°) and install flywheel locking pin SST KV109B0112. Check those valves shown in the figure. Use the same procedure as mentioned in step 3. If all valve clearances are within specification, install the rocker cover. Refer to "CYLINDER HEAD", (EM-5027). Valve clearance for checking (Cold): Intake 0.07 - 0.23 mm Cold Valve clearance for checking (Cold): Intake 0.003 - 0.009 in Cold Valve clearance for checking (Cold): Exhaust 0.22 - 0.38 mm Cold Valve clearance for checking (Cold): Exhaust 0.009 - 0.015 in Cold Adjust valve clearance while engine is cold. Adjusting Turn crankshaft, to position cam lobe on camshaft of valve that must be adjusted upward. Rotate notch toward injection nozzle (see figure) to simplify shim removal later. Engine bay view showing rocker cover area, referenced for removal step Top view of cylinder head showing valves to be checked at No.1 cylinder TDC position Cross-section diagram showing clearance measurement between valve lifter and camshaft Top view of cylinder head showing valves to be checked after crankshaft turned 360 degrees Close-up view showing notch rotation toward injection nozzle for shim removal during adjusting procedure

409.title: VALVE CLEARANCE — Adjusting (Cont'd)
409.body: VALVE CLEARANCE — Adjusting (Cont'd) EM-5029 Adjusting Valve Clearance (Cont'd) Place Tool KV109B0100 around camshaft as shown in figure. CAUTION: Be careful not to damage cam surface with Tool KV109B0100. Rotate lever so that valve lifter is pushed down. Remove adjusting shim using a small screwdriver and a magnetic finger. Determine replacement adjusting shim size using following formula. Using a micrometer determine thickness of removed shim. Calculate thickness of new adjusting shim so valve clearance comes within specified values. R = Thickness of removed shim; N = Thickness of new shim; M = Measured valve clearance. Intake: N = R + [M – 0.15 mm (0.006 in)]. Exhaust: N = R + [M – 0.30 mm (0.012 in)]. Shims are available in 59 sizes from 3.20 mm (0.126 in) to 4.90 mm (0.193 in). Select new shim with thickness as close as possible to calculated value. Install new shim using a suitable tool. Install with the surface on which the thickness is stamped facing down. Intake shim formula N = R + [M – 0.15 mm (0.006 in)] mm (in) Intake valve clearance adjustment Exhaust shim formula N = R + [M – 0.30 mm (0.012 in)] mm (in) Exhaust valve clearance adjustment Shim size range (minimum) 3.20 mm (0.126 in) 59 sizes available Shim size range (maximum) 4.90 mm (0.193 in) 59 sizes available Tool KV109B0100 placed around camshaft on cylinder head Lever being rotated to push valve lifter down Removing adjusting shim using screwdriver and magnetic finger Measuring removed shim thickness with a micrometer Installing new shim using a suitable tool

410.title: VALVE CLEARANCE — Adjusting (Cont'd)
410.body: VALVE CLEARANCE — Adjusting (Cont'd) EM-5030 Adjusting (Cont'd) Remove Tool. Recheck valve clearance. Install rocker cover. Refer to "CYLINDER HEAD" (EM-5027). Illustration showing tool KV109B0100 being used on the valve adjustment mechanism on the engine

411.title: ENGINE REMOVAL
411.body: ENGINE REMOVAL EM-5031 Rear insulate Label pointing to rear engine mount insulator on crossmember Front left engine mount upper bracket bolt 50 - 60 N·m (5.1 - 6.1 kg-m, 36.9 - 44.3 ft-lb) Engine mount upper bracket fastener Front left engine mount insulator through bolt 50 - 60 N·m (5.1 - 6.1 kg-m, 36.9 - 44.3 ft-lb) Insulator to bracket fastener Front right engine mount upper bolt 60 - 70 N·m (6.1 - 7.1 kg-m, 44.3 - 51.6 ft-lb) Right mount upper fastener Front right engine mount bracket bolt (upper) 29 - 39 N·m (3.0 - 4.0 kg-m, 21.4 - 28.8 ft-lb) Right mount bracket upper fastener Front right engine mount insulator bolt 21 - 27 N·m (2.1 - 2.8 kg-m, 15.5 - 19.9 ft-lb) Right mount insulator fastener Rear crossmember to body bolt (right side) 62 - 78 N·m (6.3 - 8.0 kg-m, 45.7 - 57.5 ft-lb) Rear crossmember right fastener Rear crossmember center bolt 64 - 73 N·m (6.5 - 7.4 kg-m, 47.2 - 53.8 ft-lb) Rear crossmember center fastener Rear crossmember to body bolt (lower center) 74 - 83 N·m (7.5 - 8.5 kg-m, 54.6 - 61.2 ft-lb) Rear crossmember lower center fastener Rear crossmember to body bolt (left side) 64 - 73 N·m (6.5 - 7.4 kg-m, 47.2 - 53.8 ft-lb) Rear crossmember left fastener : N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) VJZ variant. Rear insulate crossmember assembly shown. Front direction indicated on diagram. Exploded diagram showing engine mounting components including front left mount, front right mount with insulator, and rear insulate crossmember/stay assembly with torque specifications for all fasteners.

412.title: ENGINE REMOVAL
412.body:
ENGINE REMOVAL EM-5032 WARNING:
• Situate vehicle on a flat and solid surface.
• Place chocks at front and back of rear wheels.
• Do not remove engine until exhaust system has completely cooled down.
• For safety during subsequent steps, the tension of wires should be slackened against the engine.
• Before removing front axle from transaxle, place safety stands under designated front supporting points. Refer to GI section for lifting points and towing.
• Be sure to hoist engine and transaxle in a safe manner.
• For engines not equipped with engine slingers, attach proper slingers and bolts described in PARTS CATALOG or Eurofast. CAUTION:
• When lifting engine, be careful not to strike adjacent parts, especially accelerator wire casing, brake lines, and brake master cylinder.
• Always use engine slingers when hoisting the engine.
• When removing drive shaft, be careful not to damage transaxle oil seal. Removal Remove engine undercovers and splash guards. Remove front exhaust tube. Drain coolant from both radiator and engine. Drain transaxle oil. Remove hood. Remove fresh air duct. Disconnect all coolant hoses from engine. Disconnect fuel tubes and vacuum tubes. Release drive belt adjusting bolts and remove power steering pump from engine. Bind pump properly to the vehicle. Remove the battery and the battery tray. Disconnect or remove electrical wiring where necessary. Disconnect clutch lever cable and accelerator cable. Disconnect speedometer and neutral switch connector from transaxle housing. Remove front wheels. Remove brake caliper mounting bolts and bind caliper properly to vehicle LH & RH. Disconnect tie rod ball joints and lower ball joints (LH & RH). Remove stabilizer. Remove drive shafts from transaxle. Mount suitable slingers to cylinder head. Use a suitable hoist to release the force on the engine mountings. LH & RH distinctions noted for brake calipers and ball joints. Procedure applies to engine/transaxle assembly removal. Power steering pump removal step implies power steering equipped variant. Photograph showing engine bay with engine removal setup, slingers and mounting points visible

413.title: ENGINE REMOVAL
413.body: ENGINE REMOVAL EM-5033 Removal (Cont'd) Remove upper mounting (Right side). Release transaxle shift linkages. Release transaxle mounting (Left side). Remove center member. Remove engine with transaxle as shown. Installation Install top engine mounting brackets and fixing bolts. Install RH insulator to vehicle body and tighten to the specified torque using SST KV109B0090. Right side upper mounting and left side transaxle mounting referenced; engine removed with transaxle as an assembly. Upper mounting (Right side) removal — arrows indicating mounting points Transaxle shift linkages and transaxle mounting (Left side) — arrows indicating release points Center member removal — arrows indicating fastener locations Engine with transaxle being removed from vehicle Top engine mounting bracket and RH insulator — installation illustration showing SST usage

414.title: ENGINE REMOVAL - Installation (Cont'd)
414.body: ENGINE REMOVAL - Installation (Cont'd) EM-5034 Installation (Cont'd) Install centermember. Tighten RH bracket and Rear bracket to the specified torque. Tighten finally the transaxle insulator bolts. Other installation procedures are in the reverse order of removal. Underside view showing centermember installation with arrows indicating RH bracket and Rear bracket bolt locations Close-up view showing transaxle insulator bolts with arrows indicating bolt locations

415.title: CYLINDER BLOCK
415.body: CYLINDER BLOCK EM-5035 Piston ring Do not re-use; Lubricate with new engine oil Piston Lubricate with new engine oil Piston pin Lubricate with new engine oil Connecting rod bearing Lubricate with new engine oil Connecting rod Main bearing cap Front oil seal Front oil seal retainer Apply liquid gasket Flywheel Do not re-use Rear oil seal Rear oil seal retainer Apply liquid gasket Cylinder block Main bearing Lubricate with new engine oil Crankshaft Lubricate with new engine oil Thrust washer Lubricate with new engine oil Oil pan Apply liquid gasket Drain plug Oil pump Apply liquid gasket Oil pump chain Oil jet Connecting rod bearing cap bolt torque 40 (4.1, 29.5) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Main bearing cap bolt torque 30 (3.1, 22.1) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Flywheel bolt torque 65 (6.6, 47.9) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Rear oil seal retainer bolt torque 10 (1.0, 88.5) N·m (kg-m, in-lb) Front oil seal retainer bolt torque 20 (2.0, 14.8) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Oil pan bolt torque 20 + 50° (2.0 + 50°, 14.8 + 50°) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) angle tightening Drain plug torque 30 (3.1, 22.1) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Oil pump bolt torque 10 (1.0, 88.5) N·m (kg-m, in-lb) Rear oil seal retainer (top) bolt torque 30 (3.1, 22.1) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Cylinder block front bolt torque 10 (1.0, 88.5) N·m (kg-m, in-lb) Legend: Circle-N symbol = N·m (kg-m, in-lb); Circle-N-ft symbol = N·m (kg-m, ft-lb); X symbol = Do not re-use; Solid square = Apply locking sealant; Diagonal square = Apply liquid gasket; Triangle = Lubricate with new engine oil. VJZ engine variant. Page reference code NEM315. Exploded view of cylinder block assembly showing all major components including pistons, connecting rods, crankshaft, bearings, oil pan, oil pump, flywheel, and associated seals and retainers with torque specifications.

416.title: CYLINDER BLOCK
416.body: CYLINDER BLOCK EM-5036 Preparation Drill two holes into the attachment (KV10115300). PISTON AND CRANKSHAFT Place engine on a work stand. Drain coolant and oil. Remove timing belt. Refer to "TIMING BELT", (EM-5012). Remove water pump. Remove oil pan and oil pump. Remove cylinder head. Refer to "CYLINDER HEAD", (EM-5020). Remove pistons with connecting rods. Remove oil seal retainers front and rear. Remove piston rings with a tool. Remove main bearing caps, crankshaft, and main bearings. Before removing main bearing caps, measure crankshaft end play. (Refer to cylinder block assembly section.) Bolts should be loosened in two or three stages and in the order shown. Place the bearings and the caps in their correct order. Journal No. 2 and No. 4 bearings have an oil groove. Bearings caps are numbered from 1 to 5, starting with No. 1 at flywheel side. Attachment (KV10115300) with drilling dimensions: 11mm spacing, Ø11mm holes, 25.5mm, 22.5mm, Ø11mm, 25.5mm Engine on work stand with tools KV10115300 and ST0501S000 attached Piston with piston rings being removed using a tool Cylinder block showing main bearing cap bolt loosening order, numbered 1-10 with positions 5,9,7,3,1,4,8,10,6,2

417.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection
417.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection EM-5037 PISTON AND PISTON PIN CLEARANCE Measure inner diameter of piston pin hole "dp". Measure outer diameter of piston pin. Calculate piston pin clearance. Piston pin hole inner diameter - Piston pin outer diameter. If it exceeds the above value, replace piston assembly with pin. Piston pin hole inner diameter "dp" — Standard diameter 23.003 - 23.008 mm (0.9056 - 0.9058 in) Piston pin outer diameter — Standard diameter 22.996 - 23.000 mm (0.9054 - 0.9055 in) Piston pin clearance 0.003 - 0.012 mm (0.0001 - 0.0005 in) Piston pin hole inner diameter minus piston pin outer diameter End gap — Top ring 0.20 - 0.40 mm (0.008 - 0.016 in) End gap — 2nd ring 0.15 - 0.35 mm (0.006 - 0.014 in) End gap — Oil ring 0.25 - 0.50 mm (0.010 - 0.020 in) If out of specification, replace piston ring. CYLINDER BLOCK DISTORTION AND WEAR Clean upper face of cylinder block. Measure the distortion of the upper face of cylinder block. If out of specification, replace cylinder block. Cylinder block upper face distortion — Limit 0.10 mm (0.0039 in) Nominal cylinder block height from oil pan gasket face 265.23 mm (10.442 in) PISTON-TO-BORE CLEARANCE Using a bore gauge, measure cylinder bore. Check for scratches and seizure. If seizure is found, replace cylinder block. Cylinder bore standard inner diameter 77.011 - 77.038 mm (3.0319 - 3.0330 in) If both cylinder block and piston are replaced with new ones, select piston height type. Refer to "DETERMINING THE PISTON TYPE", (EM-5041). Measuring inner diameter of piston pin hole using a bore gauge/dial indicator tool Measuring outer diameter of piston pin using a micrometer Measuring piston ring end gap inside cylinder bore using feeler gauge, showing piston press-fit, ring positions Measuring distortion of upper face of cylinder block using a straight edge and feeler gauge, with numbered measurement positions (1-6) Measuring cylinder bore inner diameter using a bore gauge

418.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd)
418.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) EM-5038 Piston Inspection Measure piston skirt diameter. Check piston-to-bore clearance. If out of specification, replace piston and/or cylinder block. Piston diameter "A" 76.939 mm (3.0291 in) mm (in) Piston-to-bore clearance "B" 0.07 - 0.10 mm (0.003 - 0.004 in) mm (in) Crankshaft Inspection Check crankshaft main and pin journals for score, wear or cracks. With a micrometer, measure journals for taper and out-of-round. Measure crankshaft runout. Out-of-round (X – Y) Less than 0.005 mm (0.0002 in) mm (in) Taper (A – B) Less than 0.005 mm (0.0002 in) mm (in) Runout (Total indicator reading) Less than 0.05 mm (0.0020 in) mm (in) Main Bearing Clearance Set main bearings in their proper positions on cylinder block and main bearing cap. Main bearing layout showing positions of thrust bearing (upper only), upper bearing with oil groove, lower bearing with oil groove, and No.1 bearing (flywheel side) with front direction indicated. Thrust bearing (upper only) Upper bearing Oil groove No.1 bearing (flywheel side) Oil groove Lower bearing Front Micrometer measuring piston skirt diameter at dimension 'A' Diagram showing crankshaft journal taper (A-B) and out-of-round (X-Y) measurement points Crankshaft runout measurement with dial indicator Main bearing layout diagram showing thrust bearing (upper only), upper bearing, lower bearing, oil groove positions, and No.1 bearing (flywheel side) with front direction indicated

419.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd)
419.body:
CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) EM-5039 Main Bearing Clearance Inspection Install main bearing cap to cylinder block. Tighten all bolts in correct order to the specified torque. Measure inner diameter of each main bearing. Measure outer diameter of each main journal in crankshaft. Calculate main bearing clearance. Main bearing clearance = Inner dia. - Outer dia. If it exceeds the standard clearance, replace bearing. If clearance cannot be adjusted within the standard of any bearing, grind crankshaft journal and use undersized bearing. If crankshaft, cylinder block or main bearing is reused, measure main bearing clearance. If crankshaft, cylinder block and main bearings are replaced with new ones, it is necessary to select thickness of main bearings as follows: a. The code of each cylinder block halfshell is always B (black). b. The code of each main bearing cap halfshell is punched on the crankshaft and cylinder block. c. Select main bearing with suitable thickness according to the following tables. Main bearing clearance (Standard) 0.01 - 0.036 mm Inner dia. minus Outer dia. Main bearing clearance (Standard) 0.0004 - 0.0014 in Inner dia. minus Outer dia. When grinding crank pin and crank journal:
a. Grind until clearance is within specified standard bearing clearance.
b. Refer to S.D.S. for standard bearing clearance and available spare parts. Dial bore gauge being used to measure inner diameter of main bearing installed in cylinder block with bearing cap fitted Micrometer being used to measure outer diameter of crankshaft main journal Cylinder block showing main bearing cap code identification stamp location (No.5 cap, codes A E K O U) and Main journal No.1 flywheel side orientation

420.title: CYLINDER BLOCK - Inspection (Cont'd)
420.body: CYLINDER BLOCK - Inspection (Cont'd) EM-5040 Halfshell Type Standard Halfshell (reference) Halfshells for cylinder block side - Smooth (black) Grooved (black) Halfshells for bearing cap side - Smooth (blue) Grooved (blue) Halfshells for bearing cap side - Smooth (black) Groove (black) Halfshells for bearing cap side - Smooth (green) Grooved (green) TYPE B A B C THICKNESS 1.858 (0.0731) 1.844 (0.0726) 1.858 (0.0731) 1.869 (0.0736) Unit: mm (in) For example: The first letter on the crankshaft is S and the first letter on the cylinder block is E then according to the tables the main bearing cap halfshell for bearing No. 1 will be type A (blue). The cylinder block halfshell will always be B (black). Connecting rod bearing (Big end) Install connecting rod bearing to connecting rod and cap. Install connecting rod cap to connecting rod. Tighten bolts to the specified torque. Measure inner diameter of each bearing. Matrix/lookup table chart showing crankshaft letter codes vs cylinder block letter codes for main bearing selection, with letters A, B, C filling the grid cells Photo of a halfshell bearing (grooved black type) showing the bearing shell shape with oil hole Photo showing measurement of connecting rod bearing inner diameter using a bore gauge/measuring tool on the connecting rod big end

421.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) / Connecting Rod Clearance / Determining the Piston Type
421.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) / Connecting Rod Clearance / Determining the Piston Type EM-5041 Connecting Rod Bearing Clearance Measure outer diameter of each crankshaft pin journal. Calculate connecting rod bearing clearance. Connecting rod bearing clearance = Inner dia. – Outer dia. Standard: 0.010 - 0.044 mm (0.0004 - 0.0017 in) If it exceeds the standard clearance, replace bearing. If clearance cannot be adjusted with the standard bearing, grind crankshaft journal and use undersized bearing. Refer to step 7 of "BEARING CLEARANCE – Main bearing". Connecting rod bearing thickness Type Thickness — Unit: mm (in) Standard 1.814 - 1.820 (0.071 - 0.072) Undersize 1.964 - 1.970 (0.077 - 0.078) Connecting Rod Clearance (Small end) Measure inner diameter of connecting rod small end. Standard: 23.007 - 23.02 mm (0.9058 - 0.9063 in) Measure outer diameter of piston pin. Standard: 22.996 - 23.000 mm (0.9054 - 0.9055 in) Calculate connecting rod bushing clearance. Connecting rod bushing clearance = Inner diameter – pin outer diameter. Standard: 0.011 - 0.02 mm (0.0004 - 0.0008 in) If it exceeds the standard clearance, replace connecting rod assembly and/or piston and pin. When replacing pistons, crankshaft, cylinder block or connecting rods always determine piston type. Determining the Piston Type Install crankshaft with correct bearings and tighten main bearings caps to the specified torque. Mount the No. 4 cylinder connecting rod on the calibration piston SST KV109B0050. Install a new pair of connecting rod bearings. Install the calibration piston with connecting rod in the corresponding cylinder. Measuring outer diameter of crankshaft pin journal with a micrometer Measuring inner diameter of connecting rod small end with a dial bore gauge Measuring outer diameter of piston pin with a micrometer Calibration piston KV109B0050 installed on No. 4 cylinder connecting rod in the cylinder block

422.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd)
422.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) EM-5042 Piston Height Selection Procedure (Cont'd) Install the connecting rod bearing cap and tighten to the specified torque. Install the dial indicator on the dial support. Place the indicator/support above the center of calibration piston. Turn the crankshaft to TDC, and zero out the dial indicator. Move the indicator/support onto the deck. Take a precise reading of the difference in level between calibration piston and the deck. Follow the same procedure for the other cylinders, using the corresponding connecting rods. Select piston height types according to the following table. Piston Height Selection Table Reading from Dial Indicator Piston Reference Final Protrusion Dimension Below 2.26 (0.089) Check dimensions of all elements 1.09 ± 0.05 (0.043 ± 0.002) 2.50 - 2.40 (0.098 - 0.094) A 1.09 ± 0.05 (0.043 ± 0.002) 2.43 - 2.33 (0.096 - 0.092) B 1.09 ± 0.05 (0.043 ± 0.002) 2.36 - 2.26 (0.093 - 0.089) C 1.09 ± 0.05 (0.043 ± 0.002) Above 2.5 (0.098) Check dimensions of all elements 1.09 ± 0.05 (0.043 ± 0.002) Unit: mm (in) Dial indicator (KV109B0060) mounted on dial support (KV109B0050) positioned above calibration piston on cylinder block deck — setup for TDC zeroing Dial indicator (KV109B0060) mounted on support moved onto the cylinder block deck to measure piston protrusion difference

423.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Assembly: PISTON
423.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Assembly: PISTON EM-5043 PISTON Assembly Align the direction of piston and connecting rod; the piston cloverleaf sits opposite the bearing halfshell retainers. Install new snap ring on one side of piston pin hole. Install new snap ring. After assembling, ascertain that piston swings smoothly. Confirm that the correct piston height type is used. Refer to "Inspection" (EM-5042). Install piston rings with a suitable tool. a) When installing piston rings, make sure that stamped mark "TOP" is facing top of piston. Align gaps of piston rings with intervals of 120°, as shown in illustration. b) Align gaps of piston rings with intervals of 120°, as shown in illustration. Piston and connecting rod assembly showing piston height type mark, clover leaf marking on piston crown, and bearing retainers on connecting rod big end Illustration of piston rings being installed onto piston using a suitable ring expander tool Top-down view of piston showing piston ring gaps aligned at 120° intervals around the piston circumference

424.title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Assembly (Cont'd) — CRANKSHAFT
424.body: CYLINDER BLOCK — Assembly (Cont'd) — CRANKSHAFT EM-5044 Crankshaft Assembly Set main bearings in their proper positions on cylinder block and main bearing cap. Confirm that correct main bearings are used. Refer to "Inspection" (EM-5040). No. 2 and No. 4 main bearing halfshells have an oil groove. The oil grooves of the thrust bearing should face the crankshaft. Install crankshaft and main bearing caps and tighten bolts as follows: Main bearing cap bolts — (1) Tighten to 20 N·m (2.0 kg-m, 14.8 ft-lb) in order shown. (2) Turn bolts to 50° degrees clockwise with an angle wrench in order shown. After securing bearing cap bolts, make sure crankshaft turns smoothly by hand. Measure crankshaft end play. If beyond the standard clearance, replace thrust bearing with a new one. Crankshaft end play — Standard 0.07 - 0.27 mm Standard clearance Crankshaft end play — Standard 0.0024 - 0.0102 in Standard clearance Main bearing cap bolt torque — Step 1 20 N·m Initial tighten in order shown Main bearing cap bolt torque — Step 1 2.0 kg-m Initial tighten in order shown Main bearing cap bolt torque — Step 1 14.8 ft-lb Initial tighten in order shown Main bearing cap bolt torque — Step 2 50 degrees Angle tighten clockwise with angle wrench in order shown Exploded/assembly diagram showing main bearing layout: thrust bearing (upper only), upper bearing, lower bearing, oil grooves, No.1 bearing (flywheel side), front orientation Top view of cylinder block showing main bearing cap bolt tightening sequence numbers (1 through 10) Photo showing crankshaft being rotated by hand after bearing cap bolts are secured, verifying smooth rotation Photo showing dial gauge setup to measure crankshaft end play

425.title: CYLINDER BLOCK - Assembly (Cont'd)
425.body: CYLINDER BLOCK - Assembly (Cont'd) EM-5045 Assembly (Cont'd) Install connecting rod bearings in connecting rods and connecting rod caps. Install pistons with connecting rods. Install them into corresponding cylinders with Tool. Be careful not to scratch cylinder wall by connecting rod. Arrange so that piston clover leaf faces toward the oil filter. Install connecting rod caps. Tighten connecting rod cap nuts to the specified torque. After installation, check piston protrusion. Refer to "Inspection" (EM-5042). Install rear oil seal retainer. Before installing rear oil seal retainer, remove all traces of liquid gasket from mating surface using a scraper. Also remove traces of liquid gasket from mating surface of cylinder block. Apply a continuous bead of liquid gasket to mating surface of rear oil seal retainer. Use Genuine Liquid Gasket or equivalent. Connecting rod cap nuts torque 40 N·m Connecting rod cap nuts torque 4.1 kg-m Connecting rod cap nuts torque 29.5 ft-lb Liquid gasket bead width 2.0 - 3.0 mm rear oil seal retainer mating surface Liquid gasket bead width 0.079 - 0.118 in rear oil seal retainer mating surface Cylinder block with pistons and connecting rods installed, arrow indicating oil filter side Bottom view of cylinder block showing connecting rod cap nuts tightening sequence with arrows Hands cleaning/scraping rear oil seal retainer mating surface Rear oil seal retainer showing liquid gasket bead application area, bead width 2.0-3.0 mm (0.079-0.118 in)

432.title: Outer Component Parts - Rocker Cover (Cont'd)
432.body: Outer Component Parts - Rocker Cover (Cont'd) EM-7004 Rocker Cover Refitting (Continued) Fit the cover. Tighten the bolts in the sequence shown to the correct torque. Recheck bolts. Check a third time. Reconnect the hoses. Refit ignition coils. Rocker cover bolt tightening sequence diagram showing numbered bolt positions (1-10) on the rocker cover, with caption 'Tighten in numerical order.'

433.title: COMPRESSION PRESSURE
433.body: COMPRESSION PRESSURE EM-7005 Measurement of Compression Pressure Warm up engine. Turn ignition switch off. Release fuel pressure. Refer to section EF & EC. Disconnect and remove ignition coils. Remove all spark plugs. Attach a compression tester to No. 1 cylinder. Use compressor tester whose end (rubber portion) is less than 20 mm (0.79 in) dia. Otherwise, it may be caught by cylinder head during removal. Depress accelerator pedal fully to keep throttle valve wide open. Crank engine and record highest gauge indication. Repeat the measurement on each cylinder as shown above. Always use a fully-charged battery to obtain specified engine revolution. If cylinder compression in one or more cylinders is low, pour a small amount of engine oil into cylinders through spark plug holes and retest compression. If adding oil helps compression, piston rings may be worn or damaged. If so, replace piston rings after checking piston. If pressure stays low, a valve may be sticking or seating improperly. Inspect and repair valve and valve seat. (Refer to S.D.S.) If valve or valve seat is damaged excessively, replace them. If compression in any two adjacent cylinders is low and if adding oil does not help compression, there is leakage past the gasket surface. If so, replace cylinder head gasket. Compression pressure — Standard 1324 (13.2, 13.5, 192) kPa (bar, kg/cm², psi) at 350 rpm Compression pressure — Minimum 1128 (11.3, 11.5, 164) kPa (bar, kg/cm², psi) at 350 rpm Difference limit between cylinders 98 (0.98, 1.0, 14) kPa (bar, kg/cm², psi) at 350 rpm View of engine showing ignition coils and spark plug locations on cylinder head Compression tester tool illustration showing rubber portion diameter of 20 mm (0.79 in). Note: Use compressor tester whose end (rubber portion) is less than 20 mm (0.79 in) dia. Otherwise, it may be caught by cylinder head during removal.

434.title: TIMING CHAIN
434.body: TIMING CHAIN EM-7006 After removing timing chain, do not turn crankshaft and camshaft separately, or valves will strike piston heads. When installing chain tensioner, oil seats, or other sliding parts, lubricate contacting surfaces with new engine oil. Apply new engine oil to bolt threads and seat surface when installing camshaft sprocket and crankshaft pulley. When removing oil pump assembly, remove camshaft position sensore (PHASE), then remove timing chain from engine. Be careful not to damage sensor edges. Removal Drain engine coolant from radiator and cylinder block. Be careful not to spill coolant on drive belts. Remove the alternator and power steering drive belts. Remove air cleaner. Remove front right-side wheel. Remove front and right-side splashguards. Remove front exhaust tube. Remove ignition coils. Remove rocker cover. Remove all spark plugs. Set No. 1 piston at T.D.C. on compression stroke. Drive belt arrangement showing alternator and power steering drive belts Ignition coils and rocker cover with loosen sequence numbered in order Crankshaft pulley with pointer and TDC timing marks (5°, 10°, 15°, 20°)

435.title: TIMING CHAIN — Removal (Cont'd)
435.body: TIMING CHAIN — Removal (Cont'd) EM-7007 Removal (Cont'd) Remove camshaft position sensor (PHASE). Do not allow any magnetic materials to contact the camshaft position sensor (PHASE). Be careful not to damage sensor. Remove cam sprocket cover. Remove chain tensioner. Loosen idler sprocket bolt. Remove camshaft sprocket bolts. Remove camshaft sprockets. Remove cylinder head front bolts. Remove idler sprocket bolt. Drain engine oil. Remove upper timing chain. Remove oil pan. Refer to "Removal" in "OIL PAN". Remove oil strainer. Remove crankshaft pulley. Support engine with a suitable jack. Remove engine front mounting bracket. Do not allow any magnetic materials to contact the camshaft position sensor (PHASE). Be careful not to damage sensor. Camshaft position sensor (PHASE) removal location on engine Chain tensioner, idler sprocket bolt, camshaft sprocket bolts and camshaft sprockets removal Cylinder head front bolts and idler sprocket bolt removal Engine with upper timing chain, oil pan, oil strainer and crankshaft pulley removal Engine front mounting bracket removal with jack support

436.title: TIMING CHAIN
436.body: TIMING CHAIN EM-7008 Removal (Cont'd) Remove front cover bolts and front cover as shown. Remove lower timing chain tension guide. Remove lower tensioner. Remove idler sprocket shaft. Remove lower timing chain. Remove oil pump drive spacer. Remove crankshaft sprocket. Check for cracks and excessive wear at roller links. Replace if necessary. Front cover with bolts indicated by arrows showing removal points Lower timing chain assembly diagram showing idler sprocket, slack guide, tension guide, lower tensioner, oil pump drive spacer, crankshaft sprocket, and lower timing chain Timing chain roller link inspection diagram showing crack and wear indicators on chain links

437.title: TIMING CHAIN — Installation
437.body: TIMING CHAIN — Installation EM-7009 Installation Confirm that No. 1 piston is set at T.D.C. on its compression stroke. Install crankshaft sprocket and lower timing chain. Set timing chain by aligning its mating mark with the one on crankshaft sprocket. Make sure sprocket's mating mark faces engine front. The number of links between the alignment marks (silver) are the same for the left and right sides, so either side can be used during alignment with the sprocket. Fit the lower chain tensioner and the chain guide. Apply liquid gasket to front cover. Install front cover. Check alignment of mating marks on chain and crankshaft sprocket. Align oil drive spacer with oil pump. Before installing front cover remove all traces of old liquid gasket. Ensure O-rings are fitted correctly. Be careful not to damage oil seal when installing front cover. Install engine front mounting. Install oil strainer. Install oil pan. Refer to "Installation" in "OIL PAN". Install crankshaft pulley. Install center member. Liquid gasket bead width on front cover 2.0 - 3.0 mm (0.079 - 0.118 in) Applied to front cover mating surface Timing chain links between alignment marks (left side) 18 links Between mating marks on lower timing chain Timing chain links between alignment marks (right side) 18 links Between mating marks on lower timing chain Crankshaft key position — shows crankshaft with key at top dead centre position Lower timing chain installation — shows mating marks (yellow) on chain and sprockets, 18 links on each side, same number link callout, mating marks on crankshaft sprocket Front cover with liquid gasket application — bead width 2.0–3.0 mm (0.079–0.118 in) indicated Front cover rear face showing O-ring positions

438.title: TIMING CHAIN - Installation (Cont'd)
438.body: TIMING CHAIN - Installation (Cont'd) EM-7010 Installation (Cont'd) Set idler sprocket by aligning the mating mark on the larger sprocket with the yellow mating mark on the lower timing chain. Install upper timing chain and set it by aligning the mating mark on the smaller sprocket with the silver mating marks on the upper timing chain. Make sure sprocket's mating mark faces engine front. Install idler sprocket shaft from the rear side. Ensure that the shaft is located correctly, as shown. Assemble camshaft sprocket with chain. Set timing chain by aligning mating marks with those of camshaft sprockets. Make sure sprocket's mating marks face engine front. Install camshaft sprocket bolts. Make sure camshafts are aligned as shown in figure. Lower and upper timing chain mating mark positions Mating mark (silver) — upper left sprocket 5 links 7 links 11 links Mating mark (bronze) Mating mark (yellow) Lower timing chain Upper timing chain Mating mark (bronze) Idler shaft and dowel correct installation position Dowel Idler shaft Camshaft sprocket upper timing chain mating mark positions Mating mark (silver) — left 5 links 7 links 11 links Mating mark (silver) — right Mating mark (bronze) Mating mark Camshaft knock pin alignment positions for correct camshaft installation Knock pin Knock pin Upper and lower timing chain mating mark alignment diagram showing mating marks (silver, bronze, yellow) and link counts (5 links, 7 links, 11 links) for idler sprocket and timing chains Idler shaft installation showing dowel and idler shaft location, illustrating correct shaft positioning Camshaft sprocket and upper timing chain mating mark alignment diagram showing mating marks (silver, bronze) and link counts (5 links, 7 links, 11 links) Camshaft alignment diagram showing knock pin positions on both camshafts to verify correct camshaft alignment during sprocket bolt installation

439.title: TIMING CHAIN - Installation (Cont'd)
439.body:
TIMING CHAIN - Installation (Cont'd) EM-7011 Tensioner body Piston Ratchet Spring Plunger Installation (Cont'd) Assemble the tensioner by rotating and pushing the ratchet up the spiral until it stops. The tensioner is now pre-set. Chain lash will automatically set the tensioner when the engine is started. Install the tensioner. Apply liquid gasket to cam sprocket cover as illustrated then install cam sprocket cover. Clean the rocker cover, ensure it is free from grease. Fit the gasket & cover within 5 minutes of applying the liquid gasket, as follows: CAUTION:
• Check no problems occur when engine is rotated.
• Make sure that No. 1 piston is set at T.D.C. on its compression stroke. Liquid gasket bead width (cam sprocket cover) 2.0 - 3.0 mm Liquid gasket bead width (cam sprocket cover) 0.079 - 0.118 in Fit the gasket & cover within 5 minutes of applying the liquid gasket, as follows: Exploded view of tensioner components: Tensioner body, Piston, Ratchet, Spring, Plunger Hands assembling tensioner - rotating and pushing ratchet up the spiral on the piston Installed tensioner on engine with timing chain visible Cam sprocket cover showing liquid gasket application areas with bead width 2.0 - 3.0 mm (0.079 - 0.118 in)

440.title: TIMING CHAIN — Installation (Cont'd)
440.body: TIMING CHAIN — Installation (Cont'd) EM-7012 Liquid gasket sealant bead width (each side) 3.0 (0.118) mm (in) Applied to cylinder head at rocker cover mating surface Installation (Cont'd) Apply liquid gasket to the cylinder head as shown. Fit the gasket to the cover. Fit the rocker cover to the cylinder head. Tighten the bolts in the correct order, as shown. Recheck bolts. Check a third time. Install camshaft position sensor (PHASE). Install all spark plugs and ignition coils. Install engine front mounting bracket. Install front exhaust tube. Install the front and side splashguards. Install front right wheel. Install air cleaner. Install and correctly tension the alternator and power steering drive belts. Refer to MA section for correct tension. Refer to MA section for correct tension. Plan view of cylinder head showing liquid gasket (sealant) application points. Two sealant bead dimensions shown: 3.0 (0.118) mm on each side. Rocker cover showing bolt tightening sequence numbered 1 through 10 in numerical order.

441.title: OIL SEAL REPLACEMENT
441.body: OIL SEAL REPLACEMENT EM-7013 REAR OIL SEAL Remove flywheel. Remove rear oil seal retainer. Remove traces of liquid gasket using scraper. Remove seal from rear oil seal retainer. Apply engine oil to new oil seal and install it using a suitable tool. Apply liquid gasket to rear oil seal retainer. Apply thread sealant to the bolt indicated in the figure. Support cover when installing seal. Using a scraper to remove traces of liquid gasket from rear oil seal retainer mating surface Removing seal from rear oil seal retainer using a tool Installing new oil seal into rear oil seal retainer using a suitable tool and hammer Rear oil seal retainer showing location where liquid gasket is applied Engine rear view showing bolt location where thread sealant is to be applied
