539.title: Engine Races When Driving
539.body: Engine Races When Driving AT-82 Symptom: The engine races when driving Diagnosis item: Inhibitor switch is faulty; Manual control linkage adjustment or control linkage is faulty; Control valve is faulty; N-CVT control unit power supply or N-CVT control unit is faulty; Belt slipping (A loud noise that sounds like rubbing metal objects); Electromagnetic clutch is faulty Engine Races When Driving — Check Procedure Perform self diagnosis Inspect and repair based on trouble code. When O.K. code or trouble code is not output, check N-CVT control unit. Can the symptom be reproduced? Cannot be reproduced easily Drive the vehicle while "CLUTCH CURRENT" is displayed on CONSULT screen When the engine speed is increased, does the clutch current flow? Check control linkage Adjust or replace selector arm or control linkage Check inhibitor switch circuit Repair or replace inhibitor switch harness or connector Check electromagnetic clutch circuit Repair or replace electromagnetic clutch main unit, brush holder or harness. Replace N-CVT control unit Does vibration or shock occur after the engine speed is increased? Replace electromagnetic clutch Check or repair main unit: 1. Oil pump 2. Control valve 3. Belt and pulley mechanism N.G. O.K. Cannot be reproduced easily Can be reproduced easily Yes No N.G. O.K. N.G. O.K. N.G. O.K. No Yes N-CVT transmission diagnosis. Applies to K11 Micra with N-CVT (automatic) transmission.

540.title: Trouble Diagnosis — Stall Test
540.body: Trouble Diagnosis — Stall Test AT-83 Stall Test Check the oil level in engine and N·CVT. Replenish if necessary. Drive the vehicle approximately 10 minutes to warm the engine and N·CVT oil to 50 to 80°C. Set the parking brake and block the wheels securely. Start the engine, press the brake pedal and move the selector lever to the "D" range. While pressing the foot brake, depress the accelerator pedal gradually until it is wide open. Quickly read the engine stall speed. Remove your foot from the accelerator pedal immediately. Do not open the throttle valve for more than 5 seconds during the test. Move the select lever to the "N" range. Allow the N·CVT fluid to cool off. Run the engine at idle speed at least one minute before performing the stall test again. Refer to diagnosis chart on following page Stall speed 1,700 to 2,300 rpm Selector lever in D range, wide open throttle, foot brake applied Procedure applies to N·CVT (Nissan Continuously Variable Transmission) equipped vehicles. Illustration showing engine and N·CVT oil level check Illustration showing vehicle with parking brake set and wheels blocked Illustration showing selector lever moved to D range with brake pedal pressed Illustration showing driver pressing accelerator pedal to wide open throttle while pressing foot brake Illustration showing selector lever moved to N range

541.title: Trouble Diagnosis Quick Reference (1)
541.body: Trouble Diagnosis Quick Reference (1) AT-84 Trouble Diagnosis Quick Reference (1) TROUBLE DESCRIPTION SUSPECTED PARTS ACTION Engine stops Brake switch circuit; Vehicle speed signal circuit Check or replace the appropriate part N-CVT warning lamp blinks Slip ring contaminated by oil; Clutch is overheated (excessive clutch circuit resistance); Vehicle speed sensor circuit Check the clutch circuit and vehicle speed signal Acceleration is faulty Stall test — If stall speed is high, the clutch torque decreases Check or replace electromagnetic clutch Acceleration is faulty Stall test — If stall speed is normal, the N-CVT pulley is fixed in the overdrive position Check the N-CVT transaxle assembly Acceleration is faulty Stall test — If stall speed is low, the engine torque decreases or clutch torque becomes too high Check engine; Check or replace the electromagnetic clutch Clutch does not engage There is a short-circuit between the electromagnetic clutch circuit negative side (-) and ground Check the electromagnetic clutch circuit Engine speed is uneven at constant speeds over 30 km/h There is an abnormal decrease in clutch torque; Vehicle speed signal is faulty Check electromagnetic clutch; Check vehicle speed sensor circuit There is a sharp shock when the accelerator pedal is depressed from the released position between 10 and 20 km/h Shift characteristics are incorrect for low speeds Check the N-CVT transaxle assembly The clutch does not disengage readily when decelerating Vehicle speed signal circuit is faulty Check the vehicle speed signal system The clutch disengages when the accelerator pedal is released when driving faster than 20 km/h The brake switch circuit is faulty (malfunctions by staying ON); The ABS operation signal circuit is faulty Check the brake switch; Check the ABS operation signal circuit The clutch disengages when the brake pedal is depressed in Ds range Brake switch circuit is faulty (malfunctions by staying OFF) Check the brake switch N-CVT transaxle specific trouble diagnosis. Applies to Nissan Micra K11 with N-CVT automatic transmission.

546.title: Transmission Assembly Removal
546.body: Transmission Assembly Removal AT-90 Spring clip Conical washer Plain washer Lock plate Control cable Insulator Transaxle assembly Dust cover Member Drive shaft Throttle wire Transaxle assembly bolt torque 29.4 - 39.2 N·m Transaxle assembly bolt torque 3.0 - 4.0 Kgm Transaxle assembly bolt torque 22 - 29 ft-lb Member bolt torque (upper) 16 - 21 N·m Member bolt torque (upper) 1.6 - 2.1 Kgm Member bolt torque (upper) 12 - 15 ft-lb Member bolt torque (lower) 44 - 55 N·m Member bolt torque (lower) 4.5 - 5.5 Kgm Member bolt torque (lower) 33 - 40 ft-lb Member bolt torque (side) 16 - 21 N·m Member bolt torque (side) 1.6 - 2.1 Kgm Member bolt torque (side) 12 - 15 ft-lb Transmission Assembly Removal Remove battery, fuse box and relay box. Remove A/T throttle wire from throttle drum. Separate the connectors of the inhibitor switch, line pressure solenoid, vehicle speed sensor, and speedometer cable. Separate connecting parts of the selector linkage and control cable. Remove drive shafts. Refer to section FA, "Drive Shaft". Drain the transmission fluid Remove the oil cooler hoses. Automatic transmission (A/T) only Exploded diagram of transaxle assembly removal showing control cable, lock plate, spring clip, conical washer, plain washer, insulator, transaxle assembly, dust cover, member, drive shafts, throttle wire, and torque specifications Photo showing control cable and selector linkage separation Photo showing oil cooler hose location for removal

547.title: Transmission Assembly Removal and Installation
547.body: Transmission Assembly Removal and Installation AT-91 Transmission Assembly Removal (Cont'd) Set the transmission jack on the A/T and engine main units. When attaching the transmission jack, be careful not to make contact with the drain plug. Remove the engine mount and engine gusset. Refer to section EM, "Engine Removal and Installation". Remove the dust cover. While rotating the crankshaft, remove the four electromagnetic clutch bolts. Remove the transaxle bolts. Remove the starter motor. Refer to section EL, "Starter Motor". Lower the transmission jack and remove the transaxle assembly. Perform the following inspections. Assembly is the reverse sequence of disassembly. Tighten bolts to the specified torque. Drive Plate Surface Runout Inspection Attach a dial gauge to the drive plate. Rotate the crankshaft. Make sure the runout is within the standard value. Runout standard value 0.5 maximum mm Drive plate surface runout When the runout is greater than the standard value, replace the drive plate and ring gear assembly. TRANSAXLE BOLTS Bolt No. 1 2 3 4 No. of bolts 1 2 2 4 Length (ℓ) 50 70 25 20 Torque (Nm, kgm) 30-40 (3.1 - 4.1) 30-40 (3.1 - 4.1) 16-21 (1.6 - 2.1) 16-21 (1.6 - 2.1) Procedure applies to A/T (Automatic Transaxle) equipped vehicles. Bolt torque and length vary by bolt position number (1-4) as shown in transaxle bolt diagram. Engine bay photo showing engine mount/gusset area with arrow indicating removal point Underside photo showing transaxle assembly and surrounding components Drive plate and ring gear assembly showing surface for runout inspection with dial gauge Transaxle bolt location diagram showing bolt positions numbered 1-4, with legend: O = to eng., circled O = to T/A

548.title: Removal and Installation — Electromagnetic Clutch
548.body:
Removal and Installation — Electromagnetic Clutch AT-92 Inspection (Cont'd) Install the transaxle and other parts and then check the control linkage and inhibitor switch. Refer to "On-Vehicle Service". Adjust the throttle wire. Refer to "On-Vehicle Service". Be careful not to deform, crack or peel off the seal rubber. Replace the seal rubber if it is damaged or deformed. Electromagnetic Clutch Removal Remove the N-CVT main unit from the vehicle. Refer to "Removal and Installation" Remove electromagnetic clutch from N-CVT main unit. (1) There should be no oil on the slip ring. Do not touch it with your hands or rags containing oil. If there is any oil on the slip ring, wipe it off with thinner or a solvent. (2) Do not hit the outside of the slip ring. (3) After removing the clutch, avoid unnecessary movement of the unit. Store the clutch with the slip ring facing upwards. Note:
(1) There should be no oil on the slip ring. Do not touch it with your hands or rags containing oil. If there is any oil on the slip ring, wipe it off with thinner or a solvent.
(2) Do not hit the outside of the slip ring.
(3) After removing the clutch, avoid unnecessary movement of the unit. Store the clutch with the slip ring facing upwards. Check if there is any grease around slip ring and if the bearing moves smoothly. Replace the electromagnetic clutch assembly if the grease overflows or the bearing moves unevenly. Applies to N-CVT (N-continuously variable transmission) equipped Nissan Micra K11 N-CVT main unit with electromagnetic clutch being removed, showing overall assembly Electromagnetic clutch shown in storage position with slip ring facing upwards Electromagnetic clutch assembly viewed from above showing slip ring side

549.title: REMOVAL & INSTALLATION
549.body: REMOVAL & INSTALLATION AT-93 Check the resistance between the electromagnetic clutch slip rings with a tester. Check that the resistance is within the following range. Standard value 2 to 4 Ω Resistance between electromagnetic clutch slip rings If there is no continuity or the resistance value is extremely low, replace the electromagnetic clutch assembly. Do not press the tester probe with excessive force against the slip ring and do not damage the outside surface. Check the continuity between the slip ring and electromagnetic clutch main unit bracket (aluminium) with a tester. Replace the electromagnetic clutch if continuity exists. Do not press the tester probe with excessive force against the slip ring and do not damage the outside surface. Wipe off any dust, debris or oil that adheres to the outside of the slip ring with thinner or solvent. Check the slip ring for damage. Performing the inspection on a flat, clean surface free from dust and oil. Make sure that the slip ring side faces up. Perform the following procedures to install the electromagnetic clutch in the main unit. Assembly Rotate the oil pump shaft (B spline shaft) and check that the splines are engaged correctly. Insert the electromagnetic clutch main unit and check that it is engaged correctly with both the A and B spline shafts. Rotate the electromagnetic clutch slightly if it is difficult to insert, because the splines engage at two locations. (1) Do not apply excessive force to the A and B spline shafts. (2) If the electromagnetic clutch is inserted with excessive force, it may interfere at the location indicated by an asterisk (*). This may cause damage to the clutch assembly and internal components. (3) If the electromagnetic clutch is removed and installed, always replace the O-rings on the shaft with new parts. Continuity check being performed on electromagnetic clutch slip ring with a tester Insulation check being performed between slip ring and electromagnetic clutch main unit bracket with a tester Electromagnetic clutch slip ring placed flat side down on clean surface for condition check Assembly diagram showing A spline shaft, B spline shaft, and O-ring locations for electromagnetic clutch installation

550.title: REMOVAL & INSTALLATION
550.body: REMOVAL & INSTALLATION AT-94 Measure the distance from the end surface of t[he housi]ng to the end surface of the electromagnetic clutch. Standard value — distance from housing surface to electromagnetic clutch end surface 16 to 20 mm Assembly check measurement (Clutch to drive plate) Installation (Clutch to drive plate) Connect the drive plate and the electromagnetic clutch assembly from the lower side (oil pan) with four bolts, after assembling the transaxle to the engine. Tighten the four bolts loosely and then tighten them to the specified torque. Tightening torque — Clutch to drive plate bolts 30 to 40 (3 to 4, 22 - 29) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Four bolts, clutch to drive plate Tighten the four bolts loosely and then tighten them to the specified torque. Procedure relates to electromagnetic clutch assembly on automatic transaxle. Access from lower side (oil pan) required. Cross-section diagram showing housing and clutch assembly with 16-20 mm measurement dimension indicated from housing surface to electromagnetic clutch end surface Cross-section diagram showing drive plate and bolt installation location for clutch to drive plate connection from lower side (oil pan)

551.title: DISASSEMBLY
551.body: DISASSEMBLY AT-95 Disassembly Remove the inlet oil cooler pipe. Remove the outlet oil cooler pipe. Remove the oil cooler check valve. Discard the O ring. Inspect sliding surfaces of the spool and valve body for wear, damage, etc. Ensure the valve components are clean and free from foreign matter. Apply clean ATF during assembly. Remove the manual shaft nut while locking the arm with an 8mm dia. pin inserted in the cut-outs as shown. Remove the selector linkage spring pins. Remove the linkage and spacers. Remove the inhibitor switch. Remove the harness bracket. Inlet oil cooler pipe location on transmission, with callout label Outlet oil cooler pipe location on transmission, with callout label Check valve assembly location on transmission valve body Manual shaft nut removal with 8mm pin locking the arm in the cut-outs; selector linkage spring pins and linkage/spacers removal Inhibitor switch and harness bracket removal location on transmission

552.title: DISASSEMBLY
552.body: DISASSEMBLY AT-96 Transaxle Disassembly Fit the transaxle assembly to the transmission stand, SST KV319-K0160 Drain any remaining ATF fluid from the transmission. Refit the plug. Carefully remove the oil pump drive shaft. Check the clip on the end of the shaft for damage. Inspect the bearing surfaces for wear or damage. Replace the shaft/clip assembly if necessary. Remove the fourteen bolts and separate the clutch housing and transmission. Lightly tap the protruding surfaces to free the locating dowels, as necessary. Clutch Housing Bolt Specifications Bolt A B C Number  1 5 8 Length (mm) 100 35 40 Automatic Transaxle (AT) disassembly procedure Transaxle assembly fitted to transmission stand SST KV319-K0160 Removal of oil pump drive shaft from transaxle assembly Cross-section diagram showing bushing and clip location on oil pump drive shaft Clutch housing and transmission separation Clutch housing face showing bolt positions labeled A, B, and C

553.title: DISASSEMBLY
553.body: DISASSEMBLY AT-97 Disassembly (continued) Lift out the final drive assembly. Remove the oil pipe securing bolt. Remove the pipe and nozzle. Rotate the shift cam until the roll pin is pointing in the direction shown. Gently tap out the roll pin. Discard the pin. Do not re-use. Remove the engine brake cam circlip. Remove the cam plate and pin. Remove the rear circlip. Lift out the shift arm securing pin using pliers. Discard the pin. Do not re-use. Hands lifting out the final drive assembly from the transmission case Oil pipe and nozzle location in the transmission case, with Pipe and Nozzle labelled Shift cam with roll pin direction shown at 15 degrees in inset diagram; tap out roll pin Transmission case showing engine brake cam circlip, cam plate and pin, and rear circlip locations Pliers being used to lift out the shift arm securing pin

554.title: DISASSEMBLY
554.body: DISASSEMBLY AT-98 Disassembly Remove the shift arm. Remove the shift cam/parking rod assembly. Remove synchroniser needle bearing. Remove the circlip. Use flat-blade circlip pliers. Take care not to scratch the bearing surface. Lift the synchroniser assembly slightly for access, and lift out the counter gear and shaft. Remove the synchroniser/reverse gear as an assembly. Remove needle bearing. Removal of the shift arm, with callout labelling 'Shift arm' Removal of synchroniser needle bearing Removal of circlip using flat-blade circlip pliers, with callout 'Rolling surface of the needle bearing' Lifting synchroniser assembly and lifting out counter gear and shaft, with callout 'Sleeve and hub ASSY' Removal of synchroniser/reverse gear assembly and needle bearing

555.title: DISASSEMBLY
555.body: DISASSEMBLY AT-99 Disassembly Remove the reverse idler gear and shaft. Remove the engine brake pipe and valve body. Remove the oil pan. Discard the gasket. There may still be some ATF in the oil pan. Have a container ready. Disconnect the control cable. Remove the oil strainer. Remove and discard the O-ring. A considerable amount of ATF will flow from the inlet port. Have a container ready. Removing the reverse idler gear from the transmission assembly, with callout indicating 'Reverse idler gear' Removing the engine brake pipe and valve body, with callouts for 'Brake pipe' and 'Valve body' Removing the oil pan from the transmission Disconnecting the control cable from the transmission Removing the oil strainer from the transmission, with arrows indicating the O-ring location

556.title: DISASSEMBLY
556.body: DISASSEMBLY AT-100 Disassembly Disconnect the harness. Remove the spring clip and disconnect the modulator rod. Fit Tool (control valve holder) to prevent the pressure regulator valve assembly jumping out. Fit the regulator end of the holder first, then press down at the opposite end, and locate the holder around the centre bolt. Remove the valve and holder as a unit. Ensure that the valve and holder do not separate. Do not disassemble the control valve. Illustration showing disconnection of the harness Illustration showing modulator rod and spring clip location for disconnection Illustration showing SST KV319-K0100 (control valve holder) fitted to prevent pressure regulator valve assembly jumping out Illustration showing fitting the regulator end of the holder first, pressing down at opposite end, locating holder around centre bolt Illustration showing removal of valve and holder as a unit

557.title: DISASSEMBLY
557.body: DISASSEMBLY AT-101 Disassembly (continued) Remove the oil pump securing bolts. Fit Tool and remove the oil pump. Remove the side case securing bolts. Do not remove the three pulley retaining bolts. Separate the side case from the transmission as shown, tap lightly with a soft hammer if necessary. Do not tap the oil pan mating surface. Lift out the ratio sensor assembly. Lift out the parking pawl assembly. Do not remove the three pulley retaining bolts. Do not tap the oil pan mating surface. Oil pump removal using tool KV319-K0150 with M6 bolt and puller bolts fitted to oil pump Side case with pulley retainer bolts visible, showing which bolts not to remove Separating the side case from the transmission using a soft hammer Ratio sensor assembly being lifted out from the transmission Parking pawl assembly being lifted out from the transmission

558.title: DISASSEMBLY
558.body: DISASSEMBLY AT-102 CVT Disassembly (continued) Remove the pulley retaining bolts complete with O rings. Fit two harness ties to the drive belt as shown. This will prevent the bands separating. Fit Tool to the secondary pulley, and expand the groove width to 52 - 53 mm (2.05- 2.09 in). Do not expand the groove width any more than stated, internal damage may occur. Ease the secondary pulley away from the side case. Engage the pulley grooves and remove the drive belt from the primary pulley. Remove the secondary pulley and drive belt together. Pulley/drive belt removal is best achieved with two technicians. While one lifts the secondary pulley and engages the grooves, the other removes the belt from the primary pulley. Keep components safely stored, in clean conditions, until required for re-assembly. Pulley/drive belt removal is best achieved with two technicians. While one lifts the secondary pulley and engages the grooves, the other removes the belt from the primary pulley. Keep components safely stored, in clean conditions, until required for re-assembly. CVT (Continuously Variable Transmission) disassembly procedure. Applicable to K11 Micra with automatic CVT transmission. View of transmission showing pulley retaining bolts with arrows indicating their location Drive belt with harness ties fitted to prevent band separation Tool KV319-K0090 fitted to secondary pulley showing groove width expansion dimensions of 52-53 mm (2.05-2.09 in), with Seat callout Secondary pulley being eased away from side case with drive belt engaged in pulley grooves Two technician hands removing secondary pulley and drive belt together from the transmission

559.title: DISASSEMBLY
559.body: DISASSEMBLY AT-103 Disassembly Position the pitot tube as shown, prior to primary pulley removal. This will prevent the pulley damaging the tube. Lift out the primary pulley assembly. Store in clean conditions. View of transmission casing showing pitot tube positioned prior to primary pulley removal, with arrow indicating tube position Hands lifting out the primary pulley assembly from the transmission casing

560.title: REPAIR FOR COMPONENT PARTS — Secondary Pulley Assembly
560.body: REPAIR FOR COMPONENT PARTS — Secondary Pulley Assembly AT-104 Disassembly Fit the pulley to Tool and fit to a vice. Remove and discard the lock nut. Do not use an air wrench to loosen the lock nut, internal damage may occur. Press out the bearing as shown. The pulley cannot be disassembled. Check the bearing for wear, scoring, seizure or noisy operation. Check the gear for broken or worn teeth. Inspect the bearing surface contact area for damage. Replace parts as necessary. Assembly Press the reduction gear into the pulley. Press the bearing on to the pulley. Fit and tighten a new lock nut. Stake the lock nut after tightening. Lock nut torque 123 - 132 N·m Assembly step 3 Lock nut torque 12.5 - 13.4 kg-m Assembly step 3 Lock nut torque 90 - 97 ft-lb Assembly step 3 Holder KV319-K0070 fitted to the secondary pulley assembly in a vice Pressing out the needle bearing race using a drift (20 mm dia.) Pressing the reduction gear into the pulley using a bearing adaptor Pressing the bearing onto the pulley using a bearing adaptor, showing needle bearing race and reduction drive gear

561.title: REPAIR FOR COMPONENT PARTS — Oil Pump Assembly
561.body:
REPAIR FOR COMPONENT PARTS — Oil Pump Assembly AT-105 Disassembly Remove the three bolts. Install two bolts as shown, and gently tap the pump apart. Check for scratches on the cover inner surface and internal diameter of the gear ports.
Check for smooth movement of lubrication valve, and scratches on sliding surfaces.
Check smooth operation of pump gears.
Ensure components are clean and free from foreign matter.
Replace the oil pump as an assembly, if necessary. Apply ATF to all moving parts during assembly.
Tighten the bolts evenly.
Check shaft rotates smoothly. Bolt torque 9.5 - 10.5 N·m Tighten the bolts evenly Bolt torque 0.97 - 1.07 kg-m Tighten the bolts evenly Bolt torque 7 - 8 ft-lb Tighten the bolts evenly Oil pump assembly showing outer cover, inner cover, and housing components Illustration showing two bolts installed and pump being tapped apart with a drift/punch Exploded view of oil pump components with arrows indicating inspection points including lubrication valve, spring, and gears

562.title: REPAIR FOR COMPONENT PARTS — Main Drive Shaft Assembly
562.body:
REPAIR FOR COMPONENT PARTS — Main Drive Shaft Assembly AT-106 Disassembly Remove the securing bolts. Tap the end of the shaft with a plastic hammer to remove. Remove and discard the oil seals from drive shaft and the clutch housing. Remove the circlip. Press out the bearing. Check for broken or worn teeth.
Check for damaged or seized cone surface.
Check bearing contact surface.
Check for noisy operation and signs of seizure.
Replace parts as necessary. Check the clearance between the baulk ring and cone. Clearance between baulk ring and cone (C) — Standard 1.0 mm (0.039 in) Standard Clearance between baulk ring and cone (C) — Limit 0.4 mm (0.016 in) Limit Check contact surfaces for wear or damage.
Check the hub shift insert for wear marks.
Replace parts as necessary. View of main drive shaft assembly in clutch housing showing securing bolts location Tapping the end of the shaft with a plastic hammer to remove Press tool pressing out the bearing from the retainer Diagram showing synchroniser baulk ring and cone with clearance C indicated, Ring and Cone labelled

563.title: REPAIR FOR COMPONENT PARTS — Main Drive Shaft Assembly
563.body: REPAIR FOR COMPONENT PARTS — Main Drive Shaft Assembly AT-107 Assembly Press a new oil seal into the drive shaft as shown. Drift diameter: 21 mm dia. Press depth: 2.5 mm Press a new seal into the clutch housing as shown. Use oil seal drift Install the shaft into the clutch housing, ensure correct alignment. Fit and tighten the retaining bolts. Retaining bolt torque 4.5 - 5.5 N·m Step 4 — retaining bolts Retaining bolt torque 0.46 - 0.56 kg-m Step 4 — retaining bolts Retaining bolt torque 3 - 4 ft-lb Step 4 — retaining bolts Oil seal press depth 2.5 mm Step 1 — oil seal into drive shaft Drift diameter 21 mm dia. Step 1 — pressing oil seal into drive shaft Cross-section diagram showing oil seal being pressed into drive shaft using a drift (21 mm dia.), with 2.5 mm depth dimension indicated Cross-section diagram showing new seal being pressed into clutch housing using an oil seal drift Cross-section diagram showing shaft being installed into clutch housing with press, and retaining bolts being tightened

564.title: REPAIR FOR COMPONENT PARTS — Drive Pinion Shaft Assembly
564.body: REPAIR FOR COMPONENT PARTS — Drive Pinion Shaft Assembly AT-108 Disassembly Remove securing bolts. Remove shaft assembly. Fit the shaft to Tool, and fit to a vice. Remove and discard the lock nut. Press out the bearing and gear. Check the gear teeth for wear and damage. Check the cone surface for wear or seizure damage. Check bearings for noise, damage or signs of seizure. Assembly Press the gear onto the shaft. Fit the retainer plate. Removing securing bolts from drive pinion shaft assembly in transmission case Fitting shaft to Tool KV319-K0080 in a vice, removing lock nut Pressing out bearing and gear using drift and press Pressing gear onto shaft using press

565.title: REPAIR FOR COMPONENT PARTS — Drive Pinion Shaft Assembly
565.body: REPAIR FOR COMPONENT PARTS — Drive Pinion Shaft Assembly AT-109 Assembly (Cont'd) Press fit the bearing. Ensure that the ID marks on the bearing face upwards. Fit and tighten a new lock nut. Torque = 137 - 157 N·m (14.0 - 16.0 kg-m, 101 - 116 ft-lb) Stake the lock nut after tightening. Fit the shaft assembly to the housing. Ensure correct alignment. Fit and tighten the retaining plate bolts. Torque = 22 - 26 N·m (2.2 - 2.7 kg-m, 16 - 20 ft-lb) Lock nut torque 137 - 157 N·m New lock nut Lock nut torque 14.0 - 16.0 kg-m New lock nut Lock nut torque 101 - 116 ft-lb New lock nut Retaining plate bolts torque 22 - 26 N·m Retaining plate bolts torque 2.2 - 2.7 kg-m Retaining plate bolts torque 16 - 20 ft-lb Pitot tube Remove the pin using pliers, and remove the tube. Replace if damage is evident. Replace if damage is evident. Shifter and Park Rod Assembly Check for wear on the shifter cam and pawl areas. Check the park rod for damage or distortions. Replace components as necessary. Cross-section illustration showing press fit of bearing using Oil Seal Drift tool onto retainer plate Illustration showing shaft assembly fitted to housing with retaining plate bolts Photo showing Pitot tube location on the transmission housing assembly Exploded illustration of Shifter and Park Rod Assembly components

566.title: Assembly
566.body: Assembly AT-110 Before commencing, ensure total cleanliness of all components. Remove any residual ATF from housings, channels, etc. Replace primary & secondary pulleys as a unit, not individually, if either one is damaged. If the drive belt shows "slip" marks, replace the belt and both pulleys. Assembly Attach the side casing to SST KV319-K0160 transmission stand. Connect the pitot pipe. Point the pin towards the centre of the bearing, as shown. The pin must remain like this, until the pulleys/transmission case are assembled. Check the pin position frequently. Install new seal rings on the secondary pulley guide using Tool. Pulley Alignment and Primary End Play (Replacement of pulleys and side casing) Pulley Alignment (1) Remove all traces of old liquid gasket from the side case mating surface, and clean as required. (2) Dress the mating surface of the side case to remove burrs. (3) Wash clean the side case to remove all traces of grease. (4) Secure the gauge Tool to the side case using bolts. Applicable to CVT (Continuously Variable Transmission) automatic transmission assembly procedure Side casing attached to SST KV319-K0160 transmission stand Pitot pipe connection with pin pointed towards centre of bearing Seal rings installation on secondary pulley guide using Guide KV319-K0110 Gauge Tool KV319-K0130 secured to side case using bolts (tighten bolt from lower side)

567.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
567.body:
Assembly (Cont'd) AT-111 Assembly (Cont'd) Measure the depth between the mating surface of the side case and the mounting portion of the primary pulley ball bearing and between the mating surface of the side case and the mounting portion of the secondary pulley ball bearing, using the depth gauge Tool. The depths must be respectively measured at four points. Determine the average values of the measurements. Equation for calculation (Unit: mm): X = A - B - (Lp - Ls) - 24.396

where:
t = Shim thickness (As a result of the calculation of the above equation (x). Select a suitable shim from among those listed in the following table)
A: Indicated primary pulley dimension [On service parts only]
B: Indicated secondary pulley dimension [On service parts only]
Lp: Depth between upper surface of gauge and mounting portion of primary pulley bearing (average value of four-point measurements)
Ls: Depth between upper surface of gauge and mounting portion of secondary pulley bearing (average value of four-point measurements)
24.396: Constant Shim Selection Table x mm (Calculated value) t mm (in) (Shim thickness) 0 - 0.050 0 (0) 0.050 - 0.150 0.1 (0.004) 0.150 - 0.250 0.2 (0.008) 0.250 - 0.350 0.3 (0.012) 0.350 - 0.450 0.4 (0.016) 0.450 - 0.550 0.5 (0.020) 0.550 - 0.650 0.6 (0.024) If the shim thickness determined by calculation overlaps the value in the next range, use a shim in either range. For example, if the calculated shim thickness is 0.250 mm, either a 0.2 or 0.3 mm (0.008 or 0.012) shim can be used. Depth gauge tool KV319-K0020 being used to measure depth at mating surface of side case for primary pulley ball bearing mounting portion Cross-section diagram showing Gauge KV319-K0130 positioned with Lp and Ls measurements indicated for primary and secondary pulley bearing depths Hand installing or adjusting shim inside CVT side case assembly

568.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
568.body:
Assembly (Cont'd) AT-112 Primary pulley end play Remove all traces of old liquid gasket from the mating surface of the transmission case and clean, as required. Dress the mating surface of the side case to remove burrs. Wash clean the side case to remove all traces of grease. Secure the gauge Tool to the transmission case using bolts. Using the gauge and the depth gauge Tool, measure the "depth" (distance) between the mating surface of the transmission case and the stepped portion at the 70 mm (2.76 in) dia. hole. Equation for calculation (Unit: mm): X = C + Lp - D - 2t

where:
T: Snap ring thickness (Select a suitable snap ring whose thickness is determined by the above calculation (x) from among those listed in the table below.)
C: Depth or distance between upper surface of gauge and the recess for the snap ring at the 70 mm (2.76 in) hole (Average value of fourpoint measurements)
Lp: Dimension measured under "Adjusting the pulley alignment"
D: Indicated primary pulley dimension
t: Gauge thickness (stamped on the side of gauge) Snap Ring Selection Table x mm (Calculated value) T mm (in) (Snap ring thickness) 1.2 - 1.28 1.35 (0.0531) 1.28 - 1.36 1.43 (0.0563) 1.36 - 1.44 1.51 (0.0594) 1.44 - 1.52 1.59 (0.0626) 1.52 - 1.60 1.67 (0.0657) 1.60 - 1.68 1.75 (0.0689) 1.68 - 1.76 1.83 (0.0720) If the snap ring thickness determined by calculation overlaps the value in the next range, use a snap ring in either range. For example, if the calculated thickness is 1.36 mm, either a 1.43 or 1.51 mm (0.0563 or 0.0594 in) snap ring can be used. Gauge tools KV319-K0020 and KV319-K0130 shown secured to transmission case Cross-section diagram showing gauge, primary pulley, secondary pulley, side case, mating surface, and 70 mm (2.76 in) dia. hole measurement location Illustration of depth gauge tool being used to measure the stepped portion

569.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
569.body:
Assembly (Cont'd) AT-113 Pulley Alignment & Primary end Play (Replacement of side casing only) Pulley Alignment & Primary end Play (Replacement of side casing only) Pulley alignment Dress the mating surface of the side case to remove burrs. Wash clean the side case to remove all traces of grease. Secure the gauge Tool to the side case using bolts. Measure the depth between the mating surface of the side case and the mounting portion of the primary pulley ball bearing and between the mating surface of the side case and the mounting portion of the secondary pulley ball bearing, using the depth gauge Tool. The depths must be respectively measured at four points. Determine the average values of the measurements. Equation for calculation: LD = Lp - Ls
Where: LD: Calculated value Measurements must be made on the new and old side cases.
L_D1: Calculated value of the old side case
L_D2: Calculated value of the new side case Shim selection: Select shim(s) determined by Equation "L_D2 - L_D1" or "L_D1 - L_D2", as applicable. Shim Selection Table Row Condition Action 1 If L_D2 - L_D1 is less than 0.05 use a new shim whose thickness is the same as that used on the secondary side of the old side case ("old shim") 2 If L_D2 - L_D1 is equal to or greater than 0.05, but less than 0.15 use a new shim 0.1 mm (0.004 in) thinner than the old shim. If the old shim is 0.1 mm (0.004 in) thick, a shim need not be used. 3 If L_D2 - L_D1 is equal to or greater than 0.15, but less than 0.25 use a new shim 0.2 mm (0.008 in) thinner than the old shim. 4 L_D1 - L_D2 is less than 0.05 Same as (1) above. 5 If L_D1 - L_D2 is equal to or greater than 0.05, but less than 0.15 use a new shim 0.1 mm (0.004 in) thicker than the old shim. 6 If L_D1 - L_D2 is equal to or greather than 0.15, but less than 0.25 use a new shim 0.2 mm (0.008 in) thicker than the old shim. Illustration showing gauge tools KV319-K0020 and KV319-K0130 being used on the side case to measure depth for pulley alignment Diagram of side case showing bolt tightening pattern (marked with X) and note 'Thighten from lower side' Cross-section diagram showing gauge positioned on side case with Lp and Ls measurement labels

570.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
570.body: Assembly (Cont'd) AT-114 Shim Thickness t mm (in) (Shim thickness) 0.1 (0.004) 0.2 (0.008) 0.3 (0.012) 0.4 (0.016) 0.5 (0.020) 0.6 (0.024) Pulley end play Select a shim measured as "Lp" under "Pulley Alignment". Lp1: Dimension of the old side case. Lp2: Dimension of the new side case. Snap ring selection. Select a snap ring determined by Equation "Lp2 - Lp1" or "Lp1 - Lp2", as applicable. Illustration showing a hand tool being used on a transmission assembly component, related to pulley end play/snap ring selection procedure

571.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
571.body: Assembly (Cont'd) AT-115 Unit: mm Snap Ring Selection Table Condition # Condition Action 1 If Lp2 - Lp1 is less than 0.05 use a new snap ring whose thickness is the same as that used on the old primary pulley side ("old shim"). 2 If Lp2 - Lp1 is equal to or greater than 0.05, but less than 0.13 use a new snap ring one rank higher in thickness than the old snap ring. 3 If Lp2 - Lp1 is equal to or greater than 0.13, but less than 0.21 use a new snap ring two ranks higher in thickness than the old snap ring. 4 If Lp2 - Lp1 is equal to or greater than 0.21, but less than 0.29 use a new snap ring three ranks higher in thickness than the old snap ring. 5 If Lp2 - Lp1 is equal to or greater than 0.29, but less than 0.37 use a new snap ring four ranks higher in thickness than the old snap ring. 6 If Lp2 - Lp1 is equal to or greater than 0.37, but less than 0.45 use a new snap ring five ranks higher in thickness than the old snap ring. 7 Lp1 - Lp2 is less than 0.01 Same as (1) above. 8 If Lp1 - Lp2 is equal to or greater than 0.01, but less than 0.09 use a snap ring one rank lower in thickness than the old snap ring. 9 If Lp1 - Lp2 is equal to or greater than 0.09, but less than 0.17 use a new snap ring two ranks lower in thickness than the old snap ring. 10 If Lp1 - Lp2 is equal to or greater than 0.17, but less than 0.25 use a new snap ring three ranks lower in thickness than the old snap ring. 11 If Lp1 - Lp2 is equal to or greater than 0.25, but less than 0.33 use a new snap ring four ranks lower in thickness than the old snap ring. 12 If Lp1 - Lp2 is equal to or greater than 0.33, but less than 0.41 use a new snap ring five ranks lower in thickness than the old snap ring. Snap Ring Thickness Values T mm (in) (Snap ring thickness) 1.35 (0.0531) 1.43 (0.0563) 1.51 (0.0594) 1.59 (0.0626) 1.67 (0.0657) 1.75 (0.0689) 1.83 (0.0720) Top-down view of transmission assembly showing bolt/component locations marked with X symbols Cross-section diagram showing Gauge and Lp measurement location on primary pulley assembly

572.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
572.body: Assembly (Cont'd) AT-116 Assembly (Cont'd) Attach Tool to the secondary pulley and expand the groove to 52 - 53 mm (2.05 - 2.09 in). Do not expand the groove beyond this point. Fit the belt to the secondary pulley and engage the grooves of both pulleys. Fit the belt to the primary pulley while manoeuvring the assemblies into position on the side casing. Ensure the belt is fitted correctly as shown. Take care not to scratch the grooves. Ensure that all shims are located correctly. Remove the Tool from the secondary pulley. Remove the harness ties from the belt. Install the pulley retainer. Fit new bolts (with new O rings) Torque = 14.2 - 17.2 N·m (1.43 - 1.76 kg-m, 10 - 13 ft-lb) Fit the ratio sensor on to the side casing, locate to the pulley, and insert the sensor rod. The stepped end of the rod goes towards the casing. If the sensor (or primary pulley) has been replaced, fit Tool to valve mating surface as shown. Turn back fully the adjuster bolt. Turn in the adjuster bolt until it just makes contact with the tool. Tighten the lock nut and remove the tool. Pulley retainer bolt torque 14.2 - 17.2 N·m New bolts with new O rings Pulley retainer bolt torque 1.43 - 1.76 kg-m New bolts with new O rings Pulley retainer bolt torque 10 - 13 ft-lb New bolts with new O rings Secondary pulley groove expansion 52 - 53 mm Using Tool KV319-K0090 Secondary pulley groove expansion 2.05 - 2.09 in Using Tool KV319-K0090 Tool KV319-K0090 attached to secondary pulley to expand groove Belt fitted to both primary and secondary pulleys engaged in grooves, viewed from side casing Pulley retainer installed with new bolts and O rings, arrows indicating bolt locations Ratio sensor fitted to side casing with sensor rod inserted Tool KV319-K0140 fitted to valve mating surface showing adjuster bolt, bolt, locknut, and sensor locations

573.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
573.body: Assembly (Cont'd) AT-117 Assembly (Cont'd) Fit the park pawl assembly to the side casing. Ensure the guide is located in the hole correctly (shown by the arrow). Ensure the spring is not trapped between the pawl and guide plate. Apply liquid gasket to the mating faces and install the transmission casing to the side casing. Ensure mating faces are clean. Apply a continuous bead of liquid gasket, but do not over-apply. Tighten the bolts in the sequence shown. Fit the needle bearing. Transmission casing bolt torque 22 - 26 N·m Tighten in sequence shown Transmission casing bolt torque 2.2 - 2.7 kg-m Tighten in sequence shown Transmission casing bolt torque 16 - 20 ft-lb Tighten in sequence shown Park pawl assembly being fitted to side casing, arrow indicating guide location in hole Close-up showing Spring, Pawl, and Guide plate arrangement to ensure spring is not trapped Transmission casing being installed to side casing after applying liquid gasket to mating faces Bolt tightening sequence diagram showing numbered positions (1-10) for transmission casing bolts Hand fitting needle bearing into transmission assembly

574.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
574.body: Assembly (Cont'd) AT-118 Assembly (Cont'd) Install the reverse idle gear. Ensure the pin is positioned correctly in the transmission case. Assemble the synchroniser using clean ATF. Note the two wire springs are different thicknesses. Fit the thinner spring facing the transmission casing, and the thicker one facing the clutch housing. Align the spring gaps. Install the sleeve and hub assembly, shift fork and reverse gear. Fit the counter gear (small end facing the drive belt) to the shaft as shown. Install the counter gear assembly. Lift the synchroniser sleeve assembly up slightly for access. Lower the counter gear assembly into position. Lower the synchroniser. Ensure the counter shaft pin is correctly located in the transmission casing groove. Fit the circlip. Install the needle bearing. Install the engine brake valve assembly. Installing the reverse idle gear into the transmission case Synchroniser assembly showing thick spring and thin spring positions Fitting the counter gear (small end facing drive belt) to the shaft Installing the counter gear assembly with synchroniser sleeve lifted Installing the engine brake valve assembly

575.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
575.body: Assembly (Cont'd) AT-119 Ensure the brake pipe is firmly fixed into the side casing. Assembly (Cont'd) Fit and tighten the securing bolts. Torque = 3 - 4 N·m (0.31 - 0.41 kg-m, 2 - 3 ft-lb) Position the park rod and shift cam into the casing. Position the shift fork pin into the cam, and the park rod into the guide. Insert the shift arm through the casing and shift cam. Fit the retaining pin. Securing bolts torque (step 25) 3 - 4 N·m Securing bolts torque (step 25) 0.31 - 0.41 kg-m Securing bolts torque (step 25) 2 - 3 ft-lb Automatic transmission assembly procedure Engine brake pipe firmly fixed into the side casing Brake valve pipe with securing bolts being tightened Shift cam and park rod positioned into the casing Fork pin positioned into the shift cam, park rod into guide Shift arm inserted through casing and shift cam, retaining pin being fitted

576.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
576.body: Assembly (Cont'd) AT-120 Assembly (Cont'd) Fit the inner circlip to the shift arm. Fit the cam plate and pin. Fit the outer circlip. Secure the shift arm to shift cam using a new roll pin. Fit the final drive assembly in to position. Apply a thin coat of petroleum jelly to the needle bearing as shown. Do not use grease. If any of the following parts are replaced, adjust differential side bearing preload: Differential/final drive; Differential side bearing; Clutch housing; Transmission housing. Remove side bearing and shim. Reinstall side bearing without shim. Install final drive assembly. Install clutch housing on to transmission. Tighten bolts. Illustration showing fitting of inner circlip to shift arm, cam plate and pin, and outer circlip Illustration showing securing shift arm to shift cam using new roll pin, with 15 degree angle reference, and fitting final drive assembly Illustration showing application of petroleum jelly (not grease) to needle bearing

577.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
577.body: Assembly (Cont'd) AT-121 Assembly (Cont'd) Set dial gauge onto differential case as shown. Insert Tool and move up and down. Measure gauge deflection. Select correct shim from S.D.S. table. Install shim. Reassemble final drive/clutch housing. Tighten bolts. Measure turning torque. Turning torque = (new bearing) 2.0 - 7.8 N·m (20 - 80 kg-cm, 17 - 16 lb-in) Old bearing may be slightly less torque. Apply liquid gasket to mating surfaces. Fit the clutch housing on to the transmission. Turn the input shaft to assist gear mesh and housing location. Tighten the bolts in the sequence shown. Torque 23 - 26 N·m (2.4 - 2.7 kg-m, 17 - 20 ft-lb) Fit the control valve (with holder Tool) on to the transmission. Turning torque (new bearing) 2.0 - 7.8 N·m New bearing Turning torque (new bearing) 20 - 80 kg-cm New bearing Turning torque (new bearing) 17 - 16 lb-in New bearing Clutch housing bolt torque 23 - 26 N·m Tighten in sequence Clutch housing bolt torque 2.4 - 2.7 kg-m Tighten in sequence Clutch housing bolt torque 17 - 20 ft-lb Tighten in sequence Dial gauge set onto differential case with tool KV38105900 being inserted and moved up and down to measure gauge deflection Tool KV38105900 being used to measure turning torque on final drive/clutch housing assembly Clutch housing bolt tightening sequence showing numbered bolt positions on housing mating face Control valve with holder tool being fitted onto the transmission

578.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
578.body: Assembly (Cont'd) AT-122 Assembly (Cont'd) Ensure that the engine brake lever contacts the rear of ratio sensor adjusting bolt. Partially tighten the valve securing bolts. Support the valve body by hand, and remove the SST holder. Tighten the valve bolts. Connect the modulator rod. Fit Tool as shown. Slacken off the adjuster bolt. Push the adjuster plate in the direction of the arrow, and tighten the bolt to remove linkage free play. Remove the tool. Fit the cable. Fit the harness connector. Valve bolts torque 8.5 - 9.5 N·m Step 55 Valve bolts torque 0.87 - 0.97 kg-m Step 55 Valve bolts torque 6 - 7 ft-lb Step 55 Adjust bolt torque 3 - 4 N·m Step 59 Adjust bolt torque 0.31 - 0.41 kg-m Step 59 Adjust bolt torque 2 - 3 ft-lb Step 59 Engine brake lever location on valve body assembly Modulator plate KV319-K0030 installed, showing Adj.plate and Adj.bolt Pushing adjuster plate in direction of arrow to remove linkage free play Cable fitting on transmission assembly Harness connector and clip installation on valve body

579.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
579.body: Assembly (Cont'd) AT-123 Assembly (Cont'd) Fit the oil strainer. Use a new O ring, ensure it is installed correctly. Fit the oil pan. Ensure the magnet is seated in the concave area of pan, and is clean and free from foreign matter. Do not re-use the old gasket. Fit the inhibit switch. Partially tighten the securing bolts. Fit the selector linkage, spacers and spring pins. Tighten the lock nut while holding the linkage with 8 mm dia. pin (refer to disassembly procedure). Align the inhibit switch using a 4mm. dia pin through the linkage as shown. Tighten the switch securing nuts. Automatic transmission assembly procedure. A/T specific. Oil strainer with O-ring callout being fitted to transmission Oil strainer installed view from above showing oil pan area Oil pan being fitted to transmission Inhibit switch and selector linkage fitting with arrows indicating securing bolts Inhibit switch alignment using 4mm dia pin through linkage, showing Pin, Linkage, Inhibit switch and Adjust callouts

580.title: Assembly (Cont'd)
580.body: Assembly (Cont'd) AT-124 Assembly (Cont'd) Position the pitot tube as shown. Fit a new oil pump gasket in to position. Fit a new O ring to the oil pump. Fit the oil pump to the casing. Tighten the bolts evenly. Torque = 9.5 - 10.5 N·m (0.97 - 1.07 kg-m, 7 - 8 ft-lb) Install the oil pump drive shaft. Fit the oil cooler check valve and pipes. Use new sealing washers. Do not re-use old ones. Torque: Inlet = 32 - 37 N·m (3.3 - 3.8 kg-m, 24 - 28 ft-lb), Outlet = 35 - 43 N·m (3.6 - 4.4 kg-m, 26 - 32 ft-lb) When fitting the outlet pipe, tighten the joint nut first, then the flare nut. When fitting the inlet pipe, ensure that there is more than 3 mm clearance between the pipe and the shift connecting rod, when the rod is in "N" position. Oil pump bolt torque 9.5 - 10.5 N·m Tighten evenly Oil pump bolt torque 0.97 - 1.07 kg-m Tighten evenly Oil pump bolt torque 7 - 8 ft-lb Tighten evenly Oil cooler check valve/pipe torque - Inlet 32 - 37 N·m Use new sealing washers Oil cooler check valve/pipe torque - Inlet 3.3 - 3.8 kg-m Use new sealing washers Oil cooler check valve/pipe torque - Inlet 24 - 28 ft-lb Use new sealing washers Oil cooler check valve/pipe torque - Outlet 35 - 43 N·m Use new sealing washers Oil cooler check valve/pipe torque - Outlet 3.6 - 4.4 kg-m Use new sealing washers Oil cooler check valve/pipe torque - Outlet 26 - 32 ft-lb Use new sealing washers Inlet pipe clearance from shift connecting rod in N position more than 3 mm Rod in N position Automatic Transmission (AT) assembly procedure. Oil cooler pipe inlet clearance check references shift connecting rod in N position. Oil pump assembly showing gasket and pitot tube positioning Oil pump fitted to casing showing bolt tightening arrows
