585.title: PRECAUTIONS
585.body: PRECAUTIONS ST-2 Precautions Before disassembly, thoroughly clean the outside of the unit. Disassembly should be done in a clean work area. It is important to prevent the internal parts from becoming contaminated by dirt or other foreign matter. When disassembling parts, be sure to place them in order on a part rack so they can be reinstalled in their proper positions. Use nylon cloths or paper towels to clean the parts; common shop rags can leave lint that might interfere with their operation. Before inspection or reassembly, carefully clean all parts with a general purpose, non-flammable solvent. Before assembly, apply a coat of recommended A.T.F.* to hydraulic parts. Vaseline may be applied to O-rings and seals. Do not use any grease. *: Automatic transmission fluid Replace all gaskets, seals and O-rings. Avoid damaging O-rings, seals and gaskets during installation. Perform functional tests whenever designated. *: Automatic transmission fluid *: Automatic transmission fluid

586.title: PREPARATION — SPECIAL SERVICE TOOLS
586.body: PREPARATION — SPECIAL SERVICE TOOLS ST-3 Special Service Tools Tool number Tool name Description KV48100700 Torque adapter Measuring pinion rotating torque ST27180001 Steering wheel puller Removing and installing steering wheel HT72520000 Ball joint remover Removing ball joint ST27091000 Pressure gauge Measuring oil pressure KV48102500 Pressure gauge adapter Measuring oil pressure ST3127S000 Set: (1) GG91030000 Torque wrench, (2) HT62940000 Socket adapter, (3) HT62900000 Socket adapter Measuring turning torque Torque adapter KV48100700 — cylindrical socket shape Steering wheel puller ST27180001 Ball joint remover HT72520000 Pressure gauge ST27091000 with connections to oil pump outlet, control valve, and shut-off valve Pressure gauge adapter KV48102500 — various adapter fittings ST3127S000 set: (1) Torque wrench GG91030000, (2) Socket adapter HT62940000, (3) Socket adapter HT62900000

587.title: ON-VEHICLE INSPECTION — Steering Wheel
587.body: ON-VEHICLE INSPECTION — Steering Wheel ST-4 The vehicle is fitted with either a 2-spoke or 3-spoke steering wheel; the service procedures for each type is identical. Checking Steering Wheel Play With wheels in a straight-ahead position, check steering wheel play. Steering wheel play: 35 mm (1.38 in) or less If it is not within specification, check steering gear assembly when front suspension and axle, steering gear assembly and steering column are mounted correctly. Steering wheel play 35 (1.38) or less mm (in) Wheels in straight-ahead position Check that wheel alignment is correct – refer to FA section for full details of wheel alignment procedure. Wheel Alignment Specifications LOADING FRONT 2 REAR 1 — All FRONT 1 REAR 0 — 1 liter FRONT 1 REAR 0 — 1.3 liter FRONT 0 REAR 0 — 1.0 liter FRONT 0 REAR 0 — 1.3 liter Camber angle 0.0° 0.2° ± 0.75° 0.22° ± 0.75° 0.32° ± 0.75° 0.38° ± 0.75° Positive caster angle 2.9° 2.2° ± 0.75° 2.23° ± 0.75° 2.27° ± 0.75° 2.32° ± 0.75° Toe-in mm (in) 0(0) 0.5 ± 1 (0.020 ± 0.039) 0.5 ± 1 (0.020 ± 0.039) 0.5 ± 1 (0.020 ± 0.039) 0.5 ± 1 (0.020 ± 0.039) Toe angle 0.0° 0.09° ± 0.18° 0.09° ± 0.18° 0.09° ± 0.18° 0.09° ± 0.18° Kingpin angle 13.1° 12.82° ± 0.75° 12.76° ± 0.75° 12.62° ± 0.75° 12.53° ± 0.75° Track mm (in) 1,365 (53.74) 1,363 (53.66) 1,363 (53.66) 1,361 (53.58) 1,360 (53.54) Wheel center mm (in) 40 (1.57) 27 (1.06) 25 (0.98) 19 (0.75) 16 (0.63) Verify that the steering gear is centered before removing the steering wheel. Checking Neutral Position on Steering Wheel Check that the steering wheel is in the neutral position when driving straight ahead. If it is not in the neutral position, remove the steering wheel and reinstall it correctly. If the neutral position is between two serrated teeth, loosen tie-rod lock nut and move tie-rod in the opposite direction by the same amount on both left and right sides to compensate for error in the neutral position. Wheel alignment specifications vary by engine size (1.0 liter and 1.3 liter) and loading condition (FRONT 2 REAR 1, FRONT 1 REAR 0, FRONT 0 REAR 0). FRONT 2 REAR 1 loading applies to all models. Illustration showing a steering wheel with a dial gauge tool attached to measure steering wheel play Two steering wheels side-by-side showing O.K. (correct neutral position) and N.G. (incorrect neutral position) Illustration showing a wheel/tire with alignment checking equipment attached

588.title: On-Vehicle Inspection — Steering Wheel (Cont'd)
588.body:
On-Vehicle Inspection — Steering Wheel (Cont'd) ST-5 Front Wheel Turning Angle Rotate steering wheel all the way right and left; measure turning angle. If it is not within specification, check rack stroke. Front Wheel Turning Angle Specifications Tire 155/70 R13 175/60 R13 Rack stroke mm (in) 64.6 (2.543) 60.6 (2.386) Inner Wheel (Unladen) 37° - 41° 34° - 38° Outer Wheel (Unladen) 31° - 35° 29° - 33° Checking Gear Housing Movement Check the movement of steering gear housing during stationary steering on a dry paved surface.
Apply a force of 49 N (5 kg, 11 lb) to steering wheel to check the gear housing movement.
Turn off ignition key while checking.
Movement of gear housing: ±2 mm (±0.08 in) or less If movement exceeds the limit, replace mount insulator after confirming proper installation of gear housing clamps. Movement of gear housing ±2 mm (±0.08 in) or less Diagram showing inner wheel (A) and outer wheel (B) turning angle measurement, with front direction indicated Illustration showing dial gauge placed on steering gear to measure gear housing movement

589.title: ON-VEHICLE INSPECTION — Power Steering
589.body:
ON-VEHICLE INSPECTION — Power Steering ST-6 Check the power steering drive belt is correctly tensioned. Refer to MA section for full details of belt tensioning procedures and specifications. Fluid level should be checked when fluid is cold. CAUTION:
• Do not overfill.
• Recommended fluid is Automatic Transmission Fluid "DEXRON™" type. Checking Steering Wheel Turning Force Park vehicle on a level, dry surface and set parking brake. Start engine. Bring power steering fluid up to adequate operating temperature. [Make sure temperature of fluid is approximately 60 to 80°C (140 to 176°F).] Tires need to be inflated to normal pressure. Check steering wheel turning force when steering wheel has been turned 360° from neutral position. Steering wheel turning force: 44.1 N (4.5 kg, 9.9 lb) or less If steering wheel turning force is out of specifications, check rack sliding force to detect condition of steering gear assembly. Disconnect steering column lower joint and knuckle arms from the gear. Start and run engine at idle to make sure steering fluid has reached normal operating temperature. While pulling tie-rod slowly from the neutral position, make sure rack sliding force is within specification. Rack sliding force: 147 N (15 kg, 33 lb) or less If rack sliding force is not within specification, replace steering gear assembly. Steering wheel turning force (after turning 360° from neutral) 44.1 N (4.5 kg, 9.9 lb) or less Engine running, fluid at 60–80°C (140–176°F), tires at normal pressure Rack sliding force 147 N (15 kg, 33 lb) or less Engine at idle, steering column lower joint and knuckle arms disconnected, fluid at operating temperature Power steering fluid operating temperature 60 to 80 °C (140 to 176°F) Drive belt routing for power steering pump Water pump Power steering pump Front of vehicle Idler Crankshaft Power steering variant only. Recommended fluid: ATF DEXRON™ type. Drive belt routing diagram showing water pump, power steering pump, idler, and crankshaft pulleys with front-of-vehicle direction arrow Power steering fluid reservoir cap/dipstick being checked Steering wheel with spring scale attached, showing measurement after turning 360° from neutral position Rack and tie-rod assembly with tool attached for measuring rack sliding force

590.title: ON-VEHICLE INSPECTION — Power steering (Cont'd)
590.body: ON-VEHICLE INSPECTION — Power steering (Cont'd) ST-7 Power steering system components in engine bay Reservoir Pressure Switch Pump Steering Rack Check all hoses for damage, cracks and deterioration. Check clamps, union bolts and fixings for improper attachment or loose connection. Checking Fluid Leakage Run engine at idle speed until temperature of fluid in oil reservoir rises to 60 to 80°C (140 to 176° F). Turn steering wheel fully clockwise and anti-clockwise several times. Hold steering wheel at each "lock" position for five seconds and carefully check all fluid lines for signs of seepage. CAUTION: Do not hold the steering wheel in a locked position for more than 15 seconds at a time. If fluid seepage at connectors is evident, check attachment is correct and free from contamination; loosen flare nut and retighten. Do not overtighten connector as this can damage O-ring, washer and connector. If fluid seepage is evident from cracks or cuts in the hoses or fluid lines; replace defective section. Bleeding Hydraulic System Raise front end of vehicle until wheels are clear of the ground. Fill oil reservoir with recommended fluid, and quickly turn steering wheel several times in both directions with the engine switched off. Add extra fluid if the level drops and continue operation until the fluid level settles at the correct height in the reservoir. Start engine and run at idle speed; conduct procedure specified at step 2 above, until the fluid level settles and the bubbles in the tank disappear. Engine bay view showing power steering components: Reservoir, Pressure Switch, Pump, and Steering Rack

591.title: ON-VEHICLE INSPECTION — Power Steering (Cont'd)
591.body:
ON-VEHICLE INSPECTION — Power Steering (Cont'd) ST-8 Incomplete air bleeding will cause the following to occur. When this happens, bleed air again.
a. Generation of air bubbles in reservoir tank
b. Generation of clicking noise in oil pump
c. Excessive buzzing in oil pump
While the vehicle is stationary or while moving the steering wheel slowly, fluid noise may occur in the valve or oil pump. This noise will not affect performance or durability of the system. Ensure drive-belt to power steering pulley is correctly tensioned (refer to MA section for tensioning procedures). Ensure tires are inflated to the correct pressure. Checking Hydraulic System Fit oil pressure gauge in fluid line between the pump and steering gear using the relevant adapters. Open the shut-off valve and bleed air from the system as previously instructed. Run engine at idle speed until temperature of fluid in reservoir rises to 60 to 80°C (140 to 176° F). Check the pressure level when the steering wheel is turned fully clockwise and anti-clockwise with the engine set at idle speed. Ensure the pressure gauge shut-off valve is fully opened when the engine is running. If the engine is allowed to run with the shut-off valve closed, oil pressure in the oil pump will increase to relief pressure, resulting in an abnormal rise in oil temperature. CAUTION: Do not hold the steering wheel in a locked position for more than 15 seconds. If the oil pressure is below the standard, slowly close the shut-off valve while checking the change in pressure shown on the gauge.
• If the pressure becomes standard, the steering gear is damaged.
• If the pressure remains low, the pump is damaged. CAUTION: Do not close shut-off valve for more than 15 seconds. If the oil pressure is higher than standard pressure, the pump is damaged. After checking hydraulic system, remove pressure gauge and add fluid to the reservoir as necessary. Ensure that all the air is completely bled out of the system using the procedure stated previously. Oil pump maximum standard pressure 5,786 - 6,375 kPa Steering wheel turned fully clockwise/anti-clockwise, engine at idle Oil pump maximum standard pressure 57.9 - 63.7 bar Steering wheel turned fully clockwise/anti-clockwise, engine at idle Oil pump maximum standard pressure 59 - 65 kg/cm² Steering wheel turned fully clockwise/anti-clockwise, engine at idle Oil pump maximum standard pressure 839 - 924 psi Steering wheel turned fully clockwise/anti-clockwise, engine at idle Illustration showing KV481 02500 adapter fittings and ST2700/000 tool installed on the power steering hydraulic line for pressure gauge fitting. Diagram of the power steering hydraulic circuit showing pump, gear, tank, high-pressure hose, low-pressure hose, direction of oil flow, and ST27091000 pressure gauge tool.

592.title: STEERING WHEEL AND STEERING COLUMN
592.body: STEERING WHEEL AND STEERING COLUMN ST-9 Horn pad Steering wheel Upper column cover Lower column cover Ignition switch grommet Steering column assembly Boot Steering wheel nut torque 29 - 39 N·m Steering wheel nut torque 3.0 - 4.0 kg·m Steering wheel nut torque 22 - 29 ft·lb Steering column upper mounting bolt torque 14.7 - 18.6 N·m upper position Steering column upper mounting bolt torque 1.5 - 1.9 kg·m upper position Steering column upper mounting bolt torque 10.8 - 13.7 ft·lb upper position Steering column lower mounting bolt torque 14.7 - 18.6 N·m lower position Steering column lower mounting bolt torque 1.5 - 1.9 kg·m lower position Steering column lower mounting bolt torque 10.8 - 13.7 ft·lb lower position Boot clamp bolt torque 3.9 - 4.9 N·m Boot clamp bolt torque 0.4 - 0.5 kg·m Boot clamp bolt torque 2.9 - 2.4 ft·lb Steering Wheel Removal Pull out horn pad. Remove horn connections. Remove steering wheel nut. Disconnect horn earth terminal from top face of steering wheel. Remove steering wheel with puller. Exploded view of steering wheel and steering column assembly showing all components with torque specifications Illustration showing removal of horn pad from steering wheel Illustration showing removal of steering wheel using a puller tool

593.title: STEERING WHEEL AND STEERING COLUMN
593.body:
STEERING WHEEL AND STEERING COLUMN ST-10 Before installing steering wheel, apply grease (DAIKYO DYNAMIC or equivalent) to the slip ring face and cancel pin to prevent noise. Steering Wheel Installation Fit steering wheel onto steering column after ensuring wheel alignment is correct and steering gear is centered. Check that steering wheel is in neutral position and tighten steering wheel nut to the specified torque. CAUTION:
• Do not hit steering wheel when fitting to column.
• Check that steering wheel is in neutral position when driving straight ahead and wheel play is less than 35 mm.
• Attach horn connections to column and horn pad.
• Push fit horn pad in place and check operation. Steering Column Removal Remove shelf from dashboard beneath steering wheel to ease access to the steering column. Remove screws (4) from steering column cover and remove upper and lower column covers. Remove nuts securing steering column boot to body and slacken boot retaining clip. Pull back boot for access to lower joint (alternatively, remove bolts and retaining plate from pinion dust boot and peel back dust boot to gain access to the lower steering joint from the engine bay). Turn steering column to give best access to upper tightening bolt of lower joint and remove bolt. Do not forcibly spread flange of lower joint when disassembling. Remove windscreen wiper and lighting switches from the combination switch base by squeezing top and bottom fixing wings and pulling switch away from base. Remove connectors from switches. Remove steering column retaining bolts from bracket and withdraw steering column. Steering wheel installation showing slip ring face and cancel pin area, top and bottom views Steering column lower joint area showing lower joint location

594.title: Steering Wheel and Steering Column — Steering Column (Cont'd) Installation
594.body: Steering Wheel and Steering Column — Steering Column (Cont'd) Installation ST-11 Steering Column Installation Attach column shaft to lower joint, making sure the cutout portion of the shaft faces the lower joint slit and tightening bolt. Fit lower joint bolt but do not tighten yet. Fit steering column securing nuts and bolts; firstly finger-tighten lower bracket and clamp retaining bolts, then tighten to the specified torque. Do not apply undue stress to the steering column. Tighten lower joint bolt to specified torque. Tighten nuts securing steering column boot to body and tighten boot retaining clip. Replace combination switches, steering wheel, horn pad, column covers and dashboard shelf. After installing steering column, turn steering wheel to ensure it moves smoothly and that the number of turns from the straight forward position to left and right locks are equal. Be sure that the steering wheel is in a neutral position when driving straight ahead. Illustration showing column shaft cutout portion alignment with lower joint slit and tightening bolt

595.title: STEERING WHEEL AND STEERING COLUMN — Disassembly and Assembly
595.body: STEERING WHEEL AND STEERING COLUMN — Disassembly and Assembly ST-12 Snapring Do not disassemble Washer O-ring Combination switch Assembly Jacket tube assembly Do not disassemble Boot Column shaft assembly Lower joint Combination switch assembly mounting torque 3.4 - 4.4 N·m Combination switch assembly mounting torque 0.35 - 0.45 Kg-m Combination switch assembly mounting torque 2.5 - 3.3 ft-lb Lower joint bolt torque (lower) 24 - 29 N·m Lower joint bolt torque (lower) 2.4 - 3.0 Kg-m Lower joint bolt torque (lower) 17 - 22 ft-lb Lower joint bolt torque (upper) 24 - 29 N·m Lower joint bolt torque (upper) 2.4 - 3.0 Kg-m Lower joint bolt torque (upper) 17 - 22 ft-lb Combination Switch Base Removal To remove combination switch base, remove base attaching screw, then turn switch base while pressing on it to prise base protrusion guide from location hole on shaft. Combination switch base removal detail showing retaining strap, hole, and protrusion guide. Combination switch base retaining strap Hole Protrusion guide Exploded diagram of steering column assembly showing jacket tube assembly, snapring, washer, O-ring, combination switch assembly, boot, column shaft assembly, lower joint, and torque values. Two illustrations showing combination switch base retaining strap, hole, and protrusion guide for removal procedure.

596.title: STEERING WHEEL AND STEERING COLUMN — Disassembly and Assembly (Cont'd)
596.body: STEERING WHEEL AND STEERING COLUMN — Disassembly and Assembly (Cont'd) ST-13 Snap Ring Installation on Upper Shaft Ensure that rounded surface of snap ring faces toward bearing when snap ring is installed. Install snap ring on upper shaft with a suitable tool. Steering Lock a. Break self-shear type screws with a drill or other appropriate tool. b. Install self-shear type screws and then cut off self-shear type screw heads. After installing steering column, check tilt mechanism operation (Vehicles with tilt system). CAUTION: Do not attempt to disassemble jacket tube assembly; if defective, replace as a unit. CAUTION: Carry out steering column assembly/disassembly in a clean dust-free environment. Steering column measurement (upper) 10 mm (0.4 in) See diagram UST022 Steering column measurement (lower) 20 mm (0.8 in) See diagram UST022 Tilt mechanism check applies only to vehicles equipped with tilt system. Illustration showing installation of snap ring on upper shaft using a suitable tool (SST) Illustration showing self-shear type screw location on steering lock assembly, being broken with a drill Illustration showing installation of self-shear type screw and cutting off the screw head Steering column assembly with dimensional measurements: 10 mm (0.4 in) and 20 mm (0.8 in). Unit: mm (in).

597.title: STEERING WHEEL AND STEERING COLUMN
597.body: STEERING WHEEL AND STEERING COLUMN ST-14 Inspection When steering wheel can not be rotated smoothly, check the steering column for the following matters and replace damaged parts. a. Check column bearings for damage or unevenness. Lubricate with recommended multi-purpose grease or replace steering column as an assembly, if necessary. b. Check jacket tube for deformation or breakage. Replace if necessary. When the vehicle is involved in a light collision, check column length "L". If it is not within specification, replace steering column as an assembly. Column length "L" — RHD 555.9 - 558.1 mm (21.89 - 21.97 in) RHD Column length "L" — LHD 545.9 - 548.1 mm (21.49 - 21.58 in) LHD Column length 'L' specification differs between RHD (555.9 - 558.1 mm) and LHD (545.9 - 548.1 mm) variants. Steering column assembly showing column length 'L' measurement

598.title: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE
598.body: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE ST-15 Gear and linkage assembly Rack mounting insulator Gear housing mounting bracket Cotter pin Tie rod retaining nut torque (upper left fastener) 24 - 29 N·m Tie rod retaining nut torque (upper left fastener) 2.4 - 3.0 kg-m Tie rod retaining nut torque (upper left fastener) 17 - 22 ft-lb Tie rod retaining nut torque (upper right fastener) 24 - 29 N·m Tie rod retaining nut torque (upper right fastener) 2.4 - 3.0 kg-m Tie rod retaining nut torque (upper right fastener) 17 - 22 ft-lb Tie rod end castle nut torque 29 - 39 N·m Tie rod end castle nut torque 3.0 - 4.0 kg-m Tie rod end castle nut torque 22 - 29 ft-lb Gear housing mounting bracket bolt torque 73 - 97 N·m Gear housing mounting bracket bolt torque 7.4 - 9.9 kg-m Gear housing mounting bracket bolt torque 54 - 72 ft-lb Removal Remove front wheels. Remove split pins and retaining nuts from tie rods. Detach tie-rod outer sockets from knuckle arms using ball joint remover. Remove nuts and retaining plates securing lower joint dust boot to body and peel back boot for access to lower joint tightening bolts. Exploded view of manual steering gear and linkage assembly showing torque values for fasteners, gear and linkage assembly, rack mounting insulator, gear housing mounting bracket, and cotter pin locations. Photo showing tie-rod outer socket removal from knuckle arm using ball joint remover tool HT72520000. Photo showing lower joint dust boot area with retaining plate, boot retaining strap, and lower joint dust boot callouts.

599.title: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Removal (Cont'd)
599.body: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Removal (Cont'd) ST-16 Removal (Cont'd) Slacken lower joint tightening bolts and remove lower joint from steering gear pinion. Ensure joint and pinion are marked for correct alignment during re-assembly. CAUTION: Do not forcibly spread flange of lower joint when disassembling. Support transmission housing with engine jack and remove engine centre member and rear engine mounting (see EM section for details). Disconnect transmission linkage (support rod and control rod for manual transmission vehicles and transmission cable and mounting bracket for N-CVT vehicles) and place aside. Remove steering gear mounting bolts. Slacken dust boot retaining strap and remove dust boot from pinion. For LHD vehicles, rotate gear and displace through right-hand wheel arch until left-hand tie rod is clear to drop out through bottom of engine bay, then remove assembly. For RHD vehicles, rotate gear and displace through left-hand wheel arch until right-hand tie-rod is clear to drop out through bottom of engine bay, then remove assembly. CAUTION: Do not forcibly spread flange of lower joint when disassembling. Step 7 distinguishes between M/T (support rod and control rod) and N-CVT (transmission cable and mounting bracket) vehicles. Step 10 distinguishes between LHD and RHD vehicles for gear removal direction. Lower joint tightening bolts on steering gear pinion — shows bolts to slacken Engine jack supporting transmission housing with engine centre member and rear engine mounting visible M/T transmission linkage showing control rod and support rod Steering gear mounting bolts and dust boot retaining strap on pinion Steering gear being displaced through wheel arch for removal

600.title: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE
600.body: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE ST-17 Installation Installation is basically the reverse procedure to removal. Ensure that there are no cracks or splits in any of the dust boots prior to fitting. Ensure that the rack mounting clamps are oriented correctly with the oval holes at the top. When replacing steering gear, ensure correct rack length is selected. Short travel is for vehicles fitted with wide tyres (175/60-13). Standard travel rack is for all other vehicles. Short travel is for vehicles fitted with wide tyres (175/60-13) Standard travel rack is for all other vehicles. When fitting rack mounting bolts, initially install bolts 1 and 2 so that the mating parts are not clamped and then install bolts 3 and 4 and tighten in the sequence shown. When fitting lower joint ensure upper slit is aligned with steering column cut out and lower slit aligns with plastic guide plate. Ensure upper bolt fits through cut-out portion of column and the lower bolt fits through the cut-out in the plastic guide on the pinion. Fit both bolts loosely. Then tighten lower bolt to specified torque first, followed by upper bolt. CAUTION: After installing, turn the steering wheel to make sure it moves smoothly and that the number of turns from the straight forward position to left and right locks are equal. Be sure that the steering wheel is in a neutral position when driving straight ahead. Short travel rack is for vehicles fitted with wide tyres (175/60-13). Standard travel rack is for all other vehicles. Rack mounting bolt tightening sequence diagram showing bolts numbered 1-4 with temporary tightening (bolts 1 and 2) and final tightening (bolts 3 and 4) sequence indicated Lower joint installation diagram showing column, cut out, lower joint, guide plate, and section A-A view illustrating alignment of upper slit with steering column cut out and lower slit with plastic guide plate

601.title: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE
601.body: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE ST-18 Steering gear assembly Do not disassemble Boot band Dust boot Boot band Outer tie-rod Outer tie-rod torque 37 - 46 N·m Outer tie-rod torque 3.8 - 4.7 kg-m Outer tie-rod torque 27.3 - 33.9 ft-lb Use soft jaws when holding steering gear housing. Handle gear housing carefully. Do not grip cylinder in a vise. Disassembly Remove outer tie-rod. Remove dust boot. Thoroughly clean all parts in cleaning solvent or automatic transmission fluid "Dextron™" type, and blow dry with compressed air, if available. Exploded view of manual steering gear and linkage components including steering gear assembly, boot bands, dust boot, and outer tie-rod with torque specification Illustration showing how to hold steering gear housing with soft jaws during disassembly

602.title: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Inspection (Cont'd)
602.body: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Inspection (Cont'd) ST-19 Steering Gear Assembly Inspection Check for smooth operation through a full stroke. Check rack and tie-rod for cracks, deformation or other damage. Check pinion rotating torque. Check rack sliding force. Pinion rotating torque 0.7 - 0.9 N·m Pinion rotating torque 7 - 9 kg-cm Pinion rotating torque 6.1 - 7.8 in-lb Rack sliding force 250 or less N Rack sliding force 25.5 or less kg Rack sliding force 56 or less lb Tie-Rod Outer and Inner Socket Inspection Check ball joint for swinging force. Check ball joint for rotating torque. Check ball joint for axial end play. Ensure the ball joint boot is not split or cracked and that it fits correctly. Replace if necessary. Tie-rod outer ball joint swinging force 6.9 - 63.7 N Tie-rod outer ball joint swinging force 0.7 - 6.5 kg Tie-rod outer ball joint swinging force 1.5 - 14.3 lb Tie-rod inner ball joint swinging force 4.9 - 83.4 N Tie-rod inner ball joint swinging force 0.5 - 8.5 kg Tie-rod inner ball joint swinging force 1.1 - 18.7 lb Tie-rod outer ball joint rotating torque 0.3 - 2.9 N·m Tie-rod outer ball joint rotating torque 3 - 30 kg-cm Tie-rod outer ball joint rotating torque 2.6 - 26.0 in-lb Tie-rod outer ball joint axial end play 0.5 or less mm Tie-rod outer ball joint axial end play 0.020 or less in Tie-rod inner ball joint axial end play 0 mm Tie-rod inner ball joint axial end play 0 in Pinion rotating torque check using tool KV48100700 and ST3127S000 on steering gear assembly Rack sliding force check using a slide hammer / force gauge tool on rack Tie-rod ball joint swinging force check with measuring point indicated Tie-rod outer ball joint rotating torque check using ST3127S000 Ball joint axial end play check with axial end play callout

603.title: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Assembly
603.body: MANUAL STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Assembly ST-20 Assembly Tighten outer socket lock nut. Measure rack stroke. Before installing boot, coat the contact surfaces between boot and tie-rod with grease. Tie-rod length "L" 183.6 mm (7.23 in) After tightening outer socket lock nut Rack stroke "L" — Short track 60.6 mm (2.386 in) Short track Rack stroke "L" — Standard track 64.6 mm (2.543 in) Standard track Rack stroke differs between short track and standard track variants: Short track = 60.6 mm, Standard track = 64.6 mm. Tie-rod assembly showing length dimension L Rack and pinion assembly showing rack stroke dimension L on both sides Boot and tie-rod assembly showing grease application area

604.title: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE
604.body: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE ST-21 Cotter pin Replace after removal Rack mounting insulator Gear housing mounting bracket Gear and linkage assembly Tie rod end retaining nut torque 29 - 39 (3.0 - 4.0, 22 - 29) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Upper fastener torque (top center) 24 - 29 (2.4 - 3.0, 17 - 22) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Right side fastener torque 24 - 29 (2.4 - 3.0, 17 - 22) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Gear housing mounting bracket bolt torque 73 - 97 (7.4 - 9.9, 54 - 72) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Removal Remove front wheels. Remove split pins and retaining nuts from tie rods. Detach tie-rod outer sockets from knuckle arms using ball joint remover. Remove Carbon Canister (from LHD vehicles only) to improve accessability to lower joint and pinion. Note connections of hoses to cannister stub pipes for installation. Remove Carbon Canister bracket. Step 4 applies to LHD vehicles only. Carbon Canister removal required only on Left Hand Drive vehicles to improve access to lower joint and pinion. Exploded diagram of power steering gear and linkage assembly showing torque values for various fasteners, components including rack mounting insulator, gear housing mounting bracket, cotter pin, and gear and linkage assembly Ball joint remover tool HT72520000 being used to detach tie-rod outer socket from knuckle arm Carbon canister component photograph

605.title: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Removal (Cont'd)
605.body: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Removal (Cont'd) ST-22 Removal (Cont'd) Remove nuts and retaining plates securing lower joint dust boot to body and peel back boot for access to lower joint tightening bolts. Remove low pressure (1) and high pressure (2) connections from rack and drain system. Slacken lower joint tightening bolts and remove lower joint from steering gear pinion. Do not forcibly spread flange of lower joint when disassembling. Support transmission housing with engine jack and remove engine centre member and rear engine mounting (see EM section for details). Disconnect transmission linkage (support rod and control rod for manual transmission vehicles and transmission cable and mounting bracket for N-CVT vehicles) and place aside. Step 9 distinguishes between manual transmission vehicles (support rod and control rod) and N-CVT vehicles (transmission cable and mounting bracket). Lower joint area showing dust boot and retaining plates securing lower joint dust boot to body Pinion area showing low pressure (1) and high pressure (2) connections on rack and drain system Lower joint tightening bolts location on steering gear pinion Engine jack supporting transmission housing with engine centre member and rear engine mounting visible N-CVT transmission cable and bracket with lock nuts shown on rack assembly

606.title: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Removal (Cont'd) / Installation
606.body:
POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Removal (Cont'd) / Installation ST-23 Removal (Cont'd) Remove steering gear mounting bolts. Slacken dust boot retaining strap and remove dust boot from pinion. For LHD vehicles, rotate gear and displace through right-hand wheel arch until left-hand tie rod is clear to drop out through bottom of engine bay, then remove assembly.

For RHD vehicles, rotate gear and displace through left-hand wheel arch until right-hand tie rod is clear to drop out through bottom of engine bay, then remove assembly. Installation Installation is basically the reverse procedure to removal. Ensure there are no cracks or splits in any of the dust boots; all fluid lines and glands are secure and free from leaks prior to installation. Ensure that the correct mounting clamps and rubbers are used for the power steering rack and they are oriented correctly with the oval holes located at the top. When replacing steering gear, ensure correct rack length is selected. Short travel is for vehicles fitted with wide tyres (175/60-13). Standard length rack is used for all other vehicles. When fitting rack mounting bolts, initially install bolts 1 and 2 so that the mating parts are not clamped and then install bolts 3 and 4 and tighten in the sequence shown. Ensure that the steering gear is correctly aligned in housing brackets, and that transfer tubes do not foul bulkhead or subframe. Refit high and low pressure fluid lines. Ensure high pressure line gland is tightened to the recommended torque. Ensure low pressure line is fitted up to flare nut before tightening hose retaining clip. High pressure line 15 - 25 N·m High pressure line 1.5 - 2.5 kg·m High pressure line 14 - 19 ft·lb Low pressure line retaining clip 3.5 - 4.6 N·m Low pressure line retaining clip 0.36 - 0.47 kg·m Low pressure line retaining clip 2.6 - 3.4 ft·lb CAUTION:
• Excessive tightening can damage threads or connector O-ring.
• The O-ring in low pressure pipe connector is larger than for high-pressure connector. Take care to install the correct O-ring. LHD vehicles: displace gear through right-hand wheel arch. RHD vehicles: displace gear through left-hand wheel arch. Short travel rack for vehicles fitted with wide tyres (175/60-13); standard length rack for all other vehicles. Photo showing boot retaining strap and mounting bolts on steering gear assembly Photo showing steering gear being displaced through wheel arch for removal Diagram showing rack mounting bolt tightening sequence — temporary tightening bolts 1 and 2, final tightening bolts 3 and 4, sequence: 1→2→3→4→1→2 Diagram showing high pressure line and low pressure line retaining clip with torque values

607.title: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Installation (Cont'd)
607.body: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Installation (Cont'd) ST-24 Installation (Cont'd) When fitting lower joint ensure upper slit is aligned with steering column cut-out and lower slit aligns with plastic guide plate. Ensure upper bolt fits through cut-out portion of column and the lower bolt fits through the cut-out in the plastic guide on the pinion. Fit both bolts loosely. Then tighten lower bolt to specified torque first, followed by upper bolt. Tighten nuts on tie-rod outer socket and knuckle arms to 29 to 39 N·m (3 to 4 kg-m, 22 to 29 ft-lb) and fit new split pin. Fill reservoir with the recommended fluid and turn steering wheel several times in both directions with the engine off. Add fluid if level drops and repeat procedure until the fluid level settles. Start engine and run at idle speed; top up fluid level if required and turn steering wheel backwards and forwards until fluid level settles and bubbles disappear. Check system to ensure all connections and lines are free from leaks. After installation turn the steering wheel to make sure it moves smoothly and that the number of turns from the straight forward position to left and right locks are equal. Be sure that the steering wheel is in a neutral position when driving straight ahead. Tie-rod outer socket and knuckle arm nut torque 29 to 39 N·m Tie-rod outer socket and knuckle arm nut torque 3 to 4 kg-m Tie-rod outer socket and knuckle arm nut torque 22 to 29 ft-lb Lower joint installation showing column cut-out alignment, lower joint, guide plate, and section A-A view Tie-rod outer socket and knuckle arm assembly showing pin hole, split pin, and torque specification of 29-39 N·m (3-4 kg-m, 22-29 ft-lb) Power steering reservoir being filled with fluid

608.title: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE
608.body: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE ST-25 Steering gear assembly Do not disassemble Boot band Outer tie-rod Dust boot Boot band Outer tie-rod torque 38 - 47 N·m Outer tie-rod torque 3.9 - 4.8 Kg·m Outer tie-rod torque 28 - 35 ft-lb Use soft jaws when holding steering gear housing. Handle gear housing carefully. Do not grip cylinder in a vise. Disassembly Remove outer tie-rod. Remove dust boot. Thoroughly clean all parts in cleaning solvent or automatic transmission fluid "Dexron™" type, and blow dry with compressed air, if available. Exploded view of power steering gear and linkage assembly showing steering gear assembly, outer tie-rod, dust boot, and boot bands with torque specification for outer tie-rod Photo showing how to hold steering gear housing with soft jaws during disassembly

609.title: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Inspection (Cont'd)
609.body: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Inspection (Cont'd) ST-26 Steering Gear Assembly Inspection Check for smooth operation through a full stroke. Check rack and tie-rod for cracks, deformation or other damage. Check pinion rotating torque. Check rack sliding force. Pinion rotating torque 0.8 - 1.3 N·m Pinion rotating torque 8 - 13 kg-cm Pinion rotating torque 6.9 - 11.3 in-lb Rack sliding force 284 or less N Rack sliding force 29 or less kg Rack sliding force 64 or less lb Check condition of dust boot. If excessively cracked, replace it. Tie-Rod Outer and Inner Socket Inspection Check ball joint for swinging force. Check ball joint for rotating torque. Tie-rod outer ball joint swinging force 6.9 - 63.7 N Tie-rod outer ball joint swinging force 0.7 - 6.5 kg Tie-rod outer ball joint swinging force 1.5 - 14.3 lb Tie-rod inner ball joint swinging force 4.9 - 83.4 N Tie-rod inner ball joint swinging force 0.5 - 8.5 kg Tie-rod inner ball joint swinging force 1.1 - 18.7 lb Tie-rod outer ball joint rotating torque 0.3 - 2.9 N·m Tie-rod outer ball joint rotating torque 3 - 30 kg-cm Tie-rod outer ball joint rotating torque 2.6 - 26.0 in-lb Special service tool KV48100700 and ST3127S000 being used to check pinion rotating torque on steering gear assembly Tool being used to check rack sliding force on steering rack assembly Measuring point indicated on tie-rod outer and inner ball joint for swinging force check Special service tool ST3127S000 being used to check tie-rod outer ball joint rotating torque

610.title: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Inspection (Cont'd) / Assembly
610.body: POWER STEERING GEAR AND LINKAGE — Inspection (Cont'd) / Assembly ST-27 Inspection (Cont'd) Check ball joint for axial end play. Check condition of dust cover. If excessively cracked, replace it. Tie-rod outer ball joint axial end play 0.5 mm (0.020 in) or less mm (in) axial end play check Tie-rod inner ball joint axial end play 0 mm (0 in) mm (in) axial end play check Assembly Tighten outer socket lock nut. Tie-rod length "L": 183.6 mm (7.23 in) Measure rack stroke. Rack stroke "L": Short track = 60.6 mm (2.386 in), Standard track = 64.6 mm (2.543 in) Before installing boot, coat the contact surfaces between boot and tie-rod with grease. Tie-rod length "L" 183.6 mm (7.23 in) mm (in) assembly — outer socket lock nut tightening Rack stroke "L" — Short track 60.6 mm (2.386 in) mm (in) rack stroke measurement Rack stroke "L" — Standard track 64.6 mm (2.543 in) mm (in) rack stroke measurement Short track and Standard track variants specified for rack stroke measurement. Ball joint axial end play check — shows axial end play measurement point on tie-rod ball joint Tie-rod assembly showing length 'L' measurement for outer socket lock nut tightening Rack assembly showing rack stroke 'L' measurement on both sides Boot installation showing contact surfaces between boot and tie-rod to be coated with grease

611.title: POWER STEERING OIL PUMP
611.body:
POWER STEERING OIL PUMP ST-28 Pulley Pully nut Pump body assembly Suction port Pully nut torque 16 - 22 (1.6 - 2.2, 11 - 8) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Pump mounting bolt torque (upper) 15 - 25 (2 - 2.6, 14.8 - 18.4) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Pump mounting bolt torque (side) 32 - 42 (3.3 - 4.3, 23.6 - 31) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Pump mounting bolt torque (bottom) 32 - 42 (3.3 - 4.3, 23.6 - 31) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Banjo union bolt torque 49 - 69 (5 - 7, 36.1 - 50.9) N·m (kg-m, ft-lb) Removal Drain fluid from system by removing hose from suction pipe. Slacken power steering drive belt (refer to MA section for details). Remove Banjo union, and remove hose from pump. Remove pump fixing bolts and remove pump. Replace power steering oil pump if any of the following items are found:
a) Oil leak from connections, unions or oil pump casing.
b) Deformed or damaged pulley.
c) Poor performance. CAUTION:
• Parts which can be disassembled are strictly limited. Never disassemble parts other than those specified.
• Disassemble in as clean a place as possible.
• Clean your hands before disassembly.
• Do not use rags; use nylon cloths or paper towels.
• Follow the procedures and cautions in the Service Manual.
• When disassembling and reassembling, do not let foreign matter enter or contact the parts. Exploded view of power steering oil pump assembly showing pulley, pully nut, pump body, suction port, and torque specifications for various fasteners

612.title: POWER STEERING OIL PUMP
612.body: POWER STEERING OIL PUMP ST-29 Installation Installation is the reverse process to removal. Replenish fluid in system as described in power steering rack installation. Ensure all connections, banjo union joints and bolts are tightened to the specified torques. Tension power steering drive belt to the specifications described in MA section.

613.title: POWER STEERING RESERVOIR
613.body:
POWER STEERING RESERVOIR ST-30 Removal Drain fluid from system. Slacken hose retaining clips and remove feed and return hoses from reservoir. Remove Bolts (3) securing reservoir to vehicle body. CAUTION
• Do not remove reservoir from bracket or rotate reservoir in bracket.
• If it is necessary to separate reservoir from bracket ensure it is orientated as disassembled when re-assembling. Note correct orientation and connection of feed and return hoses to reservoir stub pipes. Installation Installation is the reverse procedure to removal. Ensure hoses are fitted to reservoir correctly and do not interfere with drive belts. Refill reservoir as described in installation procedure of power steering rack and check fluid level after bleeding hydraulic system. Power steering reservoir assembly showing the reservoir unit with mounting bracket and hose stubs Power steering reservoir installed in engine bay with callout label identifying the power steering reservoir
